title: Smart Vending Machine For Grocery Shop with Custom Quantity Dispensing
authors: P.Ranjith Kumar, M.Vimala, S.Ramasamy, K.Kamalesh, M.Kannuppandi, B.Madhankumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The creation of an automated vending machine system with an Arduino controller for effective and user-friendly operation is presented in this project. Because the system is made to take user input via a keypad, a variety of goods, including rice, sugar, and other groceries. The machine automatically dispenses the appropriate amount of the selected item based on the user's selection. Servo motors, which are controlled by the Arduino to precisely drop the required quantity of each item, are used to manage the process. By reducing human intervention, increasing transaction speed, and guaranteeing accurate dispensing, the system seeks to optimize vending operations. Furthermore, the solution incorporates effective real-time control, guaranteeing a smooth and dependable vending process. This automated vending machine shows how Arduino-based solutions can be used in common consumer applications, offering accuracy, convenience, and dependability in a form that is simple to implement. All things considered, the developed vending machine not only streamlines the dispensing process but also encourages sustainability by cutting down on packaging waste and allowing customers to buy just the amounts they require. This demonstrates the potential of Arduino-based smart vending systems in influencing the direction of automated retail and makes it a workable and scalable solution for both urban supermarkets and rural retail settings.

title: Bias and Fairness in AI Systems: Theoretical Analysis and Empirical Validation Through Reweighing Mitigation
authors: Louis Adamu Obadiah, Daniel Godwin, Ndachimya Magaji Edward, Nibras Mohammad Abubakar, Jibrailu Daniel, Douglas Kilyobas Titus
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence increasingly influences highstakes decisions in employment, lending, healthcare, and criminal justice. While AI systems promise efficiency and objectivity, they risk institutionalizing discrimination at scale when trained on biased data or designed without explicit fairness constraints. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation bridging theoretical analysis with empirical validation to address bias and fairness in AI. A systematic analysis of bias sources and fairness frameworks is integrated with a Python-based fairness assessment framework implemented using the AI Fairness 360 (AIF360) toolkit. Using the UCI Adult Income dataset, Logistic Regression is applied as the baseline classifier, and Reweighing is implemented as a preprocessing mitigation technique to evaluate algorithmic fairness across gender groups. Empirical results demonstrate measurable gender bias in the baseline model (Statistical Parity Difference: −0.175; Equal Opportunity Difference: −0.258). Following Reweighing mitigation, fairness disparities were substantially reduced (SPD: −0.062; EOD: +0.060), representing improvements of 64.6 % and 76.7 % respectively. These fairness gains were achieved with only a modest accuracy trade-off, as model accuracy declined from 0.825 to 0.812 (−1.3 percentage points). ROC analysis further confirmed convergence in discriminative performance across gender groups, with the gender AUC gap reducing from 0.054 to −0.014 following mitigation, indicating a reversal from male to marginal female discriminative advantage post-mitigation. An extended multi-classifier evaluation further demonstrates that Reweighing's effectiveness varies substantially across algorithm types achieving strong SPD reductions in Logistic Regression (64.5 %) and SVM (63.5 %), while showing limited impact on ensemble methods such as Random Forest, suggesting that mitigation effectiveness is algorithm-dependent. These findings provide quantitative evidence that algorithmic bias is both detectable and meaningfully mitigable through systematic fairness interventions, while demonstrating that fairness improvements need not require severe degradation in predictive performance.

title: Software Sustainability Debt and the Governance-Process-Execution Framework: Evidence from 513,000 Real-World CI/CD Workflows
authors: Rohit Dhawan, Minav Suresh Patel, Priyank Desai, Ankush Dhar
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Software systems increasingly drive computing's carbon footprint, yet most workloads execute without sustainability governance. This study introduces Software Sustainability Debt (SSD) as the avoidable emissions accumulating from unmanaged execution, and the Governance-Process-Execution (GPE) framework as an enforceable solution through explicit policy guardrails. Analyzing 513,000 GitHub Actions workflows across three European grids under operationally constrained parameters, we demonstrate that governed approaches achieve 1% single-region reductions while maintaining SLA compliance, scaling to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 7 \%}$</tex> (GB-DE), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 9. 9 \%}$</tex> (GB-FR), <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0. 6 \%}$</tex> (DEFR), and 32.4% (tri-region) through cross-regional coordination. Unlike prior grid-aware systems, which act opportunistically when conditions are favorable, GPE enforces sustainability with the same operational guarantees as cost, SLA, and latency, making it deployable today rather than hypothetical. These results demonstrate that sustainability governance can operate under strict operational constraints, providing organizations a deployable framework for carbon accountability that maintains SLA compliance while enabling material emission reductions where infrastructure flexibility permits.

title: Machine Learning for Predicting and Managing Industrial Co and Nox Emissions: Data-Driven Modeling for Sustainable Production
authors: Dilara Abzhanova, Sapar Toxanov, Andrii Biloshchytskyi
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Industrial emissions of nitrogen oxides (NOx) and carbon monoxide (CO) pose significant risks to human health and climate. Conventional monitoring techniques do not enable early prediction of regulatory exceedances. This work suggests a strategy for predicting CO and NOx emissions utilizing a gas turbine unit's digital twin architecture and machine learning algorithms. Four regression models - linear, polynomial (second degree), Ridge, and Lasso - were used and contrasted. The models were trained using historical data from an industrial facility (more than 35,000 records for the years 2020–2025), including key technological parameters such as ambient conditions, compressor discharge pressure (CDP), and turbine inlet temperature (TIT). The test results showed that the polynomial model was the most accurate: the coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> climbed to 0.78, and the mean squared error (MSE) decreased by 22%. Sensitivity analysis revealed that CO emissions are positively impacted by compressor discharge pressure (CDP) and negatively impacted by turbine inlet temperature (TIT). Unlike existing studies based on simulated or standalone models, this work demonstrates a closed-loop digital twin prototype validated on real industrial sensor data with integrated diagnostics and sensitivity analysis. The proposed approach improves the accuracy of environmental performance assessment and can be used in proactive environmental management systems, ESG reporting, and sustainable industrial operations.

title: Artificial Intelligence based Object Detection Assistant for the Visually Impaired
authors: K. Sasirekha, K. Shanthini, M. Divyaraj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This paper discusses Vision Aid, a real-time, multimodal artificial intelligence system intended to provide visually impaired persons with greater awareness of their environment and the ability to move independently. The proposed system combines five complementary perception modules: YOLOv8, used to detect objects; MiDaS, used to estimate depth; EasyOCR, used to read text; MediaPipe, used to recognize gestures; and a Vision Transformer, used to analyze facial expressions. Unlike traditional assistive systems, which have isolated modules, the proposed system integrates these modules to enable holistic analysis. A centralized fusion system ranks safety-critical objects based on the depth-based ranking and contextual decision rule. To achieve minimal latency, the system manages live video streams and provides structural auditory feedback with the help of an asynchronous text-to-speech module. End-to-end and benchmark testing on real-world and experimental data show high performance at the module level, with 89.4% mAP, 91.2% accuracy, and an end-to-end task success rate of 88.6%. The framework had an average speed of 31.7 FPS and an average response latency of 0.7 seconds. The user assessment with visually impaired participants showed clear, responsive, and lower cognitive load. The findings substantiate the hypothesis that the proposed multimodal fusion architecture will deliver credible real-time support, improved situational awareness, and increased safety in a dynamic environment.

title: An Efficient Framework for Network Formation and Optimal Routing in Mobile Ad Hoc Networks
authors: S. Muruganandam, N. Pavithra, J.Arokia Renjit
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) are known as an effective model for data transmission in wireless communication. They are often used for real-time information exchange among mobile devices. However, MANETs face several challenges that impact their performance, including high power consumption and network congestion. To tackle these issues, this work introduces an Efficient Framework for Network Formation and Optimal Routing in Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (ENF-OR-MANET). The proposed model includes key functional modules like congestion control, mobility model generation, link quality assessment, optimal routing path selection, and malicious node detection. These processes work together to improve communication reliability and efficiency for mobile devices. The ENF-OR-MANET framework is implemented on the NS-3 simulation platform, and its performance is measured using metrics such as packet delivery ratio, network throughput, energy efficiency, average delay, and routing overhead. Simulation results show that ENF-ORMANET significantly boosts network efficiency and prolongs node lifetime while greatly reducing energy consumption, end-to-end delay, and routing overhead.

title: Grid-Aware Harris Hybrid Hawk Optimization for Strategic Electric Vehicle Charging Station Deployment
authors: Anita Merlin I, P.S.Meenambikai, A Cassia Jemi M., T. Dharma Raj, S.Nithya, N.Anita Femilin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The rapid rise in electric mobility has intensified the need for strategically located charging infrastructure, prompting extensive research on optimal charging station placement. However, most existing studies overlook an essential aspect—fairness in the distribution of charging stations across the network, which directly influences accessibility and user convenience. The proposed method integrates multiple critical factors, including equitable station distribution, locational advantage, and user comfort. Fairness is evaluated through uniformity of station allocation, while comfort is assessed through overall charging time experienced by users. The hybrid model effectively balances these objectives, offering an improved and intelligent approach for EV charging station planning.

title: An Xception–YOLO based Deep Learning Framework for Kidney Stone Detection in CT Images
authors: Nekkalapu Srikanth, Pendem Likhitha, Devarapalli Akhila, Boda Venkata Lakshmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Early diagnosis of kidney stones by the use of CT imaging can be used to provide renal architecture. The aim of the work is the better extraction of features, classification and localization of stones by the assistance of a deep learning framework relying on a transfer learning. The Xception model is selected as the final classifier because its depthwise separable convolution architecture efficiently extracts spatial and textural features. In addition, the models built in accordance with the YOLO object detector will identify the position of the spot in the CT image of the kidneys with high accuracy in order to be able to present the disease of the kidneys in real-time. That goes hand in hand with the positive clinical practicability of the proposed system. As it has been experimentally proven, the proposed structure offers a better classification, quicker detection, and the overall rate of generalization on kidney CT image data. The overall structure suggested will help the urologists to work less on the general finding a diagnosis, eliminate mistakes, and enhance the early diagnosis, which, in its turn, will help enhance a clinical decision-making process.

title: Small Scale Production of Biochar with a Home Microwave Oven
authors: Charles Jackson
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper will investigate the possibility of making Biochar with a commercial microwave oven using solar power to reduce the carbon footprint during production. Microwave Kilns are a recent development, and a small sized microwave kiln was used for these experiments. Heating times and methods of reducing the oxygen in the kiln volume were investigated. It has been shown that biochar can be made using a commercial microwave oven, but the impact on carbon sequestration is small.

title: Fake News Detection Capability Across Contemporary LLMs: Comparative Study of Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning Across LIAR and ISOT
authors: Emre Karacakurt, Ecem Sıla Gök, Eren Ulu
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper compares zero-shot prompting, few-shot prompting, and parameter-efficient fine-tuning in performance reliability for fake news detection across contemporary opensource and open-weight large language models. We evaluate four instruct models from the Llama, Gemma, Qwen, and Mistral families on two contrasting datasets, LIAR and ISOT. LIAR is a smaller dataset of short political claims, while ISOT is a much larger article-level dataset. Across both datasets, the task is constrained to direct label prediction. The results show that parameter-efficient fine-tuning is the most reliable strategy, although its advantage depends strongly on dataset structure, as demonstrated by the different dataset results. On LIAR, the best fine-tuned model has a macro-F1 of 0.6785, while on ISOT finetuning yields perfect performance for three models and a nearperfect macro-F1 of 0.9998 for the fourth model.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Signature Verification: Combining CapsNet with Data Augmentation Techniques
authors: Veena V. G, J. R. Jeba
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Signature verification plays a crucial role in authentication and document security across various domains. Traditional approaches, relying on manual inspection and rule-based methods, often struggle with the inherent variability in handwriting styles, limiting their effectiveness. This study presents a deep learning-based approach to signature verification, leveraging neural networks to extract and learn discriminative features from signature images. By employing advanced deep learning architectures, the proposed system enhances accuracy and efficiency in verification tasks. A key challenge in signature verification is the limited availability of training data, which can hinder model performance. To address this, Cycle-GAN is utilized as a data augmentation technique, generating synthetic signature samples that improve the robustness of the model. Furthermore, a novel verification framework built on Capsule Networks (Caps-Net) is introduced, offering superior feature extraction capabilities and improved generalization across diverse handwriting styles. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the integration of Cycle-GAN for data augmentation and Caps-Net for verification significantly enhances performance compared to conventional methods. The results highlight the system’s adaptability, scalability, and robustness, making it a viable solution for secure authentication processes. This research contributes to the advancement of biometric authentication systems by integrating cutting-edge deep learning techniques, providing a more reliable and efficient approach to signature verification. The findings underscore the importance of leveraging artificial intelligence-driven methodologies to overcome the challenges posed by handwriting variability, ensuring a more secure and accurate authentication framework suitable for real-world applications.

title: Design of a Via-Loaded Interdigital Microstrip Bandpass Filter for S-Band Applications
authors: Emirhan Gözel, Cemre Bengisu Şar, İrem Atalay, Emirhan Sağ, Sultan Can
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a seventh-order interdigital microstrip bandpass filter operating in the S-band. The filter is designed for a passband between 2.2 GHz and 2.5 GHz based on a Chebyshev prototype to achieve sharp selectivity and controlled passband ripple. Grounding vias are incorporated into the resonator structure to form via-loaded quarter-wavelength resonators and improve electromagnetic confinement. The initial equivalent circuit model was implemented in AWR Microwave Office, while the physical structure was analyzed using full-wave simulations in CST Microwave Studio Suite. Key geometrical parameters such as resonator lengths, coupling gaps, and feed position were optimized to obtain the desired response. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed filter achieves the required passband characteristics with good out-of-band suppression, achieving the sharp selectivity-controlled passband ripples.

title: 'Can AI Care?': Emotional Tone Analysis and Perceived Empathy in AI-Generated Health Advice
authors: Maryam Irfan, Tommi Kärkkäinen, António Correia
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Recent developments in the field of natural language processing (NLP) have demonstrated that artificial intelligence (AI)-enabled chatbots can produce health advice rated as more empathic than clinician-authored text in blinded evaluations. Nonetheless, a research gap exists between automated emotional analysis and human perception. By situating emotional tone and perceived empathy within the broader sociotechnical context of personalized health advice, this work articulates a research roadmap for investigating these constructs in relation to AI-based health communication. Adopting a human-centered perspective, it reframes empathy assessment as an interactional phenomenon rather than a purely text-based or model-internal attribute. We discuss key tensions between algorithmic representations of emotional tone and empathy in AI-generated health advice, highlighting how their underlying linguistic characteristics may not translate into feelings of trust, comfort, or understanding.

title: Teaching Off-Grid Solar Energy Installation Business in Liberia
authors: Charles E. Hunt, Javier A. Romero R., Abraham Foday
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: The Light For Liberia Project is an educational initiative that teaches students at African Bible College University (ABCU) skills to establish an off-grid solar energy business. The university is located in Yekepa, Liberia. The program includes two semester-long classes that use design-oriented didactic methods, creating an innovative learning environment. Along with a lecture class, a lab class using a solar energy laboratory kit, and twentythree laboratory experiments were designed. Prof. Charles E. Hunt of the University of California, Davis, taught the two courses in person during the Fall Semester, 2024, at ABCU in Yekepa, Liberia. He visited multiple vendors of solar energy system components to build a supply chain for emerging businesses. This is especially needed, given that Liberia ranks among the 10 poorest countries in the world and that less than 5% of rural residents have access to electricity. Outside of the capital city of Monrovia, virtually no energy grid exists in Liberia. The project demonstrated that students could design small solar energy systems. A group of students who completed both classes has already established a small business installing off-grid solarpower systems. There is a large customer base for these systems in rural Liberia.

title: An Explainable AI (XAI)–Driven Deep LSTM Framework for High-Precision Financial Time Series Prediction
authors: Ramachandran S, P. Vasuki
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: This research proposes an Explainable AI (XAI)-driven Deep Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) framework for high-precision financial time series prediction, with a focus on short-term cryptocurrency price forecasting. Market data sampled at five-minute intervals from the BTC/USDT trading pair on a major cryptocurrency exchange are utilized. The deep LSTM model captures complex temporal dependencies by analyzing price movements over the preceding two-hour window to predict prices for the subsequent five-minute interval. To improve interpretability, XAI methods are applied to analyze the influence of historical data points and time steps on the predicted values. Experimental evaluation shows that the model achieves consistent prediction accuracy, with average forecasting errors remaining below 0.45%. Across multiple test cases, the observed error rates range from 0.098% to 0.307%. These results indicate that the approach is effective for short-term financial forecasting and can support data-driven decision-making in cryptocurrency markets.

title: Hybrid CNN–Transformer Architectures for Real-Time Medicine Identification with Edge-Optimized Assistive Feedback
authors: Thamba Meshach W, Prathapkumar K, Ram Prasath T K, Surya R, Thanikonda Nithin, Boggavarapu Yugendhar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accurate identification of visually similar pharmaceutical tablets remains a critical challenge in assistive healthcare, particularly for visually impaired users operating in uncontrolled environments. This work presents MEDVISOR, a lightweight hybrid CNN–Transformer framework designed for real-time, edge-deployable medicine recognition with integrated confidence-aware audio feedback. The architecture combines convolutional feature extraction for fine-grained surface and contour representation with Transformer-based self-attention for global contextual modeling, enabling improved discrimination between morphologically similar pills. Evaluated on a stratified pharmaceutical image dataset, the proposed model achieves 92.6% Top-1 accuracy while maintaining an average inference latency of 16.8 ms on edge CPU configurations. INT8 quantization further reduces latency to 9.8 ms with minimal accuracy degradation (0.7%). Robustness analysis under illumination variation and motion blur demonstrates stable performance with controlled degradation. Compared to CNN-only and Transformer-only baselines, the hybrid architecture provides consistent gains in both classification accuracy and deployment efficiency. The results indicate that combining local and global visual reasoning with edge-aware optimization offers a practical and scalable solution for assistive medicine identification systems..

title: Contrastive Learning-Enhanced Attention Models for Predicting and Optimizing Brine Desalination Processes
authors: Abdallah Ragab, Tamer ElBatt, Essam Mohamed, Ahmed M. Azzam, Amira T. Mohamed, Omar Sheta, Ghada Ahmed, Elaf Seif
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Motivated by the importance and scarcity of fresh water resources globally, this paper presents a novel machine learning approach for predicting and optimizing the performance of brine desalination processes. In particular, we propose an attention-based artificial neural network (ANN) model, integrated with contrastive learning, for predicting key performance parameters in brine desalination systems. The proposed regression framework leverages self-attention mechanisms to improve feature representation and incorporates a contrastive learning objective to extract robust embeddings. To the authors' knowledge, this work is the first application of attention-based ANN with contrastive learning to water desalination processes. The proposed model is systematically evaluated against two baseline schemes, namely a conventional ANN model and an attention-augmented ANN without contrastive learning. The experimental results reveal that the proposed self-supervised attention-based model achieves superior prediction accuracy, with a substantial reduction in the Mean Squared Error (MSE) and enhanced <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> scores, compared to both baseline models. The trained model is subsequently integrated with the NSGA-II multi-objective optimization algorithm to characterize the optimal system parameters, striking a balance between multiple, possibly conflicting, objectives. The comprehensive evaluation study confirms the efficacy of integrating attention mechanisms with self-supervised contrastive learning for enhanced brine desalination system performance prediction and system optimization. The proposed model achieves an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.95, which surpasses the attention-only model (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.92$</tex>), and achieves a reduced MSE of 0.002 compared to 0.004 for the attention-only model.

title: Deployment-Aware Benchmarking of Lightweight Machine Learning for IoT-Based Smart Grid Fault Detection: A Comparative Study Across Easy, Noisy, and Public Evaluation Settings
authors: Mohammed Yasser Hussein Mjbas, Salah A. Aliesawi, Mohammed Rabeea Hashim Al-Dahhan
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Smart grid fault detection in Internet of Things (IoT) environments requires rapid, accurate, and resourceefficient inference mechanisms suitable for edge deployment. Existing approaches often rely on computationally intensive deep learning models or cloud-dependent architectures, limiting practical deployment on resource-constrained IoT nodes. This paper proposes a deployment-aware benchmarking framework for lightweight machine learning models applied to smart grid fault detection, evaluated under three settings of increasing difficulty. A physics-informed synthetic dataset comprising 2,000,000 samples across 400 scenarios and five fault classes-Normal, Single Line-to-Ground (SLG), Line-to-Line (LL), Double Line-to-Ground (DLG), and Three-Phase (LLL)-is generated from eight-channel IoT sensor signals. A feature extraction pipeline combining time-domain and FFT-based features produces 104 discriminative features per window. Fifteen machine learning models spanning Tiny, Lightweight, and Medium deployment tiers are benchmarked on an easy synthetic dataset, a hard noisy synthetic dataset (SNR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 14 ~\text{dB})$</tex>, and the public UCI Electrical Grid Stability dataset. Under hard noisy conditions, binary fault detection remains feasible with up to 88.75 % accuracy (Balanced Accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.545$</tex> for the best model), whereas multiclass faulttype discrimination degrades to near-chance levels <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx \mathbf{2 2. 9 2 \%})$</tex>, identifying it as an open research challenge requiring richer signal representations. External validation on the UCI dataset is limited to binary power-system stability classification and does not extend to the multiclass fault taxonomy of the synthetic experiments. On the UCI dataset, AdaBoost achieves 91.65% accuracy at only 0.0446 MB and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.00479 ~\text{ms} / \text{sample}$</tex> latency, suggesting potential suitability for edge deployment pending on-device hardware validation. The study frames binary fault detection as the tractable edge deployment target and explicitly positions multiclass fault-type discrimination as a substantially harder future research direction.

title: History-Aware Dynamic Quantum Time Allocation for Round Robin Scheduling
authors: İdris Batuhan Aydın, Suleyman Tosun
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: In Round Robin scheduling, fixed time quanta often lead to excessive and costly context switches, making dynamic quantum allocation a superior choice for enhancing system performance. However, determining the optimal length for such a quantum remains a complex decision problem due to the unpredictable nature of task characteristics. In this study, we present a history-aware dynamic quantum time allocation method that dynamically adapts the time quantum using cached execution history. The proposed approach leverages past execution behavior to estimate future task characteristics, enabling more informed scheduling decisions under dynamic workload conditions. We implemented the scheduler in a discrete-event simulation environment using synthetic workloads. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves lower average waiting time, turnaround time, and context switch count compared to the generic round robin scheduling algorithm.

title: GAN-Assisted Triple-Branch Swin Transformer for Diabetic Retinopathy Grading
authors: Bing Wang, Xinyi Sun, Zhongmei Gong
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Automatic grading of diabetic retinopathy (DR) plays a crucial role in clinical auxiliary diagnosis. However, marked class imbalance in real-world fundus image datasets significantly limits the ability of existing methods to accurately identify scarce samples of severe and proliferative DR. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Swin Transformer-based DR grading method that introduces the generative adversarial network (GAN) in the training stage for data generation and feature-level constraints. Guided by three-way decision theory, a triple-branch training framework is designed to encourage the backbone network to focus on severe and proliferative DR samples. Without modifying the model architecture during inference, implicit feature-level consistency constraints are introduced to enhance the learning of discriminative pathological representations. It should be emphasized that GAN-generated samples are used only for training, while real fundus images are employed in validation and testing. This strategy effectively alleviates class imbalance while minimising potential hallucinations in medical image analysis. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method maintains stable overall performance, while significantly improving the recognition ability for severe and proliferative DR grades. Specifically, the classification accuracy reaches 0.87, the Macro F1-score is 0.86, and the weighted Cohen’s Kappa coefficient achieves 0.93.

title: VulnWatch: A Lightweight Network Security Checker for Identifying Risks and Providing Actionable Fixes
authors: Md.Sajeena, T.Jhahnavi, N.Anusha, R.Swapna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Present-day computer networks are facing increasing threats from security issues owing to the presence of errors and outdated services, encryption algorithms, and opened ports. However, the output provided by the vulnerability scanning assistance available is difficult to be interpreted and acted upon by users who are not acquainted with the knowledge of computer networking. The motivation and objectives of this research work are to design and develop an interpretable and understandable security scanning system requiring only basic networking knowledge and intermittent internet connectivity. To this end, the proposed work introduces the design and development of VulnWatch, an asynchronous scanning module using the asynchronous execution environment provided by Python to quickly identify opened transmission control protocol ports and opened services. Other methods used include banner and transport-layer security data extraction to enhance scanning, followed by vulnerability scanning conducted offline using the vulnerability data stored within the local repository. Vulnerabilities identified using the scanning method are classified according to risk levels and presented as directives to close firewalls and ports. There is also output concerning the provision of a graphical display, scan history, as well as automatic report generation. The experimental study performed on the local as well as online networks has identified that the system has been providing an average of 96.8% concerning the output on port scanning, correct service identification with a ratio of 94.5%, as well as correct risk identification on a ratio of 92.3% pertaining to all vulnerabilities identified within the scanning process. Moreover, it has been identified that the asynchronous model has dealt with the scanning process within a time frame that is almost 48% less than the scan process undertaken by the model functioning on a sequential manner without compromising the responsiveness of the interface designed. This experimental study performed implies the inclination towards accepting the conclusion that the above approach has been efficient within improving the usability, efficacy, as well as clarity concerning the vulnerability scans pertaining to security education and small networks.

title: Deep Neural Networks for Coastal Climate Risk Prediction Using Flood and Climate Variables
authors: Chandralekha S, Vijaya Naga Valli K, Jayapriya N, Amandeep Singh K, Monisha R, Chairmadurai K
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Coastal areas are more vulnerable to climaterelated threats, such as floods, storm surges, and rising sea levels, requiring precise risk assessment for effective mitigation and planning. This paper introduces a deep neural network (DNN) methodology for predicting coastal flood risk, using the Coastal Climate Risk Dataset, which consists of 1,653 geospatial grid records including environmental, topographical, and climatic characteristics. Essential characteristics include elevation, land use, soil composition, drainage capability, proximity to the sea, mean precipitation, and historical flood occurrence. The DNN model was developed to categorize each grid unit as Low, Moderate, or High flood risk. The experimental findings indicate that the proposed model attains an overall accuracy of 98.55 %. Comparisons with baseline models, such as Random Forest and Support Vector Machines, demonstrate that the DNN outperforms conventional machine learning methods by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4-7 \%$</tex> in accuracy, underscoring its enhanced capacity to grasp nonlinear interactions among factors impacting floods. The findings indicate that deep learning methodologies may provide dependable, detailed forecasts for coastal flood risk, facilitating data-informed decision-making and proactive climate resilience initiatives. This approach may be adapted to other climate-sensitive areas to improve environmental planning and catastrophe readiness.

title: Deep Capsule-Based Self-Attention Mechanism for Underwater Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: B. Thiyagarajan, M. Thenmozhi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Exploration of the underwater environment requires the efficient image processing techniques due to challenges present in the underwater environment image collection and processing. The degradation of images takes place due to the water quality, turbidity, particles, etc., where the detection and classification of the object in the underwater environment is quite challenging with artificial illumination. Compared to Terrestrial images, the underwater images are affected by colour degradation and contrast loss, where the high-level spatial features are not easy to obtain. Therefore, the proposed work uses the Capsule network-based architecture that extracts the high-level features and maps them to the lowlevel features by a dynamic routing mechanism, followed by the self-attention mechanism to correlate the features that represent the regions of the object for classification. The proposed work showed 38.42 dB of PSNR with an accuracy of 92.8 % which is 8.3 % higher than the baseline CNN model using the Underwater Hyperspectral image dataset.

title: Multi Agent Reinforcement Learning Based Swarm Navigation
authors: Omer Yigit, Suleyman Efe Ceylan, Sevval Selin Palas, Arda Isik, Emir Bekar, Berkay Vatansever, Yesim Oniz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Swarm navigation in dynamic and complex environments represents a critical challenge for both decentralized control systems and dynamic obstacle avoidance. Therefore, this study presents a robust, decentralized Multi Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) model which uses a distributed-proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm and enables the navigation of swarms collectively in a decentralized manner, using a shared policy without explicit communication among agents. In order to train each agent to learn how to transform an 8 dimensional vector into a specific action from their perspective in a two dimensional arena (thus enabling them to take action in the arena), we represent the system as a decentralized Multi Agent Markov Decision Process (MAMDP) and use a multi component reward function that provides penalty against collisions, rewards agents for successful progress toward the goals, and dampens velocity to promote stability. Our simulation results show quick convergence during training, and our agents achieve high success rates in all randomized configurations of the environment. Furthermore, our agents are able to generalize well across new, unseen obstacles; therefore, they are able to maintain their ability to navigate efficiently and safely in previously unseen obstacle configurations.

title: Secure Medical Image Transmission using Deep Learning and Chaotic Map-Enhanced Homomorphic Encryption for Privacy Preservation
authors: B. Suvitha, K. Santhiya, S. Mary Selvi, S.V. Jeyasri
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Secure transmission of medical images is critical in telemedicine and healthcare applications, where privacy and data integrity are paramount. This paper presents a hybrid approach that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) with chaotic-map-based feature scrambling and homomorphic encryption to enhance the security of medical image transmission. In the proposed system, CNN is employed to extract robust image features, which are then secured using a chaotic map before encryption, providing dual-layer protection against unauthorized access. The effectiveness of the method is evaluated using Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) to assess image quality preservation, Structural Similarity Index Measure (SSIM) to assess structural fidelity, and Encryption Speed to assess computational efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid approach achieves high security, preserves image quality, and maintains efficient encryption performance, enabling efficient deployment in healthcare systems. By integrating deep learning-based feature extraction, chaotic scrambling, and Homomorphic Encryption, this work presents a robust and privacy-preserving framework for secure medical image transmission.

title: Modeling and Analysis of Hybrid Renewable Energy Systems Using Artificial Neural Networks for Short-Term Power Forecasting
authors: Ivelina Metodieva, Stoyan Bozhkov, Ivelina Takeva-Beberova
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The development of modern electric power systems is associated with the increasing integration of decentralized renewable energy sources, such as photovoltaic and wind generators. This leads to significant changes in the operating modes of power distribution networks and introduces new requirements for the analysis of operating parameters, such as the need for enhanced reliability, efficiency, and the ability to accommodate fluctuations in energy supply from these renewable sources. The present study examines the modelling of a hybrid energy system using artificial neural networks (ANN) for short-term forecasting of generated electrical energy. The model is developed based on real operational data, allowing for a more accurate representation of working conditions. The results show that ANN models can effectively capture the nonlinear relationships between input parameters and generated power, leading to improved forecasting accuracy and supporting the management of hybrid energy systems.

title: Latent Ideological Polarization in Energy Transitions: A Multi-Level Structural Causality Analysis of Climate Policy Acceptance
authors: G. Shankar, Amira S, Diya Sri, Lavanya R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Public responses to climate and energy policies are increasingly shaped by deeply embedded ideological orientations rather than uniform environmental concern. This study develops an integrated analytical framework to examine how latent belief systems structure climate policy acceptance. Using nationally representative survey data, the analysis combines exploratory factor analysis, data-driven ideological clustering, and multi-group structural equation modeling to identify differentiated causal pathways across belief-based segments. The results reveal two structurally distinct ideological publics in which institutional trust, perceived climate risk, and energy preferences interact differently to influence policy support. Rather than reflecting simple variations in environmental attitudes, polarization emerges as a latent configuration of belief systems that alters the architecture of policy evaluation. By moving beyond aggregate modeling approaches, the study demonstrates that identical policy instruments may generate divergent responses depending on underlying ideological structures. These findings underscore the importance of incorporating belief-system heterogeneity into climate governance strategies.

title:
Performance Evaluation of CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Emission Levels in Agrofood Systems for Climate Action
authors: Geetha R, Diana Grace Thomas
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The agrofood industry is a significant source of global carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) emissions owing to intensive food production, agriculture land-use change, food processing, transportation, energy usage, and population-based demand. The right categorization of the level of emissions is crucial in the effective monitoring of the environment and in the promotions of Climate Action. This paper describes a machine learning, the system of classification and evaluation of the performance of total CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emission level in agrofood systems with the usage of the orange data mining tool. The dataset is comprised of extensive range of the emission related parameters as net forest conversion, food household consumption, food retail, on-farm electricity and energy use, food processing and packaging, fertilizer manufacturing, manure management, fires in organic and humid tropical forests, population distribution, and average temperature with the target variable being total CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions. The workflow proposed includes the preprocessing of data, training models, and evaluation, in which the total CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> emissions are divided into low, medium, and high-emission groups. Several machine learning models are used and their performance is compared based on traditional performance measures such as classification accuracy (CA), precision, recall, F1-score, Area Under the Curve (AUC), and Matthews Correlation Coefficient (MCC) which include: random forest, decision tree, Logistic Regression, and support vector machine (SVM). The experiment outcomes show that ensemble and kernel-based models are more advantageous in performance. Random Forest and SVM are perfect in terms of AUC, accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of 1.000, with large value of MCC of 0.996 and 0.992 respectively, which depicts great predictability ability. In addition, the Logistic Regression demonstrates good performance with the accuracy of 0.982 and AUC of 0.900, where the Decision Tree model is relatively lower with the discriminative capability although with high accuracy.

title: System Dependent Performance Analysis of Metaheuristic Algorithms for DOCR Coordination in Distribution Networks and Microgrids
authors: İbrahim Arslanoğlu, İsmail Hakkı Altaş, Heybet Kılıç, Cem Haydaroğlu
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Directional Overcurrent Relay (DOCR) coordination plays a critical role in ensuring reliable and selective protection in modern power systems. Although numerous studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of metaheuristic optimization algorithms in distribution networks, their performance in micro-grid environments remains uncertain due to distinct operational characteristics, such as low fault current levels and bidirectional power flows. This study investigates the system-dependent performance of three metaheuristic algorithms, namely Artificial Rabbits Optimization (ARO), Nutcracker Optimization Algorithm (NOA), and Transit Search Optimization (TSO), in both distribution networks and microgrids. The DOCR coordination problem is formulated as a constrained optimization problem aiming to minimize the total operating time of primary relays while satisfying coordination constraints. The results show that, in the 6-bus microgrid system, NOA achieves the best performance with a total operating time of 0.5601 s, followed by TSO (0.6462 s) and ARO (0.8738 s). In contrast, previous distribution system results indicate that ARO provides superior performance in small and medium-scale networks (0.23 s in 3-bus and 2.86 s in 8-bus systems), while NOA becomes more effective in larger systems (5.25 s in 15-bus systems). These findings demonstrate that algorithm performance is strongly dependent on system structure, and optimization strategies developed for distribution systems cannot be directly transferred to microgrid environments without performance degradation. Therefore, system-aware algorithm selection is essential for effective DOCR coordination in modern power systems.

title: A Scalable OCR System to Bridge the Annotation Gap in Low Resource Languages
authors: Ashlin Deepa R N, R Lakshmi Sravani, Arra Deepthi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) has evolved significantly over the years and has achieved strong performance for languages such as English using traditional machine learning and deep learning techniques. However, OCR for Indian languages remain a challenging task due to the complexity of scripts and large variations in handwriting styles. Although research has been carried out in this domain, achieving real-time OCR performance for Indian languages is still limited. Most machine learning, deep learning, semi-supervised, and self-supervised approaches require a large amount of labeled or unlabeled data, which is scarce for Indian languages. This data scarcity becomes a major bottleneck in developing robust OCR systems for Indian scripts. To address this limitation, this paper focuses on analyzing Few-shot learning which is particularly suitable for low-resource scenarios. This technique aims to achieve accurate recognition using a pretrained model and a support set. The training is limited to a small set of labelled samples. The research shows that Few shots approach outperforms many State-of-the-art techniques under supervised learning. Here, the applicability of few-shot learning techniques for handwritten OCR in Indian languages is explored in detail.

title: Hardware Acceleration of User-Based Collaborative Filtering on an FPGA-Based SoC
authors: Burak Batmaz, Zeynep Batmaz
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: User-based collaborative filtering is effective in recommender systems, but its similarity computation stage is computationally expensive for embedded platforms. This paper presents a hardware-accelerated user-based collaborative filtering implementation on the AMD Xilinx Kria KV260 FPGA-based embedded SoC. The proposed design accelerates cosine similarity computation using a memory-centric architecture with optimized AXI interfaces for efficient data transfer. The accelerator is implemented using Vitis High-Level Synthesis and integrated with a C++ host application through the Xilinx Runtime framework. Experimental results on the MovieLens <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 M}$</tex> dataset show that the proposed approach preserves prediction accuracy while achieving up to approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times$</tex> speedup over a single-core software implementation. These results show the potential of FPGA-based acceleration for low-latency recommender systems in embedded environments.

title: Leveraging Harmonic Analysis for Feature Extraction and Selection in Time-Series Data Processing
authors: Yong Wang, Qiongqiong Yao, Xiaowen Chen, Jinghao Hei, Bing Liu, Jinke Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Traditional time-domain analysis and conventional frequency-domain deep learning architectures often lack adaptive frequency-domain feature selection mechanisms, easily leading to severe parameter redundancy, feature aliasing, and overfitting in low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) environments. To address this issue, this study proposes a comprehensive algorithm framework for time series data feature extraction and selection based on harmonic analysis (TF-HAS). This framework innovatively introduces the theory of diagonal bidirectional integral bispectrum (DBIB) and harmonic aggregation entropy (HaAgEn) based on higher-order statistics, effectively isolating and removing Gaussian background noise from the physical level using a quadratic phase coupling mechanism. Based on this, a spectral feature sparse selection mechanism integrating frequency-domain self-attention and Top-K masking is designed to actively block the backpropagation of high-frequency redundant noise. Furthermore, the top layer of the model combines contrastive time-frequency representation enhancement techniques with a KolmogorovArnold network (KAN) using B-spline functions, significantly improving the model's decision resolution and generalization performance under complex mappings. Experimental results based on the Mendeley power quality disturbance dataset, real sea trial axis-frequency electromagnetic field data, and long-sequence prediction benchmark datasets (ETTh1, Weather) demonstrate that the TF-HAS framework not only exhibits excellent error suppression capabilities in long-range prediction tasks but also achieves a classification accuracy of over 96.8% in extremely low signal-to-noise ratio environments. This study provides effective theoretical basis and technical reference for feature purification, dimensionality reduction selection, and lightweight modeling of complex non-stationary time series.

title:
ML-FS
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    -CDA- Meta-Learning-Driven Few-Shot Skin Lesion Segmentation with Cross-Dataset Adaptation
authors: M. Megala, T.R. Nisha Dayana
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Skin lesion segmentation is one of the most critical tasks for the early detection of melanoma; however, the performance of segmentation is often limited by the lack of annotated data and considerable domain variability among dermoscopic data available in various datasets e.g., International Skin Imaging Collaboration archive, PH2 Dataset and HAM10000. Conventional deep learning models are trained by feeding them with large labeled datasets and are not generalizable to imaging devices and acquisition settings. This paper proposes a framework for the Meta-Learning-Driven Few-Shot Skin Lesion Segmentation with Cross-Dataset Adaptation (ML-FS2-CDA) to solve these problems. The framework incorporates episodic meta-learning, prototype-guided attention segmentation, style-content feature disentanglement, domain adversarial alignment and self-supervised consistency regularization. Experimental evaluation with three public datasets shows the superior performance including Dice scores of 92.5% on ISIC 2018, 94.0% on PH2 and 96.5% on HAM10000, resulting in better performance than several state-of-the-art methods under limited supervision. The results confirm a greater cross domain robustness and a lower point of dependency for the annotations. The proposed approach provides a scalable and data efficient solution for the skin lesions segmentation in heterogeneity environments to be clinically reliable.

title: Design and Analysis of a Wideband mm-Wave Four-Port MIMO Antenna for 5G/6G Applications
authors: Maturi Thirupathi, R. Gunasundari, R. Tamijetchelvy
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: This paper presents the design and performance analysis of a wideband four-port multiple-input multipleoutput (MIMO) antenna system tailored for next-generation millimeter- wave (mm-wave) wireless communications. The proposed antenna operates across the 25 GHz to 45 GHz frequency range, providing an ultra-wide impedance bandwidth of 20 GHz. The design procedure involves a multistage approach, beginning with a single-element hybrid patch antenna that combines a rectangular hexagonal-truncated geometry for multi-resonant performance. This element is then extended into a four-port MIMO configuration, where four identical elements are arranged orthogonally to leverage polarization and spatial diversity. The antenna is designed on a 1.6 mm thick Rogers RT Duroid 5880 Substrate, selected for its low dielectric constant and low tangent which are crucial for high frequency performance. The design and simulations were conducted using HFSS. The results demonstrate a low return loss, high inter element isolation below −15 dB across the entire band and favorable diversity metrics, including a low Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC) and high Diversity Gain (DG), validating the antenna's suitability for high-speed, reliable mm-wave communication systems.

title: Automated Classification of Lung Nodules in CT Images using DenseNet-121 and Convolutional Block Attention Modules
authors: Sree Sankar J, Manimegalai.P, Jayalakshmi P. K
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Lung cancer remains one of the major causes of cancer-related mortality globally and early detection is essential for enhancing patient survival rates and treatment outcomes. A significant proportion of lung cancer cases originate from pulmonary nodules, which are small abnormal growths in lung tissue that may be benign or malignant. Therefore, accurate classification of lung nodules plays a vital role in early lung cancer diagnosis and clinical decision-making. Conventional computer-aided diagnosis systems and many existing deep learning approaches primarily rely on spatial feature extraction, which limits their ability to capture fine texture details and edge characteristics of lung nodules, leading to misclassification and reduced generalization performance. To address these issues, this study develops a DenseNet-121-based framework integrated with a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for automated lung nodule classification using Computed Tomography (CT) images. DenseNet-121 is employed as the backbone network to learn hierarchical and discriminative features, while CBAM improves feature representation through channel and spatial attention mechanisms that emphasize informative nodule regions and suppress irrelevant background information. The developed model was evaluated on a publicly available lung CT dataset obtained from Kaggle, consisting of both healthy lung images and lung nodule cases. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves an accuracy of 98.5%, with a precision of 98%, F1-score of 98.4% and a recall of 99%. Furthermore, Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) analysis yields an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.97, confirming strong discriminative capability and highlighting the effectiveness of the proposed model for reliable computer-aided lung cancer diagnosis.

title: AI in Health Informatics Through the Eyes of Medical Students
authors: Nikoleta Leventi, Alexandrina Vodenicharova
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: As artificial intelligence (AI) continues to reshape the landscape of healthcare, understanding how future physicians perceive its integration is critical for guiding education, policy, and ethical frameworks. This study presents the results of a cross-sectional study, based on a structured questionnaire administered to medical students, aiming to capture their opinions on the application of AI in clinical practice, its inclusion in medical education, and the ethical implications surrounding its use. The survey explores Medical University - Sofia medical students' awareness of current AI technologies in healthcare, and their confidence in AI's reliability and safety. It also investigates their views on the necessity of formal AI training. Ethical considerations form a core component of the study. The findings reveal nuanced perspectives, highlighting both enthusiasm and skepticism, and underscore the importance of preparing future healthcare professionals to critically engage with AI technologies. By analyzing responses, the paper identifies trends and gaps in knowledge, offering recommendations for curriculum development and ethical discourse. The results contribute to the broader conversation in Health Informatics about how to responsibly and effectively integrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> into medical practice, ensuring that tomorrow's doctors are equipped not only with technical understanding but also with ethical clarity.

title: Reducing Barriers to Eye Care: Glaucoma Detection with Novel Imaging Techniques
authors: Gurnoor Singh Dang, Gloria Wu, Ivan Chim, Swara Tewari, Priyal Badala, Brian Hoang
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Glaucoma is one of the leading causes of permanent blindness around the world, and it often progresses silently until patients experience serious loss of vision. Detecting the disease at an early stage is crucial for preserving sight, yet standard methods such as measuring intraocular pressure or performing visual field tests frequently fall short in sensitivity and reliability. In this study, we introduce a multimodal imaging strategy that brings together Optos ultra-widefield retinal imaging and optical coherence tomography to improve the identification of early glaucomatous changes. The analysis was conducted entirely with freely available open-source software, specifically IC Measure and ImageJ, showing that accurate measurements of both structural and vascular features can be obtained without the need for expensive proprietary systems. We extracted quantitative parameters from the optic nerve head and peripapillary regions, including cup to disc ratio, peripapillary atrophy, retinal nerve fiber layer thickness and texture, and peripapillary vessel density. When comparing normal, moderate, and severe glaucoma eyes, we observed consistent trends such as larger optic nerve cups, thinning of the retinal nerve fiber layer, and decreased vessel density, all strongly linked to disease severity. These results suggest that combining structural and vascular biomarkers from multimodal imaging can enable earlier and more accessible glaucoma diagnosis, supporting cost-effective screening programs in both advanced and resource-limited healthcare environments.

title: Decoupled Cost-Sensitive Thresholding–based LightGBM for Reliable Predictive Maintenance
authors: Vivek Kumar, Rashmiranjan Nayak, Umesh Chandra Pati
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Predictive maintenance (PdM) is an integral part of any modern industry. It aims to predict failure and maintenance schedules for key components across the industry using historical and current sensor data, leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI). However, the costs associated with industrial maintenance exhibit extreme asymmetry, with the economic penalty for a False Negative (missed failure) significantly higher than that for a False Positive (nuisance alarm). Furthermore, existing cost-sensitive boosting methods often struggle with mixed objectives, where misclassification costs are directly injected into the training phase (e.g., through gradient weighting). Subsequently, these models generate noisy alarm profiles that are difficult to tune to asset-specific risk tolerances. Therefore, there is a critical need for a framework that preserves ranking integrity while also allowing precise, decoupled cost optimization at deployment time. Hence, this paper proposes a Cost-Sensitive Thresholding-based Light Gradient Boosting Machine (Decoupled), abbreviated as (CST-LGBM-D) model, which separates cost-sensitive decision optimization from model training for reliable PdM. The proposed CST-LGBM-D model offers accuracy of 94.00%. The comprehensive experimental analysis on the benchmarked dataset supported by explainable AI validates the superiority of the proposed CST-LGBM-D model.

title: A Structured Approach to Grammar and Style Checking of Scientific Texts With ChatGPT
authors: Aldeniz Rashidov, Fatme Rashidova
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents a structured approach to grammar and style checking of scientific texts using ChatGPT. The approach is based on staged interaction between the author and the language model and supports iterative improvement of grammatical correctness, stylistic consistency, clarity, and conformity to academic writing standards. It includes a sequence of steps related to task initiation, specification of revision requirements, generation of an edited version, author evaluation, feedback-based refinement, and final validation of the text. An illustrative example is provided to demonstrate the practical applicability of the approach. In addition, the paper discusses its main advantages, limitations, and ethical aspects of using artificial intelligence in academic writing. The results indicate that ChatGPT can be used as an effective assistant for grammar and style checking of scientific texts when its output is subject to explicit author control.

title: Improving Web Text Classification Robustness Through Calibrated Transformer Fusion
authors: G. Lokesh, Vusthepalle Ganesh, Golla Krishna, Y.Venkata Pavan, S.Surendra Babu, C.Maddilety
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: There is high degree of diversity, noise and domain variability of web text in news articles, blogs, forums, and product reviews and therefore it is a challenging activity to robustly classify text. Single-model classifiers also tend to be affected by domain shift, style difference and adversarial noise. We overcome these shortcomings with TDE-Net, a Transformer-based Deep Ensemble model, a sequence of fine-tuned transformer encoders: BERT, RoBERTa, DeBERTa and DistilBERT, given the consideration of a small meta-fusion network and a calibrated voting process. To improve structural strength and generalization, the ensemble takes advantage of the complementary representational advantages of both models. The same enhancements are detected in the experiments of benchmark datasets, such as AG News, 20 Newsgroups and noisy web-news datasets. The accuracy increment of TDE-Net over the optimal single transformer baseline with macro-F1 increments of 3.1-7.0 is between 2.6 to 6.8 percentage points. Transformer ensembles are superior to web text classification because the proposed model achieves an average accuracy and macro-F1 of 93.2 and 0.927, respectively of 89.7 and 0.889 of single models, respectively.

title: Evaluating Human- and AI-Generated Code Quality Based on Code Smell
authors: Di Guo, Yadong Zhou, Dong Xu, Juan Wang, Xueli Jia

title: Robustness of SAM: Segment Anything under Corruptions and beyond_supp1-3698837.pdf
authors: Yu Qiao
publish: N/A
abstract: Segment anything model (SAM) is notable for its ability to cut out any object and its impressive zero-shot transfer performance with prompt guidance. However, its robustness under corruption remains underexplored. Prior work indicates that SAM is biased towards texture rather than shape. We first evaluate its robustness against style transfer (synthetic corruption), followed by 15 common corruptions, and find that SAM demonstrates robustness in these scenarios. Moreover, we assess SAM's resilience to local patch occlusion and adversarial patch attacks, finding it resilient to occlusion but vulnerable to patch attacks. We observe that patch attacks disrupt the feature map and misguide the attention mechanism, whereas patch occlusion does not cause such misguidance. Given that visible patch attacks are easily detectable, we further investigate robustness against global adversarial attacks that remain imperceptible to humans by employing basic attacks and a new mask-aware loss. Our findings suggest that SAM's vulnerability primarily stems from its more reliance on high-frequency signals compared to low-frequency ones. Moreover, we further investigate the targeted transferability of adversarial examples and propose a contrastive learning-driven approach with a plug-and-play modulation module, enabling more effective transferability across different SAM models. In addition, we investigate a range of defense strategies to enhance the robustness of SAM, as well as stronger and more adaptive attack methods. Overall, this work provides a comprehensive study of SAM's robustness across diverse scenarios and is expected to offer valuable insights into its practical applicability and effectiveness in real-world settings.

title: Enhancing Depression Detection on Social Media with DSM-V Guided RAG and Fine-Tuned DeepSeek-R1_supp1-3699501.docx
authors: Shouliang Qi
publish: N/A
abstract: Depression significantly contributes to global disability, yet remains underdiagnosed due to lack of awareness. Social media platforms provide rich, real-time data reflecting users' emotional and psychological states, making them promising resources for automated depression detection. However, general-purpose Large Language Models (LLMs) often perform suboptimally in clinical domains, lacking interpretability and domain specificity. To address these limitations, this study introduces a knowledge-augmented framework combining Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) guided by clinical psychiatric criteria from the DSM-V with a fine-tuned DeepSeek-R1-7B model. We constructed a domain specific knowledge base comprising 329 expert-reviewed, retrieval-friendly DSM-V-derived entries. By retrieving relevant DSM-V-TR diagnostic criteria alongside user posts, our framework provides clinically grounded contextual information that may support human review by relating model predictions to established psychiatric criteria. Experiments on three balanced social media datasets showed that the DSM-V-guided RAG module, when combined with fine-tuned DeepSeek-R1-7B, achieved improved performance over the compared settings under the current experimental protocol. The retrieved DSM-V entries also provided clinically grounded contextual information that may help interpret model predictions. These findings suggest that DSM-V-guided knowledge augmentation is a promising direction for improving the transparency of benchmark-based depression detection on social media.

title: Environmental Adaptation Enabled by an Amplitude-Tunable Traveling Wave Robot with a Soft Corkscrew (ATWBot)_supp1-3697245.mp4
authors: Aiguo Song

title: A Comprehensive General Model of Tendon-Actuated Concentric Tube Robots with Multiple Tubes and Tendons_supp1-3699204.mp4
authors: Pejman Kheradmand
publish: N/A
abstract: Tendon-actuated concentric tube mechanisms combine the advantages of tendon-driven continuum robots and concentric tube robots while addressing their respective limitations. They overcome the restricted degrees of freedom often seen in tendon-driven designs and mitigate issues such as restricted curvatures associated with concentric tube robots. However, a complete and general mechanical model for these systems remains an open problem. In this work, we propose a Cosserat rod-based framework for modeling the general case of n concentric tubes, each actuated by mi tendons, where i = {1, . . . , n}. The model allows each tube to twist and elongate while enforcing a shared centerline for bending. We validate the proposed framework through experiments with two-tube and three-tube assemblies under various tendon routing configurations, achieving tip prediction errors < 4% of the robot’s total length. We further demonstrate the model’s generality by applying it to existing robots in the field, where maximum tip deviations remain around 5% of the total length. This model provides a foundation for accurate shape estimation and control of advanced tendon-actuated concentric tube robots.

title: Robust Intra-Pulse Frequency Coding Against Time-Gated Frequency-Following Jamming via Belief-State Reinforcement Learning_supp1-3699318.pdf
authors: Zhenhua Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Constrained by instantaneous receiver bandwidth and intermittent jamming mechanisms, radar anti-jamming against time-gated frequency-following jammers inherently degrades into a time-frequency dual-domain Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). To overcome these physical constraints, this paper proposes an end-to-end belief-state reinforcement learning framework for robust intra-pulse frequency coding. By directly mapping short-time Fourier transform (STFT) sequences to agility commands, the architecture eliminates feature truncation errors. Furthermore, a state-transition-aware reward mechanism is designed to establish a phase transition boundary, analytically resolving the exploration-exploitation dilemma under partial observability. Feature manifold analysis reveals that the agent dynamically reconstructs the jammer's generative mechanism rather than memorizing static sequences. Simulations demonstrate the method achieves near-optimal steady-state robustness and enables rapid policy re-convergence against non-stationary strategy shifts without offline retraining.

title: A High-Speed Omnidirectional Ceiling Mobile Robot Using a Synchronized Crawler-Hanging Mechanism_supp1-3699154.mp4
authors: Hiroto Hasegawa
publish: N/A
abstract: We present a ceiling mobile robot capable of high-speed transport with versatile routing. Based on the crawler-hanging mechanism proposed in previous work, the robot introduces several key improvements. First, we enhanced hanging stability during high-speed travel by synchronizing the ceiling-hanging motion with the crawler's rotation via guide mechanisms. This approach achieved reliable operation at 1.35 m/s, a 35% improvement over earlier designs. Additionally, a position correction method utilizing claw insertion into ceiling holes effectively corrected <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula>2 mm positional errors per hanging action, ensuring accurate alignment during travel. Second, we developed a direction-changing mechanism for static ceiling panels by relocating rolling mechanisms from turntables to the robot itself. This design enabled turning at 45 deg/s with a 60 kg payload and sideway movement at 0.2 m/s, enhancing routing versatility. These advancements improve ceiling robot mobility and expand the applicability of ceiling transport systems for industrial and logistics environments.

title: Collaborative Acceleration for Server-Side Multi-User View Synthesis_supp1-3694560.pdf
authors: Chubo Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Real-time performance optimization is critical for interactive applications like Virtual Reality (VR). Existing methods primarily focus on single-user scenarios and often overlook spatial redundancy in multi-user environments. As collaborative experiences grow, delivering real-time rendering on lightweight devices becomes increasingly challenging. This paper proposes a spatial-similarity-based clustering method and a server-side multi-user multi-view rendering framework. By leveraging cross-user redundancy in reference views, the framework reuses intermediate transformation results to improve computational efficiency. Server-side rendering further enhances performance on lightweight clients. Experiments on NVIDIA A100 GPU show that our method reduces computational workload by 23.68%-24.10% and H2D memory copy overhead by 66.81% compared to traditional DIBR. Compared with VINet and IBRNet, our approach achieves higher efficiency in computation, memory, and runtime, enabling real-time synthesis of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4096 \times 2048$</tex-math></inline-formula> images within 16.63 ms (approximately 60 FPS). Project code and scripts: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yuanaiya/CASSMVS</uri>.
