title: A Structured Approach for Integrating Environmental Impact Assessment Throughout the IC Design Flow
authors: Maxime Péralta, Thibault Halter, Ivan Miro-Panades, Chiara Sandionigi
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: The production, use and disposal of integrated circuits cause multiple impacts on the environment, as a substantial carbon footprint, intensive water and energy use, and the generation of toxic waste. These impacts must be evaluated and mitigated since the design of the circuits. Despite the rising number of initiatives to quantify and optimize the end-to-end carbon footprint of circuits, there is a lack of analysis about how to integrate the assessment of multiple environmental impacts along the design flow. To address this gap, we propose a structured approach for the systematic integration of environmental assessment throughout the flow and we establish key requirements for ecodesign tools. We demonstrate that: 1) a preliminary, coarse-grain evaluation of the environmental impacts can be performed since the front-end phase of the design flow, but some metrics for environmental optimization can be applied only in the back-end; 2) designing circuits for multiple environmental criteria yields distinct solutions compared to optimizing for carbon only.

title: Life Cycle Assessment of 3D Stacked Multi-Chip Assemblies: Comparison of Three Multi-Layer CMOS Image Sensors
authors: Maxime Péralta, Mathilde Billaud
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: To prevent the growth of information and communication technologies impacts, environmental impact assessments should be systematized using the life cycle assessment (LCA) method when developing new computing devices. However, this is difficult to implement due to challenges related to the life cycle inventory, lack of adequate tooling and lack of data. This is particularly true for complex devices such as multichip assemblies. This paper introduces a novel parametric LCA model for 3D stacked multi-chip assemblies which only requires a limited set of design parameters. Using this LCA model, we present the first comparative LCA of three multi-layer CMOS Image Sensors (CIS) from the literature. Our results show that the environmental impacts of multi-layer CIS are primarily driven by the silicon wafer manufacturing. The most powerefficient CIS can only offset its greater fabrication impacts after a long utilization period. Our results also demonstrate that stacking a third layer of silicon can reduce manufacturing impacts by reducing silicon die sizes. The proposed LCA model and environmental data are released as open source to facilitate future LCA works on digital devices.

title: Fuzzy Linguistic Summarization in the Electronic Communications Sector: Protoform-Based Natural Language Insights for Digitalization and Decision Support
authors: Ali Burak Akbulut, Diyar Akay, Ümran Kaya
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The electronic communications sector, while encompassing service provision, network operation, and equipment supply in its traditional operational framework, has evolved beyond this to become a strategic data source through which regional digitalization capacity and socio-economic development can be empirically examined via access levels, usage dynamics, and technology transformation indicators. Among these data sources, fiber infrastructure data, mobile internet usage statistics, and broadband subscription figures stand out as the core indicators. Analyzing these data not only yields valuable insights into levels of digitalization and infrastructure development but also facilitates the correlation of socio-economic characteristics with fiber access, mobile internet usage, and broadband subscription rates. However, the multi-dimensional and time-varying nature of these data presents significant challenges in their interpretation and evaluation, particularly for decision-makers. In this context, the present study proposes a protoform-based fuzzy linguistic summarization (FLS) framework designed to represent data from the electronic communications sector via natural language expressions, with an application and demonstration performed on Türkiye's 2024 sector data. The findings reveal that FLS techniques can be employed as an innovative tool to strengthen decision support processes in the electronic communications sector.

title: Multimodal Paradigmatic Synergies in Hyperdimensional and Quantum-Inspired Architectures for Robust Speech Gender Analytics
authors: Selvi M, Rupesh J, Kameshwari M
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Gender recognition through speaking is one of the main processes of intelligent audio processing systems, which find application in adaptive interfaces, biometric authentication and real-time communication aid. In this work, four emerging trends in computational paradigm, Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC), Echo State Networks (ESN), Extreme Learning Machines (ELM), and QuantumInspired Evolutionary Algorithms (QIEA) are evaluated to classify genders using publicly available gender recognition dataset (Voice Gender Recognition Dataset) provided by the UCI Repository. The data set contains pre-computed acoustic characteristics, such as spectral characteristics, frequencyrelated characteristics, and prosodic characteristics that are usually utilized in speech analysis. Each of the models was trained and assessed within a common and reproducible experimental design, such as noisy conditions. It is shown experimentally, that HDC has the best accuracy of 99.8 being higher than ESN <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(97.6 \%)$</tex> and ELM <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(95.4 \%)$</tex>, and also with better ability to resist noise. The such comparative analysis, as compared to the current studies on gender recognition using speech, illuminates the competitiveness, efficiency and robustness of these non-traditional learning architectures especially in resource- constrained applications with real-time applications.

title: Blockchain-Augmented Federated Learning for Secure and Healthcare Diagnosis from Distributed Electronic Health Records
authors: Joshuva Arockia Dhanraj, Prashant Dhongle, R.Tamilamuthan, M.Lakshmanan, Nilesh Ashtankar, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The rising demand on smart and safe healthcare has fostered the innovation at the cross-breeding of Artificial Intelligence (AI), data protection, and decentralized technologies. The study proposes a unique solution that combines Federated Learning (FL) and Blockchain smart contracts to facilitate secure, privacy-preserving and real-time sharing of Electronic Health Record (EHR) and diagnosis of diseases. The framework will enable the collaboration of multiple hospital nodes to train AI models without pandemic data transfer but having the guarantee to immutable logging, consent management, and verification of model updates. The FL+Blockchain reached a high overall accuracy of 93%, precision of 92.8%, recall of 92.7% and AUC of 96% on the UCI Heart Disease dataset having 300 records of patients distributed through three nodes compared to centralized FL and non-blockchain based FL. Effective smart contracts with low gas prices and a low latency of up to 300ms and enables real-time data access with the ability to be compliant with the data protection laws, such as the HIPAA, and GDPR. A trustworthy and scalable solution to AI-driven healthcare is proposed in the form of the system; it solves problems of data privacy, auditability, and interoperability and thus stands out as a good candidate for the digital health infrastructure in the future.

title: Non-Invasive Detection of Copd Using Acoustic Features Extracted From Sustained Phonation
authors: Seethalakshmi S, Dakshanaa Shree Selvakumar, Haripriya V H, M. P. Actlin Jeeva
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is a progressive respiratory disorder characterized by persistent airflow limitation and breathing difficulty. It is caused mainly due to long-term exposure to harmful particles or gases, especially cigarette smoke. Early detection of this disease is essential to prevent its progression and improve patient outcomes. The current diagnosis includes traditional methods such as spirometry, pulmonary function test (PFT), biochemical approaches like extracellular vesicle (EV) analysis, etc,. Although these methods are clinically successful, they require trained medical professionals and considerable effort from patients. Hence, the current work proposes a non-invasive method based on acoustic features extracted from sustained phonation for training a predictive model to detect the presence of the disease. Initially the acoustic features are extracted using the eGeMAPS feature set and later the significant features are selected using recursive feature analysis (RFE). The obtained features are used to train the machine learning models namely, support vector machine (SVM) and random forest (RF). The clinical validation of the significant features extracted is carried out through statistical testing and Cohen's D-test. Experimental results were obtained through cross-validation method of leave-one-speaker-out (LOSO), and receiver operating characteristics (ROC) curves. The area under the curves (AUC) shows that the proposed feature set effectively discriminates the COPD inviduals from healthy individuals.

title: Grid Connected Energy Net-Metering System for Home Solar Installations
authors: Boda Balaji, Devireddy Vaibhav, Kumbam Shiva Charan Reddy, MD. Ayaan Khaif, K Praveen, Sama Vinod Reddy
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Measuring energy accurately under the condition of the bidirectional power flow needs to be addressed by the growing use of residential solar photovoltaic (PV) systems. Such applications cannot be done with conventional energy meters and therefore net metering is essential in making the bills transparent. This paper introduces a grid-based energy net-metering unit of home solar installation based on an embedded microcontroller. Energy imported through the utility grid and that exported through the PV system is measured by two digital energy meters independently. The optocouplers isolate pulse outputs and the microcontroller is a PIC16F72 that is used to calculate net energy consumption using meter constants set with meter constants. Imported energy, exported energy and net billing units real-time values can be seen on a 16x2 LCD. The experimental findings indicate precise measurements of the energy in both directions and constant functioning in variable load situations.

title: Low-power, High-Speed Comparator Design using FinFETs for ADC Applications
authors: N. Geetha Rani, T. Nagalakshmi, M. Sireesha, Y. Renuka, N.Praveen Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In analog-to-digital converters (ADCs), comparators are crucial components that convert analog input levels into digital decisions. However, when technology advances into deep- nanometer nodes, conventional CMOS-based comparators experience short-channel effects, leakage currents, variability, and decreased noise margins. The speed and power efficiency of contemporary high-resolution ADCs are diminished by these constraints. FinFET technology is a promising alternative for planar CMOS because it offers improved electrostatic control, decreased leakage, and increased drive current. The design and implementation of an 18nm FinFET-based comparator using Cadence Virtuoso is presented in this study. In comparison to a 45 nm CMOS design, simulation findings show that the suggested FinFET comparator achieves a 56% reduction in power consumption and a 27% improvement in delay.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Early Detection of Cashew Plant Disease
authors: Geet Sahu
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Cashew is a crop that has a very high nutritional and economic importance with its kernel, shell and cashew shell oil significantly contributing to other industries. Nonetheless, pest and disease outbreaks are a major threat to the production of cashews and this has led to significant economic losses. The present paper presents a deep learning-based system that will be used to automate the process of detecting diseases in cashew leaves. The system uses a hybrid architecture which uses EfficientNetB0 and Vision Transformer (ViT). The proposed algorithm successfully classifies the plant images into various disease categories, providing a successful substitute of the conventional check-ups. The CCMT (Cashew, Cassava, Maize, and Tomato) dataset presented an excellent and diverse cashew leaf image dataset to be used in training and evaluation. EfficientNetB0 can gather local fine-grained features, while ViT can capture global relationships, which leads to better classification. The model outperformed contemporary advanced methods with an accuracy rating of 98.30%. The solution is aimed at real-world use and could be incorporated into mobile or web-based platforms, providing the farmer with an immediate diagnosis of the disease, allowing it to intervene in time and ensuring its long-term sustainable farming. Source code: https://github.com/Geet-Sahu/Cashew-disease-detection

title: A Lightweight Hardware Accelerator for Fixed-Point Least-Squares Estimation in RISC-V SoCs
authors: Muhammet Emin Akgun, Alp Arslan Bayrakci
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Solving overdetermined linear systems is a fundamental computational challenge in real-time embedded applications, such as image processing, autonomous navigation, and robotics. In these domains, parameter estimation frequently requires resolving sensor data with high precision and low latency. Since the direct inverse of a non-square matrix is not applicable, the Least Squares Method is utilized to minimize observation errors. Traditional software-based implementations on generalpurpose processors often fail to meet the stringent latency and power constraints of edge devices. This paper presents a hardware/software co-design methodology to accelerate the Least Squares solution, specifically targeting small-scale, fixedsize parameter estimation problems (e.g., 4-by-4 state vectors). Following an algorithmic analysis, the Modified Cholesky method was identified as the optimal, square-root-free approach for hardware implementation. The proposed custom Verilog accelerator is integrated as a memory-mapped peripheral within a RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC). We utilize a real-world Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) dataset as a case study to validate the workflow. Experimental results demonstrate a 3.74x to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.21 x$</tex> speedup compared to highly optimized software-only implementations, proving the architecture's viability for real-time edge computing.

title: Improving Military Object Detection under Noisy Conditions Using Spatial Denoising Pipeline and ResNet-50 based Deep Features
authors: Patrick Wah Sekey, Oscar Kiamba Munyao, Mark Aondohemba Ortese, Marron Tarlue, Ram Chandra Barik, Niranjan Behera
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Military object recognition under difficult conditions continues to be a very complicated problem for defense technology in the modern era. Robust object recognition from noisy images is of paramount importance in the next generation of defense technologies. In this study, a complete framework for the recognition of military objects which are familiar to noises under battlefield conditions is developed. The proposed approach comprehensively computes and analyzes the performance of the following filters for denoising; Gaussian filter, Median filter, Non Local Means, BM3D, DnCNN, and Autoencoder-Based Denoiser for the recognition of military objects with Gaussian noise, Salt-and-Paper noise, Motion Blur 0°, Motion Blur 45°, and low light noises using PSNR and SSIM metrics. We used a ResNet-50 classifier on the same data set with Gaussian noise and salt and pepper noise. The results show the best performing Non Local Means Filter and Median filter for visual robustness. For the proposed model, accuracy was 78.79% for the clean images and was still maintained at 76-70% accuracy levels in cases of low noise. However, in cases of high noise, accuracy levels were significantly decreased to 20%. With the use of the Median Filter, accuracy could be restored to 76%. An accuracy rate of 98.98% could be achieved through training.

title: Transfer Matrix Method Based Electromagnetic Simulation Software for Analysis and Design of Radomes and Radar Absorbing Materials
authors: Ada Akten, Taylan Ünver, Fahrettin Tural, Sultan Can
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Radomes are single or multilayer dielectric structures designed to protect antenna systems without negatively affecting their electromagnetic performance. Radar Absorbing Materials (RAMs) are structures made to not to reflect radar waves. Accurate characterization and precise electromagnetic design is critical for successful implementation of radome and RAM. Conventional full-wave solvers provide high-fidelity results but require a substantial amount of computational resources. This paper presents a performant radome analysis tool based on the Transfer Matrix Method (TMM) implemented in C++. The proposed approach models the structure analytically thus reducing complexity. The software supports frequency sweeps, multiple incident angles and both TE and TM polarizations yielding same results for homogeneous radome and RAM structures as commercial software by also reduces time-complexity from order of minutes to order of a few milliseconds. By providing integrated tuning capabilities, the proposed software simplifies the design process of radomes and RAMs.

title: A Predictive Rule-Based Energy Management Framework Incorporating Driving Pattern Clustering for Hybrid Electric Vehicles
authors: Johnsi Rani J, Dr Suresh K
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Hybrid Electric Vehicles (HEVs) is need an Energy Management System (EMS) that can maintain good fuel economy and stable battery State of Charge (SoC) even when driving conditions is changing very fast. This work proposes a lightweight predictive rule-based EMS that adapts to traffic and road variations by combine offline driving pattern clustering with online mode detection. First, an offline dataset containing vehicle speed, acceleration, and road grade are divided into sliding windows and is represented using lowdimensional statistical feature, such as speed mean/variance, mean absolute acceleration, stop ratio, and grade statistic. Kmeans clustering is then used to form representative driving modes, including urban stop-and-go, suburban cruising, and highway operation. For each mode, a dedicated rule set are constructed to manage engine ON/OFF decision, enginebattery power sharing, and battery charge/discharge limit, while SoC hysteresis is applied to reduce frequent switching. During real-time operation, a nearest-centroid window classifier identify the current driving mode and activates the matching rule set, enable predictive control. The method is validated on a backward-facing HEV model using mixed driving profile with speed transients and road grade disturbance. Results indicate bounded SoC behavior, reduce unnecessary engine idling, and consistent power split response across different driving modes. The proposed EMS is interpretable, computationally efficiency, and suitable as a real-time baseline or as a complementary layer for future intelligent EMS framework.

title: Deep Learning-Based Framework for Multi-Stage Classification of Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: Arla Gopala Krishna, Dhiraj Kumar Raut, Ch. Praveen Kumar, Sanjay Kumar, Pandu Sowkuntla
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The computer-aided system to be used to detect Alzheimer disease (AD) should have computationally efficient models that are resistant to distortions that usually occur in medical imaging. This work presents a lightweight model of deep learning that is robustness-based and has the ability to classify AD by stage using structural MRI data. The given framework is based on the EfficientNetB0 backbone because it has a compound scaling property to enable efficient features learning with lower training complexity. The label-conscious augmentation and adversarial learning through the Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM) is also optimized to give a better resistance to imperceptible perturbation. Experimental assessment illustrates a test accuracy of 98.36% percent and an adversarial accuracy of 99.43% under Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM)-based adversarial evaluation through four stages of AD, namely, Non-Demented, Very Mild-Demented, Mild-Demented and Moderate-Demented. These findings suggest that the proposed solution is capable of high generalization, minimal computation cost, and high resistance to adversarial changes and would be an effective solution to clinical decision support in a resource-limited healthcare environment.

title: CNN-Based Turbidity Estimation in Dairy Farms Environments
authors: Abdullah Al Rawi, Behrooz Pakzadeh, Pramod Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Turbidity is one the most important parameters used for determining water quality and reported in Nephelometric Turbidity Units (NTU). Often turbidity of agricultural and drinking water is measured using a turbidity meter, which quantifies the amount of light scattered by particles suspended in a water sample. The monitoring of turbidity in a farm environment, however, is challenging due to the cost of monitoring, and technical complexity associated with the measurements. Artificial intelligence (AI) provides significant potential to advance agricultural water quality monitoring rapidly and cost effectively. In this study, we developed an AI based tool for monitoring turbidity of wastewater produced in dairy farms. A smartphone-based Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) system was developed for real-time turbidity monitoring, and this application was deployed in commercial dairy farms to determine the characteristics of wastewater. A ResNet-34 architecture with a custom classification head was trained on 1,272 smartphonecaptured water sample images across six turbidity classes: 017.13 NTU, 25-90 NTU, 150-450 NTU, 600-1200 NTU, 13002500 NTU, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>2500$</tex> NTU. The system achieved a validation accuracy of 92.94%, with F1-scores exceeding 88% across all turbidity classes. This AI based water monitoring tool addresses a critical need of agricultural and rural communities and supports sustainable agricultural water management.

title: Forecasting Agentic AI Resource Consumption in Business Processes: A Three Dimensions Cost Estimation Conceptual Framework
authors: Ioannis Patias
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid integration of agentic artificial intelligence (AI) into enterprise operations necessitates robust methodologies for evaluating business processes. Traditional frameworks, such as the American Productivity & Quality Center (APQC) Process Classification Framework, provide standardized taxonomies but lack mechanisms to quantify AIdriven resource consumption. This paper introduces a novel methodology that combines APQC's process definitions with a three-dimensional (3D) AI cost estimation model. The model decomposes AI workload into three parametrized components: Input complexity (I), measured by token count and semantic depth; Model usage (M), quantified by GPU time and memory footprint; and Output formulation (O), evaluated by token length, modality, and structural sophistication. These dimensions are integrated into a cost function <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P=\alpha \cdot I+\beta$</tex>. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M+\gamma \cdot O$</tex> where coefficients are tunable to balance cost recovery with market positioning. By applying this formula to APQC-defined processes, organizations can estimate the computational expense of AI-driven evaluation tasks. Industry frequency estimates are then incorporated to forecast monthly utilization, enabling the calculation of average total costs per process. The methodology provides a transparent, scalable, and adaptable framework for aligning AI resource consumption with business priorities. Ultimately, this approach empowers enterprises to make informed decisions about AI deployment, balancing efficiency, cost, and strategic value in process management.

title: Enhancing Crop Recommendation Accuracy using Quantum Support Vector Machines on Complex Agricultural Datasets
authors: Varun S, Abirami S
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Crop recommendation systems are very important in sustainable agriculture and yield optimization. Most of the traditional machine learning algorithms, such as SVM, do not work well on datasets for which there is a strong nonlinear relationship between the features such as soil parameters, climatic conditions and labels of crops. In this paper, we investigate the performance difference between classical SVM and Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) when trained with highly complex, non-linear agriculture dataset. A synthetic non-linear dataset was constructed to mimic challenging real-world agriculture patterns, and is used to evaluate both the models fairly. Experimental results shows that classical SVM struggles to model deep feature dependencies, while QSVM significantly outperforms it by 10% increase in the accuracy due to the advantage of quantum feature encoding and high-dimensional Hilbert space mapping. This paper illustrates the potential benefits of quantum machine learning in different agricultural applications and indicates the advantages of quantum-enhanced models when dealing with challenging data sets.

title: Development of a Neural Network-Based Algorithm for HVAC Systems Optimization, Integrating Key Environmental Variables in BEMS
authors: Daniel F. Espejel-Blanco, Oscar G. Meza-Pacheco, Jose A. Hoyo-Montano, Carlos A. Pereyda-Pierre, Jesus M. Tarin-Fontes, Jose J. E. Medina-Gutierrez
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation or a wireless network based om LoRa technology and Heltec WiFi LoRa 32 v3 microcontroller, integrated with a neural network algorithm to estimate the Mean Radiant Temperature (MRT), with the objective of reduce the energy consumption in Bulding Energy Management Systems (BEMS). The information obtained improves the energy efficiency of HVAC systems, ensuring thermal comfort and optimizing electrical consumption. A prototype was validated in an academic environment, achieving reliable data transmission up to 1000 meters and significant operational stability. The project seeks to contribute to the field of building automation and communications allowing greater efficiency in energy consumption, reducing costs and minimizing environmental impact. Finally, communication validation is planned.

title: Secure Communication Protocols for IoT-Integrated Electric Vehicle Ecosystems
authors: R C Ilambirai, J Jayasudha, Joly M, S Srinivasan, Suganya J, K Sasikala
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The combination of Internet of Things (IoT) technology with electric vehicles (EVs) has transformed contemporary transportation, facilitating functionalities such as intelligent charging, anticipatory maintenance, and vehicle-toinfrastructure communication. This interconnectedness renders EV ecosystems vulnerable to cybersecurity concerns, including as data breaches, unauthorized access, and signal interference. This paper examines secure communication protocols tailored for IoT-enabled EV networks, emphasizing lightweight authentication, encryption, and integrity-checking procedures appropriate for resource-constrained devices. Simulation results indicate that the proposed methods markedly improve data security while preserving low latency and great network efficiency. Encrypted data transfer demonstrated a 92% decrease in susceptibility to eavesdropping, whilst authentication techniques decreased unauthorized access attempts by 87 %. Furthermore, the communication protocols demonstrated little computing cost, enabling seamless operation without detracting from vehicle performance. These results underscore the viability and significance of deploying strong, scalable, and cost-effective security solutions inside IoTintegrated EV ecosystems, therefore enhancing the safety, reliability, and trustworthiness of intelligent transportation systems (ITS).

title: A Memory-Augmented Learning Framework for Adaptive Resource Allocation in Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Hema V, Palanivel Rajan S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs) require intelligent and adaptive resource allocation strategies to cope with dynamic spectrum availability and non-stationary interference conditions. Existing optimization and learning-based approaches often fail to simultaneously capture short-term spectrum fluctuations and long-term usage patterns, limiting adaptability and stability. This paper proposes a novel memory-augmented learning framework that integrates structured short-term and long-term spectrum memory within a unified decision architecture. The key innovation lies in the introduction of selective experience recall, which prioritizes high-utility historical observations to accelerate convergence and suppress noisy or outdated spectrum information. Furthermore, the learning process is formulated as a constrained optimization problem with memory-conditioned policy updates, enabling robust and adaptive spectrum allocation. Experimental evaluation on the CASS Spectrum Dataset demonstrates significant improvements in throughput, fairness, energy efficiency, adaptation speed, and primary user protection compared to conventional rule-based, optimization-based, and learning-based approaches, while maintaining moderate computational overhead.

title: Justification of Proposals for Detecting Data Transmission Channels of Low-Observable Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Serhii Herasymov, Oleg Cherniavskiy, Orest Sinyavsky, Iryna Aksonova, Oleksandr Kruchinin, Miroslav Pohasii
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The study deals with the issues of performing radio equipment tasks for air target detection, paying special attention to small-sized (low-visibility) unmanned aerial vehicles. The threat of low-visibility UAVs is revealed. It is justified to take measures to increase the combat efficiency of weapons, reduce preparation time, and increase the time for making decisions. The features of performing airspace surveys for air target detection, including low-visibility ones, are specified. The following issues are analyzed: performing sequential and simultaneous airspace surveys, deterministic and adaptive air target search methods, circular airspace surveys, and raster airspace surveys. The use of a multi-position radar complex consisting of several radio devices integrated into a single system is suggested. The benefits of the suggested multi-position radar complex are: the possibility of calculating the parameters of the air target flight trajectory on the basis of the measured radar coordinates of air targets. The development of a mathematical model of the species observation process, founded on the integration of radar measurement data for precise determination of air target flight parameters, will allow establishing requirements for the protection of data transmission through air target control channels.

title: A Comprehensive Review of AI-Driven NPC Intelligence and Procedural Content Generation in Video Games
authors: Rehan Firoz, Nikita
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The review presents the results of impactful articles published in the period between 2011 and 2025 and is based on high-impact publications, including the Nature Machine Intelligence, IEEE Transactions on Games, and major ACM conferences. It is a comparative study of the major methodologies, such as deep learning, reinforcement learning, evolutionary algorithms, large language models, and generative AI.With the introduction of the advanced artificial intelligence, the development of video games has undergone a fundamental change, in terms of the Non-Player Character (NPC) behavior and Procedural Content Generation (PCG). Such evolution has made it possible to produce adaptive and lifelike NPCs, as well as the production of game environments, levels and narratives dynamically and automatically. The article follows the path of the field since the early rule-based systems to the modern techniques of data, showcasing the key innovations and their recorded influence on the field.

title: Comparative Analysis of NI USRP-2920 SDR and HAMEG 5006 for Live FM Signal Strength Measurement
authors: Priya A, Mohammed Aamir Khan Lodi, Hasan Babu H, R. Iniyavan, Vanmathi M, T.Athisaya Anushya
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Accurate spectrum monitoring is vital for wireless systems, cognitive radio, and regulatory compliance. This paper compares two measurement platforms: the NI USRP-2920 Software-Defined Radio (SDR) and the HAMEG 5006 spectrum analyzer. Real-time experiments evaluated signal strength, acquisition fidelity, and spectrum visualization across wideband frequencies. Performance was analyzed in terms of sensitivity, flexibility, and accuracy, highlighting trade-offs between a reconfigurable SDR and a hardware-centric analyzer. Results show where each platform excels, providing evidence-based guidelines for selecting instrumentation in applications from wideband sensing to adaptive signal analysis. These findings help researchers and engineers choose optimal tools for next-generation wireless monitoring.

title: Why Beginners Abandon Quantum Programming: Barriers to Developer Experience and a Scaffolding Approach to Development Environment Setup
authors: Eduard Ciriaco, Vlad Stirbu, António Correia
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Quantum software engineering (QSE) has sparked worldwide interest due to its potential to transform classical software engineering tasks, unlock breakthroughs across fields and industries, and optimize the development of solutions for everyday practice. As an emerging area of study focused on equipping developers with the tools to design and build quantum programs and applications, QSE faces significant challenges in retaining new developers. Similarly to what happens in classical high-performance computing, quantum programming tools and frameworks can fail to provide an effective developer experience for beginners. Chief among the barriers is the fragmentation of libraries, backend interfaces, languages, and development tools (e.g., compilers, debuggers) that entails additional configuration effort and limits interoperability across quantum software ecosystems, architectures, and models deployed on virtual machines within cloud computing infrastructures. To address the nontrivial setup overhead that beginners often face when configuring a new quantum computing programming project, this work proposes a graphical user interface (GUI)-based solution that streamlines installation and configuration through customizable, pre-built settings. By doing so, the solution is intended to lower the barrier to entry for users without a strong computer science background so that they can start coding and running quantum programs with minimal manual setup.

title: HAWK-ViT-CNNNet: A Knowledge-Guided Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Breast Cancer Diagnosis
authors: Senthil Velan Sugantham, D Venkata Vara Prasad, Balasubramanian Viswanathan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Breast cancer has been ranked among the major causes of cancer related deaths amongst women, where mammograms are usually compromised due to overlap of tissues and the characteristics of the lesions being very smooth. This paper will be based on the creation of a highly accurate diagnostic model that is interpretable based on the CBIS-DDSM dataset. The most important novelty is that the knowledge of radiologists is combined with deep learning by introducing a Hybrid Attention-Weighted Knowledge (HAWK) mechanism that combines convolutional and transformer-based learning. The proposed HAWK-ViT-CNNNet is a hybrid of CNN-based local feature extraction, Vision Transformer-based global context modeling, and knowledge-directed attention fusion to give attention to clinically significant tumor areas. The results of experiment show better performance with 98.9% accuracy, 99.12% AUC, and much lower false discovery rate than the current models. These findings support the strength, stability, and feasibility of the suggested framework. The paper has come to the conclusion that the integration of domain knowledge in hybrid deep systems is a potent direction toward reliable and effective diagnoses of breast cancer in real-world mammographic screening.

title: Enhancing Industrial Assembly Processes Through the Integration of Augmented Reality Technologies
authors: Uma Mageswari T, J Raja
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The paradigm shift towards Industry 4.0 mandates innovative, human-centric interfaces to optimize manual processes, particularly in complex assembly operations. This paper presents a comprehensive, quantitative study investigating the efficacy of Augmented Reality (AR), delivered via a Head-Mounted Display (HMD), as a guidance system for industrial assembly. We first detail the architecture of an AR system providing real-time, context-aware visual instructions for a complex electromechanical task. A controlled user study ($N=30$) was conducted, comparing the AR-assisted method against conventional paper-based and static digital screen instructions across performance and human factors metrics. The experimental results demonstrate that the AR system significantly enhances operational efficiency, yielding a 34.2% reduction in mean assembly time and a 52.1% decrease in the mean error rate compared to paper instructions. Furthermore, psychological workload assessment using the NASA Task Load Index (NASA-TLX) confirmed a substantial 28.0% reduction in perceived cognitive load for operators. These findings validate AR as a critical enabling technology for achieving superior efficiency, quality, and human-system collaboration in intelligent manufacturing ecosystems.

title: Edge AI–based Malware Detection in IoT Devices using Machine Learning
authors: Sharanabasappa Tadkal, Prasad M. R., Jundale Poonam Abasaheb, Amit Raikar, Sandeep Yerrathi, Sayeli Dey
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Traditional cloud-based malware detection systems have several drawbacks in time-sensitive IoT environments such as high communication latency, excessive bandwidth consumption and privacy risks due to centralized data processing. These limitations impede the ability to provide real-time threat mitigation and scalability to handle growing amounts of IoT devices. To overcome these challenges this work proposes an edge-ai based malware detection system that deploys lightweight machine learning models on IoT gateways and edge devices that will enable local feature extraction from network traffic and device telemetry with minimal computational overhead. Model efficiency is enhanced by using techniques for knowledge distillation and compression, collaborative learning among multiple devices is achieved through a federated update mechanism without sharing raw data. The proposed framework has been experimentally evaluated on a publicly available IoT malware dataset which demonstrates the framework’s capability to achieve high detection accuracy and low inference latencies below 10ms along with significant energy consumption reduction compared to traditional cloud-based malware detection system. These results confirm that edge-based intelligent malware detection offers a scalable, privacy-preserving, and energy-efficient security solution capable of effectively protecting IoT infrastructures against emerging and evolving cyber threats.
