title: Dynamic Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Clarification-Driven Query Refinement for Context-Aware Q & A
authors: Vibhav Sharma, Ananya Singh, Prerna Choudhary, A R Revathi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as an effective approach for improving question answering by grounding generative models in external knowledge sources. However, most existing RAG pipelines rely on static, pre-indexed corpora and assume that user queries are well-formed and unambiguous, limiting their applicability in dynamic and real-world settings. Our proposed system utilizes Dynamic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (DA-RAG), a modular framework with interactive clarification to equip question answering systems for open domains. The addition of an explicit clarification stage at the beginning of the search process allows the system to identify the nuanced nature of user search queries and refine them before searching external knowledge sources. Additionally, dynamic retrieval enables access to up-to-date information through updates obtained via Wikipedia APIs. The retrieved documents are subsequently ranked through similarity searches conducted using FAISS technology and aggregated to assist evidence selection. In experiments, DA-RAG significantly outperformed both traditional RAG models and pure generation benchmarks, achieving a ROUGE-L score of 0.62 and a BERTScore of 0.43. The effectiveness of the system and its clarification stage is further demonstrated by the results, which indicate that nearly 80 % of ambiguous queries were successfully resolved.

title: Utilizing Second-Life EV Batteries to Mitigate PV-Induced Overvoltages in LV networks
authors: Evangelos Pompodakis, Arif Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) integration in low-voltage (LV) rural networks can lead to technical challenges, such as overvoltages, during peak generation periods. Several mitigation methods have been explored, including reactive power control, demand-side management, line reinforcement, and battery energy storage systems (BESS). Among them, BESS offers high technical effectiveness but remains limited by its cost and periodic replacement requirements. To overcome this barrier, second-life electric vehicle (EV) batteries offer a practical alternative, providing adequate capacity for stationary applications at a fraction of the cost of new batteries. This manuscript examines the use of second-life EV batteries to manage voltage rise in a rural LV feeder and evaluates the levelized cost of energy (LCOE) of hybrid power plants (HPPs) consisting of small PVs and retired EV batteries to quantify economic viability. The results show that second-life batteries can reduce overvoltages effectively and maintain a low LCOE, indicating that they are a viable option for increasing PV hosting capacity in LV networks.

title: FPGA Implementation of Bilateral Filter with Optimized Resources
authors: AMV Pathi, Balamuralitharan Balakrishnan, G Prasanna Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Bilateral filter is weighted average nonlinear filter. The application of Bilateral filter gives significant importance in the field of image processing. This paper mainly focuses on the FPGA implementation Bilateral filter with approximation of multiplier. The multiplier is approximated by using array product multiplier and followed by multiplexer. The Approximate Product Generator (APG) is the main important module which approximate the multiplier operation using shift registers and adders. The MUX selects the particular product based on the weight of the coefficient. This approximate multiplier utilizes the smaller number of resources in FPGA. The divider operation is also important in bilateral filter, which can be approximated by using simple shift register circuitry proposed in this paper. The proposed method is implemented in VIVADO Tools and synthesized results are compared with standard excising results. The proposed Bilateral filter architecture with approximation multiplier and divider gives the 20% reduction in power and with a little compromise of the delay.

title: Design and Implementation of Edge Computing-Aware On-Site Defect Detection in Industrial Applications through Residual Attention MobileNet SSD
authors: Gude Aruna, S. Maruthupermal
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Defect detection is essential in industrial production. However, attaining high detection accuracy typically depends on large amounts of training data, which are difficult to obtain because defects occur infrequently in industrial environments. Although generative models provide a better solution but the scarcity of defect data makes it difficult to train these models from scratch. This limitation reduces the capability to produce high-quality defect images that strictly follow specified layout constraints. Moreover, existing defect detection algorithms fail to obtain an effective balance among detection accuracy, computational cost, and inference speed due to challenges faced by complex background information, diverse defect categories, and substantial variations in defect morphology. Hence, it is important to resolve the issues presented in the prior on-site defect detection model, so a deep learning technique by considering the edge computing platforms is suggested in this research work. Initially, required images are collected from publicly available datasets. Next, the gathered images are subjected into the on-site defect detection phase. In this phase, Residual Attention MobileNet Single Shot Detector (RAMNet-SSD) is employed to execute the on-site defect detection process. Finally, the on-site defect detection outcomes are obtained from RAMNet-SSD, and their performance is identified by considering various performance measures. The distinct activation function is validated across various datasets, whereas the accuracy, dice coefficient, recall and F1-score shows the value of 96.16%, 96.15%, 96.27%, and 96.15% in surface-defect-detection-dataset at softmax activation function.

title: FEM-Based Modeling and Analysis of Demagnetization in an Outer-Rotor PMSM with FOC Control Using Co-Simulation
authors: Büşra Çelik, Tolgay Kara
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Outer rotor permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) are popular in low-speed and high-torque applications because to their high torque density and steady operation. However, high temperatures, reverse magnetic fields, or mechanical strains can cause demagnetization of permanent magnets, reducing motor performance. This study investigates the performance of an outer rotor PMSM under demagnetization faults using a co-simulation technique. The motor is created in ANSYS Maxwell using the finite element method and then combined with ANSYS Twin Builder, which creates the inverter and measurement circuits. In MATLAB/Simulink, the motor speed is controlled using the field-oriented control (FOC) method, while inverter switching signals are created using the SVPWM technique. Simulation investigations are carried out for healthy and various demagnetization levels for motor. The findings reveal that as the severity of demagnetization grows, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex>-axis current rises to compensate for the decrease in magnet flux, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d$</tex> axis current approaches zero, and torque oscillations become more pronounced. The collected results show that the suggested co-simulation framework is a viable platform for studying the performance of outer rotor PMSMs during demagnetization faults.

title: Cryptocurrency Wallet Key Management Using Secret Sharing
authors: Doğa Ece Koca, Burak Kıyak, Hakan Dalkılıç
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Cryptocurrency wallets are devices, physical mediums, programs or online services which store the keys used in cryptocurrency transactions. The loss of wallet keys can result in substantial economic damage, making secure storage crucial. Traditional approaches based on a single key have a single point of failure, therefore increasing the risk of users losing their assets. This project uses Shamir's scheme introduced by Adi Shamir in 1979. The main purpose of this algorithm is to divide a secret that needs to be encrypted into various shares. The original secret can be obtained by combining a specific number of shares (the threshold value), instead of requiring all shares to rebuild the original secret, thus making Shamir's Secret Sharing secure against an enemy with unlimited computing power. The project combines this powerful algorithm with BIP-39 Mnemonics, QR code generation from shares and the steganography (Least Significant Bit) technique which allows shares to be embedded in pictures. The MnemoShare project also possesses a security protection against web-based threats thanks to its MetaMask Snap Integration which provides the ability to split the secret without any contact to the Web API or its memory. Overall, a secure cryptocurrency key management architecture, combining a theoretical concept with a real-life application is presented by the MnemoShare system.

title: Design and Implementation of an Autonomous AI & IoT-Enabled Smart Buoy System for Real-Time Maritime Navigation and Hazard Detection
authors: A Poul Louis, H Akash, M Dhanush, G Durgadevi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Maritime Okay, so even with all these surveillance systems along the coast, safety and navigation are still big concerns. Especially in those remote coastal and offshore areas where one cannot keep an eye on everything all the time. This article highlights a smart buoy system that uses AI and IoT technology to help with navigation and warn people of any dangers in real-time. The system uses a Raspberry Pi with a 5-inch touch screen to facilitate user interaction. It also uses an Arduino Nano-based controller to effectively control hardware components. The GPS module is the most critical component in identifying the exact location. LoRa is used for communication, which is very effective over long distances and is also very reliable. It is solar-powered, meaning it can run 24/7 without having to change the battery. The buoy also has danger alerting systems such as an LCD display, buzzer, and SMS notification to warn people of any dangers. It is intelligent enough to locate itself and its surroundings. It can detect events like unusual movement, collisions, or other hazards and send real-time notifications to remote control centers. The system has been tested and proved to be able to track GPS positions correctly, communicate effectively, and send hazard notifications on time. This means that it really makes a difference in maritime safety. The device is small, with low power consumption, and is priced low enough to be reused for other purposes like ship routing, accident prevention, coastal monitoring, and border protection. The smart buoy system is an example of how AI and IoT can revolutionize maritime safety. It operates continuously through solar power, has LoRa for long-range communication, and provides real-time notifications to ensure continuous monitoring of the waters, even in remote locations. It can also be expanded and repurposed for other uses such as coastal monitoring, border protection, and navigation assistance. Makes as wonder how many accidents could be avoided if more places had something like this. The user interface based on Raspberry Pi and the control system based on Arduino Nano guarantee that the system is computationally efficient and dependable.

title: Do Costs, Energy Prices, or Peer Adoption Matter More? Insights into Renewable Implementation Decisions for SMEs
authors: Esma Sahin, Liang Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) play a critical role in shaping energy consumption patterns and advancing sustainability goals, yet their adoption of renewable energy technologies has consistently lagged behind expectations. Most existing research is limited and does not systematically compare the roles of economic factors, energy pricing, and peer influences using actual auditing data. This paper fills that gap by analyzing recommendation-level data from the U.S. Department of Energy's Industrial Training and Assessment Center (ITAC) program. The analysis uses three empirical models to assess how implementation decisions are influenced by cost, savings projections, simple payback periods, energy prices, and peer-related effects at the site-specific individual assessment level. The results show that conventional economic variables, like total costs and savings, do not add explanatory power when considered alone. Instead, it has emerged that a simple payback period in the range of 2 to 3 years is a defining threshold, significantly increasing the likelihood of renewables implementation. Energy unit costs-electricity prices, demand charges, and natural gas prices-find weak and inconsistent relationships with adoption decisions, implying price signals alone do not strongly motivate adoption. However, the study shows a strong peer context effect: adoption of renewable energy measures significantly relates to the overall implementation rate of the other recommendations in that assessment. This finding points to meaningful social and informational spillovers across recommendation portfolios. All the results suggest that SME decisions to adopt or reject renewable energy are less from isolated economic calculations and more from organizational motivation, implementation culture, and past experience in executing recommendations. This underscores the need for policy approaches that transcend technology-specific incentives to demonstrate their impact; rather, they should emphasize portfolio-based strategies, phased programs, and mechanisms that build the overall capacity and willingness of firms to implement changes.

title: 3rd Eye: Transport Companion for the Visually Impaired
authors: A. Velayudham, Hari Narayanan K, Hari Hara Sudhan G U, Arun R S, Janani Priya R
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Mobility is essential for independent living and active participation in society, enabling access to education, employment, healthcare, and social engagement. However, visually impaired individuals face significant challenges in independent travel due to environmental barriers and limited accessibility support. Although modern transportation systems incorporate digital technologies such as GPS navigation, mobile applications, automated announcements, and real time updates, most of these systems rely heavily on visual interfaces. Critical travel information including routes, boarding points, and destination alerts is typically displayed on screens, making it difficult for visually impaired users to access. This creates a substantial digital accessibility gap in public transportation systems. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes 3rd EYE, an IoT based intelligent transport companion designed to assist visually impaired individuals throughout their journey. The system integrates real-time navigation, voice interaction, and smart alert mechanisms to enhance safety, accessibility, and independence in public transportation environments.

title: Deep Learning-Based Rainfall-Runoff Modeling for River Basin Water Resource Prediction
authors: Jia Hu
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Efficient management and precise prediction of water resources in river basins are the core challenges in responding to extreme weather and climate change. Traditional physical hydrological models are limited by high computational costs and complex parameter calibration. In recent years, deep learning (such as LSTM, Transformer) has demonstrated strong nonlinear mapping capabilities, but purely data-driven models face the “black box” dilemma, having poor generalization ability in extreme flood events, and their predictions often violate physical conservation laws. In response to the demand for intelligent disaster prevention and early warning, this study proposes a Transformer prediction model that integrates physical priors and multiscale decomposition (PHY-MS-Former). This model introduces variational mode decomposition (VMD) to extract multi-scale features of runoff, uses self-attention to capture spatio-temporal dependencies, and enforces physical constraints such as water balance and monotonicity in the loss function. Rigorous simulation comparison experiments show that PHY-MS-Former significantly outperforms traditional models and standard deep networks in general runoff and extreme flood generalization predictions. Moreover, combined with SHAP explainability analysis, it reveals the nonlinear driving mechanism of rainfall intensity and previous soil moisture on peak flood. This study provides a new path for constructing a hydrological control system with high accuracy and physical consistency.

title: Downscaling of Satellite based Air Quality Maps using AI ML
authors: Nivaedithaa S, Nivetha V, S. Prince Mary
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The problem of air pollution in the city leads to serious health and environmental issues. The capability to keep close track of it has however, limited capabilities on the capability of ground sensors and the accuracy of satellite data. The satellite provides considerable information about such pollutants as the PM2.5 and the NO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>. Yet, their level of spatial resolution is poor (101 km) and hence will not be as helpful when local policy and population health are in need. This study introduces a structure providing artificial intelligence and machine learning to reduce massive sizes of satellites and generate air quality maps with a resolution greater than a kilometer. The system makes use of satellite data and other data including weather and traffic and land use data. It is based on the integrated model that involves the use of Random Forest, CNN-LSTM, or Graph Neural Networks. The framework will produce precise, context-dependent, and real-time pollution maps by recording the change of things as time and space change. The innovation is based on its capability to unite the data of a number of sources, its ability to compute data and its well-structured and sturdy design. This permits easily scalable and economical implementation in regions where ground-based sensors are few. These are the detailed maps, which serve to draw significant areas, develop the urban planning, and offer the grounding of the environmental health regulation based on data.

title: Phasor Measurement Unit-Based Synchronization of Photovoltaic and Grid System with Control and Remote Cloud Monitoring
authors: Ahmed S. Rahi, Hassan Jassim Motlak, Riyadh Toman Thahab
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Due to the increasing demand for renewable energy sources and the challenges of ensuring the stability of power plants during grid disruptions, this study presents a phase-unit measurement (PMU)-based synchronization method for grid-feeding photovoltaic power plant systems, used for dq frame control and cloud monitoring. The proposed strategy employs real-time phase-angle measurement to improve synchronization accuracy compared with conventional phaselocked loop (PLL) methods. Simulation results demonstrated improved dynamic performance, including faster response times, reduced steady-state error, and enhanced stability during sudden nonlinear load changes. The system also enables real-time monitoring via a cloud platform, leading to improved operational reliability and grid disturbance detection capabilities.

title: Functional Stability of Cyber Technological Processes Using Controllability of Discrete Systems
authors: Oleksandr Laptiev, Volodymyr Pichkur, Andrii Sobchuk, Serhii Laptiev, Serhii Holdobin, Yuriy Grytsuk
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The organization of production processes in manufacturing enterprises necessitates maintaining the functional stability of technological cycles under persistent external and internal destabilizing influences. Particular interest arises in technological processes where, at different stages of production, the number of parameters requiring control varies. Consequently, the mathematical formalization of such processes leads to discrete systems with variable statevector dimensions. This study addresses the development of a mathematical framework capable of adequately describing models of such processes, taking into account the specific requirements for controlling distinct sets of technological parameters at various production stages. Special attention is devoted to the controllability problem for discrete systems with variable state-vector dimensions. An analysis of a linear discrete system with dimensionvarying state vectors is performed. Necessary and sufficient conditions for controllability are derived, and a control strategy is constructed that ensures successful transfer of the system to an arbitrary final state. These results provide a basis for ensuring the functional stability of technological processes modeled by discrete linear systems with variable state-vector dimensions. The structural conditions for functional stability obtained herein make it possible to maintain continuous production of product batches while accounting for potential external and internal destabilizing factors.

title: Fuzzy Logic-Based MPPT Control Strategy for Stable EV Charging under Rapid Solar Irradiance Variations
authors: S V Naga Lakshmi, Gaurav Londhe, R.Tamilamuthan, Suganthi.S, M.Lakshmanan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: There is a fairly challenging task in the performance of electric vehicle (EV) charging systems with photovoltaic (PV) that are subjected to high-speed variations of solar irradiance because of temporary shading-caused cloud movements. Traditional Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) algorithms Perturb and Observe (P&O) and Incremental Conductance (INC), which try to track the extreme power point, are generally slow in their tracking and tend to oscillate at steady rates under harsh environmental conditions. This, thus, renders the charging unstable. The paper proposes an MPPT control strategy using rule-based fuzzy logic to enhance tracking capabilities that can provide stable vehicle charging performance under fluctuating irradiance. It utilizes the change in PV power (ΔP) and also the change in voltage (ΔV) as input variables, while it adjusts duty-cycle on a DC–DC converter through the predefined fuzzy inference rule set. The method does not require a mathematical PV model since it collaborates with switching time or duty-cycle design for different light conditions. The control strategy was reviewed in MATLAB under fast shadowing circumstances. The simulation findings indicate the improvement in controller convergence time, lower percentage in steady-state oscillations, with MPPT-tracking efficiency higher than 96% against traditional MPPT approaches. In addition, smoother duty-cycle regulation delivers stable tracking of SOC of the EV battery with changing irradiance conditions. The fuzzy-logic-based approach leads to a practically efficient way to cater to the needs for reliable solar-powered EV-charging applications.

title: Adaptive Feature Weighting in Aggregated Long Short-Term Memory for Air Quality Prediction
authors: J. Kavitha, M. Durga Rohith Kumar, Seelamneni.Sridevi, S. P. Santhoshkumar, R. M. Dilip Charaan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accurate short and medium-term air quality forecasting is essential for public health and urban planning. We introduce an Adaptive Feature Weighting in Aggregated Deep Learning Framework (AF-ALSTM) that combines multiple LSTM sub-networks, linear aggregation and attention-based feature input to model multi-scale spatio¬temporal dependencies in air pollutant time series. The model is preprocessed with multi-feature scaling and trained on station-level historical data. We report improved prediction accuracy over baseline LSTM and BiLSTM models across multiple pollutants on an Indian station dataset. We also benchmark the approach against recent deep architectures reported in the literature. Additional mathematical formulations and multi-objective loss functions are presented to stabilize training and emphasize robust error metrics. Experimental results indicate that the proposed AF-ALSTM reduces RMSE for PM2.5 and PM10 and achieves better overall R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> compared to conventional recurrent networks.

title: Understanding Farmer Adoption of Artificial Intelligence in Agriculture: A Systematic Review and Socio-Technical Model Grounded in Midwest Farming Systems
authors: Abeer F. Alkhwaldi, Cherie Noteboom, Amir A. Abdulmuhsin
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming agricultural systems, but its implementation is still scattered at the farm scale, especially in the U.S. Midwest. This paper undertakes a “systematic literature review” (SLR) of 23 empirical studies published since 2020 to determine the main socio-technical factors affecting the introduction of AI and AI-enabled technologies among farmers. Incorporating the insights of the Unified “Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology” (UTAUT), “Technology-Organization-Environment” (TOE), and “Socio-Technical Systems” (STS) frameworks, the review demonstrates that the factors that influence adoption are interdependent and include technological (performance expectancy, effort expectancy, task technology fit), social (trust in technology, social influence) and contextual (facilitating conditions, data privacy concern) factors. Results are compiled into the “Socio-Technical Structural Equation Model” (STS-SEM) that is used to explain the interaction of these determinants to impact the intentions of AI adoption among Midwestern farmers. The new model proposes mediating and moderating processes, namely the roles of trust, privacy, and facilitating conditions that have been under-researched in previous studies. The work has a theoretical contribution, which is that it builds on a multilevel socio-technical conceptualization of digital agriculture and a practical contribution, which is the description of policy and design implications of encouraging trustful, inclusive, and region-specific AI implementation in U.S. agriculture.

title: Voltage Control Modeling in Membrane Capacitive Deionization (MCDI) for Desalination Applications
authors: Bernard Raphael Altamira, Mark Joseph Javier, Lucky Angelico Nagpala, Rowena Carpio, Jenna Riscy Manio, Patricia Nadine Revilla, Marx Lenin Laycano
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This study presents a simulation-based adaptive voltage control system for membrane capacitive deionization (MCDI) to improve desalination efficiency, reduce energy consumption, and extend electrode lifespan. Using COMSOL Multiphysics and MATLAB Simulink, the system dynamically adjusted voltage in response to ion concentration and was compared with constant-voltage operation (0.1-1.0 V). Results showed that while high constant voltages enabled rapid ion removal at higher energy and component cost, the adaptive approach applied high voltage only when needed and reduced it as concentration declined. This strategy achieved performance comparable to 1.0 V operation while improving energy efficiency and reducing electrode stress, highlighting the potential of intelligent voltage control to enhance the sustainability and scalability of MCDI desalination systems.

title: Non-Invasive Biosensor-Integrated Artificial Pancreas System for Smart Management of Diabetes Mellitus
authors: Abinayavalli Chellapandian, Nethra Bharathwaj, Tejasvin Kansal, Karishma Prasad, S.Vasanthadev Suryakala, T.Rajalakshmi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus requires continuous monitoring and timely insulin adjustment to maintain safe blood glucose levels. Most current glucose monitoring and insulin delivery systems mainly depend on glucose values and often do not consider physiological factors such as physical activity, heart rate, and body temperature, even though these can strongly affect insulin needs. This paper presents a low-cost, multi-sensor, rule-based prototype for insulin decision support that uses simulated glucose input together with real-time physiological signals. The proposed system combines glucose, heart rate, temperature, and motion data using a simple rule-based algorithm implemented on an ESP32 microcontroller. A stepper motor is used to simulate insulin delivery, and an OLED(Organic Light-Emitting Diode) display provides real-time feedback to the user. The system was tested under various simulated physiological conditions and was compared with a glucose-only baseline approach. The results show that including additional physiological parameters reduces large fluctuations in the estimated insulin output and improves the response time of the system by about 15% compared to the glucose-only case. The proposed system is not intended to replace clinically approved continuous glucose monitoring or closed-loop insulin delivery systems. Instead, it serves as a proof-of-concept to demonstrate that a low-complexity, multi-parameter, rule-based approach can improve insulin estimation in low-cost and embedded diabetes management applications.

title: PPO-AquaRoute: Latency-Aware Opportunistic Routing in Underwater Acoustic Sensing Networks
authors: Ashok Battula, CH Jayaprakash, Dustakar Surendra Rao, Anil Kumar Koona, Voruchu Sai Babu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Underwater acoustic sensing networks enable long term ocean observation, but operate under severe bandwidth limits, long propagation delay and time varying channels. Reliable and timely data delivery is therefore critical for latency sensitive underwater applications. Many existing routing protocols do not jointly optimize latency, energy consumption and congestion, leading to unstable delay and faster node depletion as traffic increases. Heuristic depth based and vector based schemes such as DBR, VBF and EEDBR improve robustness through redundancy or energy awareness, yet still incur high control overhead and large end to end delay in dense deployments. To overcome these limitations, PPO-AquaRoute is introduced as a latency aware opportunistic routing framework that employs proximal policy optimization to learn forwarding policies balancing packet delivery, delay and energy in a principled manner. The approach couples a latency sensitive reward with an opportunistic candidate relay set so that forwarding decisions prefer low delay, energy efficient multi hop paths while avoiding redundant transmissions. Evaluation is carried out through simulations on 3D underwater topologies with 50–200 sensor nodes, realistic acoustic channel models and traffic loads from 0.2 to 0.8 packets/s, using metrics such as packet delivery ratio, end to end delay, jitter, throughput, control overhead, energy per bit and network lifetime. PPO-AquaRoute attains 94.9% delivery with 1.2 s delay and 23.8 kbps throughput in a 100 node network, while reducing overhead to 24.7% and energy consumption to 0.72 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> J/bit, which extends lifetime to 8940 rounds versus 6430 for DBR. In a 200 node topology the protocol still delivers 91.4% of packets with 1.52 s delay, compared with 74.8% and 2.79 s for DBR, and ablation studies show clear degradation when latency awareness or opportunistic candidate selection is removed. These results indicate that deep reinforcement learning with latency aware rewards provides an effective basis for scalable, energy efficient and time sensitive routing in underwater acoustic sensing networks.

title:
Critical Transit Infrastructure in Smart Cities and Urban Air Quality: A Multi-City Seasonal Comparison of Ridership and PM
                    <sub>2.5</sub>
authors: Sean Elliott, Sohini Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Public transit is a critical component of urban mobility and equity, yet mobility-air-quality linkages are rarely operationalized in reproducible smart-city analytics workflows. This study develops a transparent, multi-source monitoring dataset that integrates agency-reported transit ridership with ambient fine particulate matter <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{P} \boldsymbol{M}_{2.5}$</tex> from the U.S. EPA Air Quality System (AQS) for four U.S. metropolitan areas-New York City, Chicago, Las Vegas, and Phoenix, using two seasonal snapshots (March and October 2024). We harmonize heterogeneous ridership feeds (daily and stop-level) to monthly system totals and pair them with monthly mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$P M_{2.5}$</tex>, reporting both absolute and per-capita metrics to enable crosscity comparability. Results show pronounced structural differences in transit scale and intensity, with consistent seasonal shifts in both ridership and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{P} \boldsymbol{M}_{2.5}$</tex> that vary by urban context. A set of lightweight regression specifications is used as a descriptive sensitivity analysis, indicating that apparent mobility- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{P} \boldsymbol{M}_{2.5}$</tex> relationships are not uniform across cities or seasons and are strongly shaped by baseline city effects. Overall, the paper positions integrated mobility and environment monitoring as a practical smart-city capability, offering a scalable framework for tracking infrastructure utilization alongside exposure-relevant air-quality indicators to support sustainable communities and public-health-aware urban resilience.

title: An Integrated Machine Learning Pipeline for the Hospitality Industry: Customer Segmentation, Churn Prediction, and Hybrid Revenue Forecasting
authors: Ali Kerem Güler, Osman Saraç, Mehmet Göktürk
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This study presents an end-to-end machine learning pipeline that integrates customer segmentation, churn prediction, and revenue forecasting to support data-driven decision-making in hotel revenue management. The proposed framework was validated using approximately 1,060 days of operational data, encompassing 26,729 guest profiles from a 485 -room resort hotel in Turkey. K-Means clustering identified five distinct and interpretable guest segments, revealing that family travelers generate the highest average expenditure while high-frequency VIP guests constitute the most loyal cohort. A Random Forestbased churn prediction model achieved an ROC-AUC of 0.8702 and a weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{1}$</tex>-score of 0.9148 under stratified 5-fold crossvalidation, demonstrating robust discriminative power despite an 88.65 % class imbalance. Feature importance analysis confirmed that directly observable behavioral metrics-total stays, guest count, and average revenue per stay-serve as the strongest predictors of churn. For revenue forecasting, a comparative evaluation of SARIMA, XGBoost, LightGBM, and LSTM models revealed that LightGBM achieved the best performance with a MAPE of 6.14 %, while a performance-based hybrid ensemble maintained a MAPE of 7.78 % as a scalable production solution. The integrated pipeline was deployed as a FastAPIbased service, enabling real-time segment assignment and churn probability computation within the hotel's Property Management System. Scenario-based revenue forecasts further support proactive staffing, procurement, and dynamic pricing decisions, effectively transforming raw operational data into actionable business intelligence for the hospitality industry.

title: Shilpa: Inclusive Mobile Learning App for Visual, Auditory, Physically, and Cognitive Impaired Students
authors: Gimashi Ishara, Chamindu Wijesooriya, Ravindu Perera, Sarindu Mathota, Nuwan Kodagoda, Jenny Krishara
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Shilpa is presented as an inclusive mobile learning application designed to support primary education for students with disabilities within a unified platform. Existing educational technologies present limitations, as accessibility features are often implemented in isolation, resulting in fragmented learning experiences. An integrated system was developed to support visual, hearing, physical, and cognitive impairments within a single application environment. Shilpa was developed using mobile-based architecture with integrated accessibility modules, including Braille-based content interaction, sign language recognition, multimodal interaction mechanisms, and adaptive learning support. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model was used for Braille recognition, while a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) model was implemented for sign language recognition using hand landmark sequences. Shilpa was evaluated through model performance analysis and user-based testing. Experimental results demonstrated high accuracy in Braille recognition and sign language classification, with effective generalization and minimal overfitting. User evaluations demonstrated improved accessibility, interaction efficiency, and learning engagement across different impairment groups. The findings confirm that integrating multiple accessibility features within a single platform enhances usability and supports inclusive learning. Shilpa contributes to reducing accessibility barriers and promoting independent learning in a Sinhala-medium educational context.

title: A Smart Multi-Sensor Blind-Spot Monitoring System with Predictive Warning for Vehicles
authors: Sai Ananya R, Rithika G, Suresh Maruthai
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Rear-end collisions and blind-spot accidents are great contributors to road traffic accidents and thus there is a need to ensure that vehicles have low cost, real time, and reliable vehicle safety systems. This paper proposes a smart blind-spot and crash system that combines ultrasonic and infrared radar, IMU and LDR sensors, and the rule-based decision making and prognostic forecast. It does not operate in the same way as traditional systems but groups the driving scenarios into Safe, Warning, or Danger cases and predicts possible crash cases by observing the constant decrease in distance. The rain sensor automatically modifies safe distance settings in wet weather, whereas the LDR is optimized to perform well in day and night. It has alerts in the form of a buzzer and a LCD display which is optional, to alert the driver. The system is highly reliable and explainable as it does not rely on heavy processing, internet connections, and machine learning. Its results in experimental tests prove its ability to decrease the number of false alarms, deliver early warnings, and increase the confidence of drivers, providing a viable approach to the safety improvement of automobiles.

title: A Real-Time Hospital Resource Monitoring System for Emergency Healthcare Services
authors: G. Lokesh, A. Nagavinod, G. Vijay Kumar Yadav, D. Suresh, K. Charan, A. Sridhar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The emergency departments are still experiencing challenges in terms of operational matters arising from overcrowding and the lack of information on the availability of hospital beds and blood resources. In emergency situations, any delay in the identification of suitable hospitals and resource allocation may have a negative impact on the health of patients. This paper introduces a real-time intelligent system for emergency response and hospital bed and blood resource management. The system consists of an emergency severity identification module based on deep learning models that process triage inputs and Based on the severity level, the system assists in allocating priority to hospital bed and blood resource allocation using a centralized dashboard. In addition, an AI-based chatbot and voice assistant is incorporated to facilitate faster engagement and basic emergency services. Simulation-based experimental analysis on emergency data sets shows that the proposed system outperforms existing machine learning-based triage systems in terms of classification accuracy. System-level analysis also shows faster response time and enhanced efficiency in hospital selection during emergencies.

title:
Wafer-Scale Fabrication of 5.1-GHz Heteroepitaxial Al
                    <sub>0.8</sub>
                    Sc
                    <sub>0.2</sub>
                    N Film Bulk Acoustic Resonator With High Figure-of-Merit
authors: Wentong Dou, Xuanqi Huang, Ruidong Qin, Chen Li, Chongyang Huo, Zhiqiang Mu, Wenjie Yu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work reports the growth of high-crystallinity Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.8</sub>Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.2</sub>N piezoelectric film on an 8-inch wafer, exhibiting a full-width at half-maximum (FWHM) of 0.32° in the (002) X-ray diffraction (XRD) rocking curve. Leveraging a cavity-embedded wafer bonding and device fabrication process, Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.8</sub>Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.2</sub>N film bulk acoustic wave resonators (FBARs) were successfully realized with a resonant frequency at 5.149 GHz. The hero device exhibits a raw-measured maximum quality factor (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q_{max}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 1387, an electromechanical coupling coefficient (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K_{eff}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 15.97%, and a figure of merit (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\textit {FOM} =Q_{max}\cdot K_{eff}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 222. These results highlight the critical potential of heteroepitaxial AlScN BAW technology for next-generation 5G/6G and Wi-Fi communication applications.

title: Synthesis of a Miniature Wide-Band-Stop Filter Using a Hybrid BAW-Assisted Topology
authors: Santi Cano, Eloi Guerrero, Alfred Giménez, Pedro de Paco
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents the complete design procedure of a broadband band-stop filter using a Bridge-T (BT) topology that combines solidly mounted bulk acoustic wave (BAW) resonators with reactive elements to achieve moderate stopband attenuation, low insertion, and a small footprint. The BT topologies enable the transmission zeros (TZs) associated with each resonator to be decoupled from the fractional bandwidth (FBW), thereby overcoming a key limitation of BAW ladder filters, in which a wide FBW requires high electromechanical coupling factors (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k_{t}^{2}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). To achieve full design control over the proposed band-stop filter, a coupling-matrix (CM)-based methodology is first introduced to extract the circuit elements. Then, a fabricated band-stop prototype, whose rejection region ranges from 2.95 to 4.45GHz, is presented and compared with the synthesized and simulated models, showing very good agreement. The fabricated BAW-assisted band-stop filter employs Sc-doped AlN BAW resonators and has a footprint of 2 x 3mm. The measured band-stop filter achieves a 41.4% FBW rejection region, providing an attenuation between 15–27dB.

title: Outlier-Robust Autocovariance Least-Squares Estimation via Iteratively Reweighted Least Squares
authors: Jiahong Li, Fang Deng
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Accurate process and measurement noise covariances are indispensable for Kalman filtering, yet they are difficult to identify when the innovation used for calibration is intermittently corrupted by sensor outliers. Conventional autocovariance least-squares (ALS) estimates the noise covariances by matching empirical and theoretical innovation autocovariances, but its quadratic criterion can convert a few impulsive autocovariance entries into large covariance bias. This paper develops an outlier robust ALS estimator, ALS-IRLS, for covariance identification under such contamination. ALS-IRLS casts ALS as a structured Huber M-estimation problem on the stacked multi-lag autoco variance equations and solves the induced weighted least-squares subproblems by iteratively reweighted least squares (IRLS) within the Riccati/gain fixed-point loop. This design preserves the information that separates process and measurement noise while bounding the influence of contaminated equations. Bounded influence, threshold-sensitivity, local-convergence, admissibility, and amortized-complexity analyses are provided. Simulations under 15% contamination show that ALS-IRLS reduces covariance estimation errors by over two orders of magnitude relative to ALS and yields downstream filtering accuracy close to the oracle Kalman filter, including against adaptive robust-filter baselines.

title: A Heterogeneous Crowd Agents Collaborative Scheduling Method for Complex Spatiotemporal Environments_supp1-3696758.pdf
authors: Yimeng Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: The emergence of the concept of heterogeneous crowd sensing and the development of multi-agent reinforcement learning technology have brought new ideas for achieving low-cost, high-coverage, and real-time target tracking in complex spatiotemporal environments. In order to deal with the challenges faced by collaborative scheduling of heterogeneous crowd agents in complex environments, such as the lack of correlation between the crowd agents and the environment, and the complexity of the crowd agent collaboration modes caused by heterogeneous functional attributes, we propose a collaborative scheduling framework for cooperative competitive games based on hybrid Markov games. Firstly, we construct a correlation modeling framework for complex spatiotemporal regional environments and heterogeneous crowd agents. Secondly, we design a multi-level Markov collaborative scheduling algorithm assisted by environmental information enhancement. Crowd sensing information enhances the information transparency in incomplete information games, and the alliance formation and dissolution mechanism helps to give full play to the functional characteristics of heterogeneous crowd agents. In addition, we design a reward function containing multi-dimensional reward information. After comprehensive evaluation, the proposed method achieves higher cumulative reward, faster loss convergence, and shorter tracking time compared with baseline methods. The correctness and effectiveness of the model are verified through heterogeneous proportion analysis of crowd agents and comparative analysis of core module ablation. Experimental results show that the model can reduce tracking time and improve resource utilization efficiency in complex environments.

title: Cyber-Resilient Green AI: Intelligent Scheduling Under Energy and Security Constraints
authors: Arth Bhardwaj, Pragya Siddhi, Arun Kumar Takuli, Birendra Kumar Saraswat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: With the emerging development of artificial intelligence (AI) workloads and massive cloud systems, the electricity resources of data centers have increased exponentially, harming the issues of sustainability and performance in terms of environmental friendliness. Meanwhile, contemporary AI-enabled computing systems are getting more vulnerable to cybersecurity offenses, threats, which have the potential to interfere with the combat of scheduling systems, affect system resource allocation, and decrease the credibility of a system. Although the current studies have investigated energy-conscious workload scheduling and secure cloud computing separately, few studies have been considered in terms of combining energy efficiency and cyber resilience in managing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> infrastructure. The current paper is a proposal of Cyber-Resilient Green AI conceptual framework that is intended to aid in intelligent scheduling of workload under energy and security limitations. The proposed paper combines energy tracking, security risk assessment, and intelligent scheduling schemes to assign AI tasks effectively to the existing computing resources. The framework will aim to reducing the use of power, resource use, and possible risks to security so as to prevent system operation as insecure and unreliable. The paper also evaluates what research has already been done in the areas of green AI, energy-aware scheduling, and secure cloud computing with the aim of determining its current limitations and future research opportunities. The suggested framework argues about the necessity of integrating sustainability and cybersecurity into AI-powered infrastructures and suggests knowledge about the ability to work on the creation of resilient and energy-efficient schedules of future cloud and AI computing environments.

title: Research on NIHSS Score Prediction for Stroke Patients Based on Sentiment Mining from Chief Complaints and Multimodal Fusion
authors: Jia Ning
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Rapid assessment of NIHSS scores for stroke patients in emergency settings faces challenges. This study extracts sentiment features from chief complaints to construct a multimodal model for predicting NIHSS grading. We collected data from 1850 patients and built a three-stage cascaded model: a RoBERTa multi-task learning model to extract 9-dimensional sentiment features, a BiLSTM-CRF model to extract 20-dimensional symptom features, and an XGBoost model to fuse sentiment, symptom, and medical history features for three-class NIHSS prediction. After incorporating sentiment features, the weighted accuracy improved from 0.612 to 0.658, and the macro-average AUC increased from 0.731 to 0.782. Experiments verified that the performance of multimodal fusion significantly outperforms single-modality approaches, and sentiment features from chief complaints provide incremental predictive value, offering decision support for emergency triage.

title: FSDR-SMOTE Driven Class Imbalance Mitigation for Robust CNN-Based Rice Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Vishan Kumar Gupta, Mukesh Kumar Singh, Kireet Joshi, Shivani Agarwal, Paras Jain, Sarvesh Vishwakarma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The accurate detection of leaf diseases in rice using deep learning is usually affected by a significant level of class imbalance that inherently exists in agricultural datasets, thus contributing to imbalanced learning and a lack of generalization. To overcome the shortcoming of this technique, a novel classification framework that combines Feature-Space Density-Ratio Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (FSDR-SMOTE) and sophisticated techniques of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) has been proposed for efficient recognition of rice diseases. This technique allows the adaptive over-sampling of the minority class in the deep space of feature points, based on the estimation of local density ratios, thus maintaining the natural attributes of the disease and minimizing noises and over-fitting. Varieties of CNN designs are employed for balanced feature points to classify the five types of leaf diseases in rice, which include bacterial blight, brown spot disease, leaf blast, sheath blight, and normal leaves. The efficacy of the proposed FSDR-SMOTE-CNN technique has been verified by conducting simulations upon a genuine agricultural dataset of rice leaf images, and comparative analysis indicates that the technique outperforms traditional over-sampling approaches and traditional CNN designs in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-measure, especially for the minority class of rice leaf disease.

title: Improved Lane Detection Algorithm Based on Ultra Fast Deep Lane Detection Version 2
authors: Qiang Li, Wankun Wang, Boxuan Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Lane detection is a core task for environmental perception in autonomous driving. To address the limitations of the Ultra Fast Deep Lane Detection Version 2 (UFLDV2) baseline model in road scenarios—such as poor local curvature adaptability, occlusion-induced topological disconnection, and training oscillations triggered by distant hard samples—this paper systematically optimizes it by integrating multi-dimensional spatial feature enhancement and temporal optimization mechanisms. Specifically, the architecture introduces deformable convolution and strip pooling modules, enabling the model to adaptively fit irregular physical lane edges while effectively endowing the network with long-range context aggregation capabilities. Furthermore, an exponential moving average strategy is employed to smooth historical parameters, significantly enhancing the detection robustness for distant features. Extensive benchmark experiments on the TuSimple dataset demonstrate that the improved method achieves an accuracy of 96.12% and an F1 score of 96.77%, substantially reducing both false positive and false negative rates . These improvements overcome baseline limitations, comprehensively enhancing detection performance.

title: GS-DetLite: Efficient Geometry-Guided Lightweight Detection for Autonomous Driving
authors: Zhehao Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Real-time autonomous driving requires high-accuracy detection under strict computational constraints. While YOLO-based detectors are efficient, they often lack geometric awareness and long-range directional modeling. We propose GS-DetLite, a lightweight framework based on YOLOv11. It integrates Geometry Self-Attention (GSA), which leverages monocular depth priors for spatial structure modeling, and StripConv for capturing directional features of elongated objects. Experiments on KITTI and BDD100K demonstrate that GS-DetLite consistently outperforms YOLOv11 in accuracy while maintaining real-time speeds, offering an optimized balance between efficiency and precision for edge deployment.

title: Detection of Attacked Nodes in Computer Network Using Machine Learning
authors: Gaurav Joshi, Vivek Kumar, Neeti Taneja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are essential tools of detecting malicious traffic within the computer networks. Advanced IDS use machine learning to prevent complex attacks to both conventional networks and IoT nodes. This paper suggests a hybrid solution that will implement Bi-Directional LSTM (BiLSTM), a Sparse Autoencoder, and a Random Forest classifier to identify attacked nodes. The popular CICIDS2017 dataset is utilized.[1] that consists of benign and multiple attack classes) and a generated dataset of an IoT traffic. The autoencoder is sparsely trained to learn compact features, the BiLSTM is used to learn the time dependence of the data and the Random Forest is used to do the last classification. Our model is indeed extremely high in terms of detection performance: at test data, the accuracy rate is 99-99.7 percent and the loss concentration ranges between 0.021 and0.019. The ROC AUC is close to 1.0 which means it has good separability. The obtained results are above the majority of existing models of the IDS. [2], [3] Training curve (loss and accuracy vs. epoch) figures are provided, and it is shown that convergence and stabilization are fast. We have offered the design of this hybrid model and its implementation, empirical validation on standard and custom data, and the open sourcing of the methodology.

title: Research on Detection of Chinese Phoneme Pronunciation Errors and Interpretability Feedback Teaching Based on Self-Supervised Pretrained Models
authors: He Wei, Lihua Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of automatic diagnosis and interpretability feedback of phonemelevel pronunciation errors of Chinese as a second language learners, this paper proposes a hierarchical analysis method that integrates the structured knowledge of Chinese phonology and Self-Supervised pretrained models. By taking linguistic categories such as the place and method of initial consonants, the tongue position and lip shape of finals, and the contour and range of tones as explicit learning objectives of the model, this method guides the Self-Supervised speech encoder to extract more discriminative features, and then realizes finegrained error classification and attribution, and generates visual pronunciation physiological guidance and personalized training paths accordingly. Experiments on a pronunciation diagnosis dataset containing 320 learners show that the proposed method achieves an F1 score of 85.4% in phonemelevel error detection, a 6.8 percentage point increase compared to the optimal baseline model, with an accuracy of 84.2% in detecting errors in the place of initial consonants and an accuracy of 94.3% in tone recognition. A fourweek controlled teaching experiment further verifies that learners using this interpretable feedback system have a 12.5 percentage point higher increase in the pronunciation accuracy of target phonemes than the control group that only uses binary feedback. This study confirms that deeply integrating domain knowledge is an effective way to improve the performance and teaching utility of pronunciation evaluation models, providing a new solution for intelligent computerassisted pronunciation training and medical speech rehabilitation intervention.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)

title: Preface :LAAI 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: It is with great enthusiasm that we present the proceedings of the 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI 2025). As low-altitude airspace emerges as a critical frontier for urban mobility, logistics innovation, and smart city development, the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) has become indispensable to addressing its unique challenges-from dynamic airspace management and real-time traffic optimization to safety assurance and regulatory compliance. LAAI 2025 marks a pivotal platform where researchers, engineers, policymakers, and industry leaders converge to explore how AI can unlock the full potential of low-altitude airspace while ensuring efficiency, security, and sustainability.

title: A Secure Decentralized Time Updatable Policy-Based Chameleon Hash for Redactable Blockchain in IoT
authors: Qifu Zhang, Yewei Yin, Mengyuan Fan, Dailin Li, Haoyu Wang, Tianqi Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Blockchain is widely adopted for its decentralized and immutable features, yet this immutability impedes its development—for instance, illicit or erroneous sensor data is permanently stored in the Internet of Things (IoT). Redactable blockchain can endow blockchain systems with the ability to rewrite on-chain content, addressing the aforementioned issues of illicit data storage and regulatory compliance challenges. Recently, Huang et al. proposed a time-updatable policy-based chameleon hash (TPCH) scheme, which leverages the time-updatable chameleon hash (TUCH) primitive to achieve fine-grained redaction authorization, publicly verifiable accountability and identity traceability. However, a rigorous analysis of this scheme reveals a security vulnerability in its underlying TUCH primitive: an attacker can forge valid time-update proofs through a single legitimate redaction record, thus undermining the accountability of the scheme. In addition, the original scheme adopts a centralized architecture, which not only faces the risk of single point of failure but also cannot be deployed in distributed IoT networks. To address these flaws, we first design a security-enhanced TUCH (STUCH) construction, and integrate it with multi-authority attribute-based encryption (MA-ABE) technology to propose a decentralized TPCH scheme (DTPCH). Based on this cryptographic primitive, we further construct a high-security transaction-level redactable blockchain system, which ensures that the requirements of public accountability and identity traceability are met in a decentralized IoT environment. Performance analysis shows that the DTPCH scheme incurs an acceptable overhead while maintaining high security.

title: Analysis of Dual Agent Single Machine Scheduling Problem and Optimal Strategy with Truncated Learning Effect
authors: Lan Qing, Wang Ji-Bo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study tackles the dual-agent scheduling problem on a single machine, integrating truncation learning effects to optimize agent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> costs. It examines the problem's NP-hard complexity and proposes a hybrid heuristic algorithm combining genetic algorithm's global search with simulated annealing's local optimization. Experiments show the algorithm reduces cost deviation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> compared to standalone methods for 20-100 tasks, with robustness to learning parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex>. Sensitivity analysis indicates higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex> lowers costs by 15 %, while high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> raises costs by 12 %. The model offers theoretical and practical value for production scheduling, with potential for multi-machine extensions.

title: Research and Application Case Analysis of Flexible Control Technology for New Energy Output and Energy Storage Based on 3D Digital Twin Model of Photovoltaic Energy Storage Power Station and Particle Swarm Multi-Objective Algorithm
authors: Lanrui Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Based on the characteristics and application scenarios of the Chongqing region, this project plans the 400kW/1000kWh multi-element integrated energy storage device, including the one set of 100kW/400kWh grid type lithium battery energy storage, one set of the 100kW/200kWh grid type lithium battery energy storage, the one set of 100kW/200kWh grid type sodium ion battery, and one set of 100kW/200kWh grid type sodium ion battery. Typical experimental scenarios include supporting and off grid switching, combining the long and shortterm operations, new energy consumption, peak shaving and valley filling, voltage support, and backup power supply. Within 20 seconds, the battery current exhibits the three stepped waveforms, and SOC value shows decreasing trend with time. Additionally, the voltage value of the capacitor unit exhibits the periodic RC component charge and discharge characteristic. The 8760 hour time series was learned using the neural network, with the neural network set to the 70 iterations and the VMD-SSALSTM algorithm set to 30 population times. The paper shows that VMD-SSA-LSTM hybrid model based on the 3-dimensional digital twin model of the energy storage plant has the higher prediction accuracy of the digital model than the single LSTM model and the VMD-LSTM model. The specific error is limited to 2, and the prediction error of the traditional single model is about 8. Therefore, the hybrid VMD-SSA-LSTM model has better fitting and prediction ability.

title: Classification of Brain Tumors Using a Fused Multi-Feature Stacking Ensemble Model
authors: Akash Pandey, Suvendu Rup, Sudhakar Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The lives of people are seriously threatened by brain tumors, especially if they are undiagnosed in their early stages. Tumor type has a significant impact on mortality rates, which highlights the importance of accurate early detection and classification. Although traditional diagnostic methods are frequently employed to detect malignancies, their classification accuracy and predictive performance remain insufficient for making trustworthy clinical decisions. This method uses a hybrid multi-feature stacking ensemble approach for various categories of brain tumor classification, utilizing publicly accessible MRI datasets in order to overcome these constraints. The suggested approach combines a number of preprocessing techniques, such as data augmentation to boost sample variety and weighted fractional filtering to improve anatomical features, edge sharpness, and noise suppression. Deep features retrieved using a pre-trained Mobile-Net architecture are combined with manually created descriptors, such as Gabor filters for localized texture characteristics and Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) for global frequency information. In order to minimize dimensionality redundancy, the fused feature space is reduced using probabilistic Principal Component Analysis (PPCA). Lastly, robust tumor categorization is achieved by using a stacking ensemble classifier. On a publicly accessible brain tumor MRI dataset, experimental assessments show that the proposed framework overwhelms conventional approaches and provides significant gains across a number of performance parameters.

title: Unpaired Underwater Image Synthesis with Cycle Consistent
authors: Xiangjie Meng, Qing Hong, Xiaomin Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This study introduced a cyclic consistency generation counter-network to achieve effective desorption from paired data dependence. By integrating the Scene Parsing at Exceptional Scale (ADE20K) land scene dataset and the Enhancing Underwater Visual Perception (EUVP) underwater scene dataset, the model can deploy depth training under noncollapsing conditions, and a relatively robust image translation mechanism can be established through the discovery of intrinsic correlations between land and underwater cross-domain features. Experimental results showed that this model is excellent at improving image quality. The UCIQE score of the generated images is 0.51, and the UIQM score is 4.20. These data demonstrate that this method can effectively simulate realistic color and clarity. This work provided high-quality simulation data for underwater target exploration. This has significant technical implications for promoting the intelligence of underwater robotic systems.

title: A Secure and Stable Trust-Aware Clustering Scheme for IoT-Integrated Vehicular Ad HOC Networks
authors: Anil Kumar, Avnish Kumar, Sunil Kumar, Abhay Bhatia, Amit Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Recently, next-generation transportation systems that communicate intelligently through the Internet of Things embedded into Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks have been developed. However, network stability and cluster trustworthiness are still threatened by frequent node mobility, dynamic topology, and security weaknesses. This paper presents a Centralized Trust-Aware Clustering Framework designed to improve cluster stability and secure communication in IoTenabled VANETs. Trust values are determined based on knowledge, experience, and reputation using Roadside Units as centralized authority. Selected CHs are bootstrapped based on these trust parameters and cluster nodes with malicious or compromised nodes are evicted from the cluster. A dynamic trust evaluation process of direct and indirect feedback ensures the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C H}$</tex> is continuously adapts to the evolving node behavior. Across all performance metrics studied, including throughput, end-to-end delay, packet delivery ratio, and cluster stability, CTCF significantly outperforms existing schemes 3VSR, R2SCDT, and AODV. It can be concluded that CTCF integration of Trust management in a centralized clustering architecture is highly robust, secure, and reliable.

title: RelicFormer: Digital Rebirth of Ancient Murals via Semantic Prior Distillation
authors: Cailing Wang, Peng Huang, Xingjia Tang, Hongwei Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The restoration of painted cultural relics is crucial for cultural heritage conservation, but existing deep learning methods are mostly oriented towards general images, making it difficult to meet their special requirements of faithfully restoring materials, colors and cultural semantics. To this end, we have constructed the first benchmark for painted cultural relic restoration, and proposed the RelicFormer method, which guides restoration by distilling the global semantic prior of the pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT). On the test set containing 889 murals, this method significantly outperforms existing technologies in metrics such as PSNR (29.87dB) and SSIM (0.9287). In particular, it exhibits stronger structural fidelity and semantic coherence in moderately and severely damaged areas, providing an effective solution and evaluation benchmark for high-fidelity digital restoration of cultural relics.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
