We then propose a CNN-BiLSTM model with attention mechanisms and gated fusion, where CNN extracts local features, BiLSTM captures global contextual representations, and a gated fusion module dynamically balances the two. Experimental results show that our model achieves 91.84% accuracy and 89.97% F1-score, outperforming competitive baselines including TextCNN, BiLSTM, RCNN, and CNN-BiLSTM. Ablation studies further confirm the contribution of each component. This work provides a reference for text classification in Uyghur and other low-resource languages.

title: Predictive Analytics of Adolescent Behavioral Patterns for Mental Health Forecasting
authors: Lingya Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Mental health issues have become a pressing global concern, affecting a growing number of adolescents. Many individuals experience psychological distress without timely recognition, as traditional assessment methods relying heavily on subjective self-reports that often suffer from bias, delayed detection, and underreporting. This gap leads to exacerbated mental health deterioration, impacting quality of life, academic performance, and social functioning, underscoring the urgent need for objective, real-time prediction tools. The study addresses this need by developing a multimodal machine learning framework for early mental state forecasting, with significant implications for enabling proactive interventions and alleviating healthcare burdens. The study utilizes a dataset that integrate diverse mental health factors including demographics, stress triggers, performance metrics (CGPA), satisfaction scores, and lifestyle data. For methodology, the study first preprocesses data through normalization, missing value imputation, and feature extraction to identify behavioral patterns. Then, the study compares four types of machine learning models: logistic regression, support vector machine (SVM), random forest, and XGBoost. Evaluates the model performances based on accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and AUC-ROC. Preliminary results show the proposed framework outperforms baseline models, with XGBoost achieving the highest AUC-ROC of 0.9610, demonstrating strong capability in distinguishing mental health states. These findings validate the potential of multimodal data and optimized machine learning algorithms in mental health prediction. Looking forward, the study aims to expand datasets with more diverse populations, refine feature engineering, and enhance real-world applicability, striving to develop a scalable system that improves adolescent mental health monitoring and intervention efficacy.

title: DepreXAI: An Explainable Transformer Ensemble Framework for ICD-10 Based Depression Severity Classification from Social Media Text
authors: Reshu Tyagi, Nishant Tomar, Ritu Ranjan Chaubey, Piyush Chauhan, Paras Garg, Mohit Bohra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Depression is one of the most critical public health challenges worldwide, affecting over 260 million people. Despite increased awareness, early detection remains difficult due to social stigma and limited access to mental health professionals. Social media platforms, where users openly express emotions, provide valuable signals for early depression identification.

title: SurfSurg6D: Geometry Consistent Dense Correspondence for Textureless Surgical Instrument Pose Estimation_supp1-3699201.mp4
authors: Yueming Jin
publish: N/A
abstract: Surgical instrument pose estimation provides crucial information for promising applications, including autonomous robotic surgery, skill assessment, and standardization of surgical workflow. However, this task remains highly challenging due to high precision requirements, frequent occlusions, textureless instruments, scarcity of depth information and very limited annotated data. These constraints often lead to unsatisfactory performance when employing general object pose estimation approaches to surgical scenarios. To address these issues, we first construct a new dataset SynSurg6D, to alleviate the data shortage in this task. We further propose SurfSurg6D, a dense correspondence framework tailored for surgical instrument pose estimation. Experimental results on the SurgRIPE, EndoVis2018 and SurgPose datasets demonstrate that the introduction of our generated dataset SynSurg6D is able to diversify the pose distributions, thus enhancing the performance of existing approaches. Furthermore, SurfSurg6D outperforms existing methods, pro viding a robust solution for precise and efficient RGB-only pose estimation. The code and dataset will be open-sourced at https://github.com/StackingDataYeti/SurfSurg6D.

title: Automated and Near-Flawless Micropipette PIH Assembly to MEMS Nanoforce Sensor With A Robotic Microscopy System_supp5-3672876.mp4
authors: Wendi Gao
publish: N/A
abstract: Peg-in-hole (PIH) assembly of micropipettes to micro-electromechanical system (MEMS) nanoforce sensors can be performed to improve spatial probing resolution and operation space, but their spatial attitude angle deviations and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-axial distances lead to unstable assembly, sensor performance deterioration, and even structural damage. In this article, an automated and near-flawless micropipette PIH assembly method was proposed based on the built robotic microscopy system, to the best of our knowledge, for the first time. Utilizing an ISPR-based method with a mechanical equilibrium model and a sliding mode controller, the planar deflection angle was adjusted below 0.07<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> without being pushed out of FOV. The out-of-plane inclination angle was then reduced to below 0.05<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> by the proposed GTC-based method, whose depth is obtained from a feasible FDTBR inclination metrology and mechanical contact model. The <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-axial distance was actively adjusted by an AIHM-based recenter method during sensor probe insertion, leading to a high assembly successful rate of 100% with negligible contact forces of 1.64 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\boldsymbol{\mu}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>N. The methodology effectiveness was validated through calibration tests, wherein no structural damage and performance deterioration were produced on the processed sensor for a long-term usage, indicating its great potential in nanoforce measurement.

title: Spatially Aligned AI Modeling of Air Quality and Cough Intensity: An Integrated Approach to Environmental Respiratory Health
authors: Swarna Deepa Balasubramanian
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Ambient air pollution is a well-established risk factor for respiratory irritation and increased cough frequency. With advances in digital cough detection technologies, it is now possible to quantify respiratory activity at a population level. Two public datasets were used: (1) Cough events [34434 data points] from the COUGHVID crowdsourcing project published under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International and (2) Real time Air Quality Index from various locations [3420 data points] from Open Government Data (OGD) Platform, India, published by Central Pollution Control Board. Among the environmental variables analyzed, sulfur dioxide (SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) exhibited a statistically significant positive correlation with cough intensity (r=0.1779, p=0.000557). Although the correlation magnitude is moderate, its statistical significance indicates a consistent association between higher SO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> concentrations and increased respiratory distress signals across the population. The modest correlation strength likely reflects the heterogeneity and partial misalignment of the underlying datasets-particularly differences in temporal resolution, participant sampling, and spatial granularity between the cough and air quality datasets. This model establishes a scalable framework for integrating environmental and respiratory health datasets through spatial annotation and alignment. In regions with elevated pollution levels, where respiratory health risks are inherently higher, AI-driven cough detection can function as an early digital indicator of emerging respiratory disease activity. Future work will focus on incorporating real-time temporal and spatial synchronization between cough and air quality data. The framework will also be expanded to explore tuberculosis (TB) transmission dynamics by spatiotemporal alignment of cough data with NIKSHAY TB notification records, providing insights into how environmental pollution interacts with the spread and intensity of infectious diseases such as tuberculosis.

title: Multiple Fruit Quality Assessment System
authors: Meghana Deshpande, Rupali Kamathe, Vandana Hanchate, Ibtesam Pathan, Shital Bhadre, Sahil Kangane
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Ensuring the quality of fruits has become increasingly important as the demand for safe and fresh produce continues to rise. Manual checking based on appearance often leads to inconsistent results because it depends on individual judgment and can be slow when handled at a large scale. To overcome these limitations, this work proposes a lightweight, real-time fruit quality assessment system that identifies fruit type and evaluates freshness using deep learning. MobileNetV2 with transfer learning is used as the core model, trained on around 3,200 annotated images of apples, bananas, mangoes and oranges collected from the FruitVision dataset. All samples were resized to 224×224 pixels and labelled for both fruit category and quality (fresh/rotten). The trained model was converted to TensorFlow Lite and deployed on a Raspberry Pi 4B paired with a USB camera for live prediction. The system achieves 98.95% accuracy for fruit classification and 97.27% accuracy for quality grading under controlled testing. The results highlight that a low-cost, portable embedded setup can perform fruit quality analysis reliably, making it suitable for markets, farms, retail shops and supply-chain monitoring.

title: NARNET-Based Traffic Prediction for Dynamic Spectrum Allocation: A Pre-FLATS-5G Baseline Study
authors: Susil Kumar Nayak, Benudhar Sahu, Madhusmita Panda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In today’s world, advances in wireless communication not only demand large capacity but also the availability of resources at all times. This requires high real-time traffic prediction and allocation in various real-time sectors. The older version of network allocation faced real-time challenges as the traffic size increased drastically over the years. Currently, we need to allocate bandwidth dynamically in 5G wireless networks for accurate traffic prediction and to optimize spectrum utilization. This paper improves the results of the baseline paper, and this is only the first phase of the pre-FLATS-5G (Federated Learning-Augmented Transformer-LSTM) baseline phase, implementing Nonlinear AutoRegressive Network with eXogenous inputs (NARNET) for traffic prediction in 5G cellular networks. This paper addresses the data scarcity problem by proposing synthetic time series sequences while preserving statistical properties. Our modified version of NARNET results in a relative error (RE) of 0.0118 and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9908, significantly improving the base paper’s RE of 0.132 (91.1% improvement), and the MAE/RMSE metrics validate prediction accuracy across 173 cells. The paper showcases that with the acceptable delay parameter of (delay=8), the effective traffic prediction occurs. The future phases plan to further optimize the complete FLATS-5G framework using LSTM, transformer, and federated learning architectures.

title: Hybrid Chaos-Based Black Widow Optimization and Genetic Algorithm for Multi-Objective Load Balancing in Cloud Computing
authors: Mohith Varun Regalagadda, Nandini Kodali, Sankar Jai Pavan Uttaravalli, Keerthi Madugula, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The issue of load balancing has become central to the sphere of cloud computing, mostly because there exist seemingly conflicting goals like making the makespan minimal, eliminating the energy usage, maximizing the utilization of resources, and restraining the disproportion of the virtualized resources. Against this backdrop, the current paper outlines a hybrid meta-heuristic based framework of scheduling whereby integrating Chaos-enhanced Black Widow Optimization (CBWO) with Genetic Algorithms (GA) operators in a synergistic manner can significantly enhance the multi-objective performance in the context of heterogeneous clouds. The offered CBWO hybrid model takes advantage of chaotic perturbations as a means of intensifying the exploration of solution space, and the GA-based crossover mechanisms are used to explore good areas and increase the stability of solutions. This two-pronged approach allows making a more proportional trade-off between competing goals. Empirical test results on the CloudSim simulation framework, including increasing workload conditions, have shown that the CBWO-GA configuration provides a 25–30% decrease in makespan, an 80–85% resource utilization rate, and a near-optimal load-imbalance value of 1.1, outperforming the CBWO-only and CBWO-SA configurations. These results conclusively confirm the effectiveness of the hybrid CBWO-GA design in the modern data center that needs scalable, balanced, and energy-sensitive scheduling designs. The findings highlight the promise of using chaotic dynamics combined with evolutionary operators to tackle the problem of complex multi-objective optimization in cloud computing.

title: Aquanext: Intelligent Aquaculture Based on IoT and AI for Sustainable Shrimp Farming in Sri Lanka
authors: Gunasekara H.A.D. N, De Silva G.P.T. R, Palihawadana P.A.P. A, Senavirathna G.M.J. S, Anjana Junius Vidanaralage, Osuri Dunuwila
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Shrimp aquaculture is economically significant in Sri Lanka, yet smallholder farms frequently experience production losses due to limited continuous water-quality monitoring, inefficient feeding strategies, delayed disease-risk detection, and fragmented decision-making processes. This study proposes an integrated Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Internet of Things (IoT)-based framework designed to support sustainable and cost-effective shrimp farming under resourceconstrained conditions. The system consists of four coordinated modules: (1) water-quality prediction and automated control, (2) acoustic-based feeding optimization, (3) multimodal stress and disease-risk screening, and (4) AI-driven decision support. The water-quality module was evaluated using operational-style datasets under time-aware validation protocols, achieving a 6 hour dissolved oxygen forecasting performance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${R}^{{2}} \boldsymbol{=} {0. 9 4}$</tex> (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.31 \text{mg} / \mathrm{L})$</tex>, while ammonia-risk classification reached 91.3 % accuracy with an average lead time of 14.2 hours. The feeding module, validated using a leakage-aware experimental setup across multiple pond environments, achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95.2 \% \pm 1.3 \%$</tex> classification accuracy. Extended pilot deployments conducted over 90-120 culture days across 8-12 ponds were associated with a measurable reduction in feed waste under comparable operational conditions. While the evaluation was not conducted under a fully controlled experimental design, the observed improvements were consistent across multiple ponds and production cycles; however, further controlled large-scale validation is required to quantify causal impact. The diseaserisk module integrates environmental and visual indicators through a composite stress index, providing early warning signals aligned with retrospective outbreak patterns. The decision-support system demonstrated strong alignment with Best Management Practice (BMP)-based recommendations under controlled evaluation scenarios. While results demonstrate technical feasibility and system integration capability, the findings are derived from a combination of controlled experiments, pilot deployments, and scenario-calibrated datasets. Therefore, large-scale multi-site validation is required to confirm generalizability, reproducibility, and long-term economic impact.

title: Design and Analysis of an Ultrasonic Repeller to Combat Red Spider Mites in Tea Gardens
authors: Ram Toi, Pronita Bora, Sushanta Kabir Dutta, Pankaj Sarkar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In this paper an ultrasonic repeller is designed to control red spider mite (RSM) in tea gardens which is scientifically known as Tetranychus urticae. The repeller is designed using a voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) where a specific range of frequencies are used to combat the RSMs. The VCO is designed using single-ended delay cells, arranged in an even-numbered feedback loop to automatically produce symmetric four-phase outputs with evenly spaced phases. The design allows robust multi-phase operation across multiple phases while reducing the volume and complexity overall. A tuning mechanism with variable resistance allows for precise and smooth frequency control, enabling dual-mode operation and greater effective tuning coverage and an integrated band-switching network. Simulation results demonstrate stable oscillation and phase symmetry in a well-defined range (approximately 40 kHz to 48 kHz) and achieve efficient utilization as an ultrasonic repeller to repel RSM. A single-ended topology thus provides full-swing output with no biasing circuits, thereby contributing to better signal integrity and lesser power consumption. These results indicate that even-phase single-ended delay-cell architectures can alleviate the traditional of ring VCOs while preserving simplicity and efficiency.

title: Building Resilient Knowledge Infrastructures: A Sustainable Risk Governance Framework for Public Libraries in the Philippines
authors: Johanne Raymond S. Gaspar, Martin Luis H. Vibar, Ralph Angelico D. Lagunzad, Channen Kyle S. Cruz, Jeremy N. Nervar, Jaypy T. Tenerife
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: The research categorized risks into three main classifications: Strategic Risk, Financial Risk, and Operational Risk faced by the Quezon City Public Library, Philippines. These risks assess how each affects the public library's overall capacity for sustainable governance. The researchers conducted a qualitative analysis and gathered insights from key stakeholders through in-depth interviews. The data gathered was used to develop a structured framework on how each risk classification interacts and impacts the library. Based on the findings, a thematic analysis was conducted to clearly illustrate the identified risks and prioritize potential threats that may affect the library's purpose and direction. The findings highlighted the importance of effective governance and strategic objectives to meet the community's needs. Effective governance through coordination with Local Government Units ensures justified allocation of resources to enhance institutional facilities and technological capacity. This coordination will ensure the library has a clear objective to adapt to the community's changing needs and provide sustainable education. Modernizing the library's capacity to serve its community will elevate libraries' reputation in the public's mind, increase community readership and engagement, and strengthen the library's position in the community.

title: Design and Performance Analysis of a Minkowski Fractal–Integrated Vivaldi Antenna for Microwave Imaging Applications
authors: P.Raja, S.Sivasankari, Jagannath.R, Harrish.T, Kavin.M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Microwave imaging is an emerging non-invasive medical diagnostic technique that uses low-power, non-ionizing electromagnetic waves. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Minkowski fractal – Embedded Vivaldi Antenna (MFV) for microwave head imaging applications. The proposed antenna is of size 50 x 50 x 1.6 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. The incorporation of the Minkowski fractal geometry significantly improves the performance of the antenna by enhancing the impedance matching, as well as increasing the operational bandwidth of the antenna design. MFV antenna provides effective, wide-band operation and is capable of operating over the range of frequencies from 3.76 GHz to 9.34 GHz. MFV antenna achieved maximum gain of 8.57 dB with a radiation efficiency of 85%. The simulated results indicate that MFV antenna stands out as a strong candidate for ultra-wideband (UWB) microwave imaging applications.

title: Comparing BPR Vs. MSE Losses in the Context of Graph Based Recommenders
authors: Arya Ray, Vishv Patel, Arunachalam N.
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Graph-based recommender systems are a powerful approach to personalized recommender systems due to their ability to capture user-item interaction patterns. This study presents a comparative analysis, using a graph-based recommendation model using LightGCN, between two different loss functions, namely, Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Bayesian Personalized Ranking (BPR). Using the MovieLens <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 K}$</tex> dataset, both models are evaluated using metrics such as precision@k, recall@k, and ndcg@k. The findings in this specific scenario reveal that the MSE based model outperforms the BPR-based model with precision@20 of 0.52206 as against 0.13102. The recall@20 values are 0.63554 for the MSE-based model and 0.0471 for the BPR-based model. The model trained with MSE has ndcg@ 20 score of 0.94249 on the test data while the BPRbased model has ndcg@20 score of 0.10295. Although it can be concluded that MSE performs better as a loss function in this specific scenario, it does not necessarily imply that it is efficient and well adapted to all other recommendation scenarios. The BPR-based model performs well in an implicit feedback setting, whereas the MSE-based model outperforms BPR in an explicit feedback setting, thus making the choice and performance setting and objective dependent.

title: Facial Emotion Detection Using Pre-Trained CNN Models: A Study on Fine-Tuning and Robustness in Real-Time Settings
authors: Rovshana Haji-Hasanli, Eren Ulu
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Real-time facial emotion recognition is an important component of human-computer interaction, yet the reliability of such systems under unconstrained conditions remains insufficiently understood. This study investigates the practical reliability of a real-time facial emotion recognition system based on a pre-trained convolutional neural network across realistic webcam scenarios involving variations in illumination, pose, occlusion, and natural facial motion. The proposed pipeline integrates lightweight task-specific fine-tuning, alternative face detection pipelines, temporal smoothing strategies, and Grad-CAM-based interpretability analysis. Experimental results show that fine-tuning and temporal smoothing substantially improve prediction stability and interpretability without increasing model complexity, while also revealing persistent semantic ambiguity for visually similar expressions under degraded conditions.

title: Adaptive Sampling Pattern Design and Sparse Estimation for Compressed Spectrum Sensing: A Hybrid Algorithm Approach
authors: Fariha Alam Rafa, Md. Minhajul Islam Arnab, Sk. Shariful Alam, Rubiat Hasan Khan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Compressed Spectrum Sensing has become a powerful solution to identify opportunistic frequency bands in Cognitive Radio Networks for dynamic spectrum access. The Basic principle of compressed spectrum sensing is to exploit signal sparsity to sample signals below the sub-Nyquist rate and the samples are chosen selectively to be used in pattern designing, significantly reducing the sampling burden and energy consumption while maintaining accurate detection. Traditional frameworks such as uniform and multicoset sampling techniques relying on predefined sampling patterns with greedy reconstruction algorithms, often resulting in imperfect and poor signal recovery that increases false-alarm and miss-detection probabilities. Such approaches are mostly unsuitable for time-varying and dynamic spectral environments. This study proposes an integration of the adaptive sampling pattern with a subsequent hybrid sparse estimation approach, to estimate the active frequency bands or their location in time domain, which conventional methods mostly struggle to identify. This system framework distinguishes itself from traditional frameworks by including the adaptability to refine sampling index improving measurement efficiency and by generating an effective sensing matrix based on mutual coherence with the sparsifying basis. Further, adaptive thresholding ensures robustness by accommodating varying noise and signal conditions in practice. By integrating this adaptiveness to outperform the conventional techniques that rely on fixed sampling, this framework utilizes both Greedy reconstruction and a subsequent Deep Learning-based refinement.

title: EV Charging Mandates and Their Impact on Load Profiles in Multi-Unit Residential Buildings
authors: Yesaswini Chilukuri, Adil Usman, Pramod J Chaudhary, Payman Dehghanian, Wei-Jen Lee, Bharat Singh Rajpurohit
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) adoption is driving mandates that require a portion of parking spaces in multi-unit residential buildings (MURBs) to be EV-ready or equipped with active chargers. This study evaluates the impact of such mandates on building electrical load profiles and peak demand under varying EV adoption scenarios. Using representative MURB building models and a calculation-based peak-load analysis, the study quantifies how different levels of EV charger deployment affect evening peak electrical demand. Results show that peak electrical demand can increase by more than 130 % under full EV adoption, with EV charging becoming the dominant contributor to the evening peak. These findings provide a reference for planners, designers, and utilities to anticipate the electrical infrastructure requirements associated with EV mandates in multifamily housing.

title: EcoWardrobe: an AI-Based System for Evaluating Sustainable and Ethical Fashion
authors: P Elumalaivasan, Paruchuri Geeta Chinmayee, Mythri Gadepalli
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Growing interest in responsible production has encouraged the fashion sector to adopt intelligent assessment tools. EcoWardrobe proposes a novel computational approach that interprets user-provided clothing descriptions to estimate their ecological and ethical implications. Instead of relying on predefined product data, the system processes open-ended text to identify relevant material, coloration, and sourcing details. These clues are examined through adaptive learning models developed from verified sustainability references and fair-trade indicators. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework achieves high predictive accuracy, with the sustainability scoring model attaining an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 9 2}$</tex> and the ethical scoring model achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 0 0 0}$</tex>. These results validate the effectiveness of the selected machine learning models and the reliability of the curated environmental and ethical datasets. The outcome is a balanced evaluation reflecting both environmental and social dimensions of each garment. To promote clarity, EcoWardrobe incorporates interpretable reasoning methods and natural-language generation components that translate technical outputs into accessible insights. Built with a layered software design, the framework demonstrates how artificial intelligence can assist in transparent, scalable evaluation of sustainable fashion products.

title: Exploring the Synergy between AI and 5G Technology in Promoting the Innovation of Industrial Energy Infrastructure
authors: Xiu Li, Zhixuan Yang, Yinglu Liu, Pikunthong Nukthamna, Yingyu Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Driven by the global digital transformation and the transition to a low-carbon economy, industrial energy infrastructure is undergoing an unprecedented paradigm shift. The fourth industrial revolution (Industry 4.0) and the evolving fifth industrial revolution (Industry 5.0) not only require manufacturing and production processes to be highly automated and flexible, but also impose strict real-time and sustainability requirements on energy consumption, distribution, and optimization. Against this backdrop, the deep integration of fifth-generation mobile communication technology (5G) and artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming the core engine for reshaping industrial energy management, smart grids (Smart Grids), and the coordination of distributed energy resources (DERs). This paper comprehensively explores the collaborative effect of artificial intelligence and 5G technology in promoting innovation in industrial energy infrastructure. Through an in-depth literature review, this paper points out the research gaps in current studies in addressing dynamic grid load, resolving the “red AI “ and “green AI “ paradox of 5G high energy consumption, and the lack of an end-to-end collaborative framework. To this end, this study constructs a multi-layer theoretical framework that includes physical hardware optimization, edge-cloud collaborative network slicing, and digital twin intelligent decision-making. In response to the current lack of empirical data, this paper designs a logically consistent distributed microgrid simulation experiment framework, using hierarchical deep reinforcement learning (HDRL) and convolutional neural networks (CNN) to jointly optimize 5G traffic and energy scheduling. The simulation results show that this collaborative framework not only reduces network latency by 88.3 % in ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) scenarios, but also achieves a 65.0 % reduction in base station energy consumption while maintaining grid harmonic quality, and reduces unplanned downtime by more than 80 %. This research provides solid theoretical support and quantitative evidence for building a zero-carbon emission, adaptive, and highly resilient next-generation smart energy ecosystem.

title: EFECE: An Explainable Feature-Based Evaluation and Comparative Explanation Framework for Large Language Models
authors: Ertürk Erdaği
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Large Language Models offer successful performance in text classification and explain their decisions using natural language processing methods. However, the causal agreement between these explanations and the model's decision-making process is often unclear. This study presents a multidimensional framework called EFECE to evaluate the reliability of explanations generated by Large Language Models. This framework analyzes label agreement, lexical alignment, causal fidelity, and the consistency between these metrics. Within the scope of the study, experiments were conducted with a finely tuned transformer model for sentiment analysis on a Turkish dataset, using GPT-4o and GPT-4o-mini language models. The results show a weak relationship between lexical overlap and causal fidelity. While raw overlap was 0.05 due to the effect of sub-word tokenization, it increased to 0.25 with normalization. The study concludes that high lexical similarity is not necessarily considered high fidelity and that a holistic approach is needed in evaluating the explanation.

title: An Adjustable Window for FIR Filter Design with Improved Spectral Characteristics and Application
authors: Amit Kumar Patil, Manoj Hans, Virendra Shete, Rohan Shinde
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This paper introduces a modern flexible window function, which exhibits linear phase response and stability for designing the finite impulse response filters. It consists of a power parameter γ for regulating the shape associated with the window design. The recommended window is contrasted against the various adjustable windows, analogous to Gaussian, Kaiser, and Dolph-Chebyshev. The simulation-driven outcomes indicate that the finite impulse response filter developed utilizing the presented window offers 89.42 dB higher attenuation of sidelobes than the Gaussian window, 53.77 dB and 87.53 dB improved attenuation of sidelobes against the Kaiser window and the Dolph-Chebyshev window. All the stated filters are employed on a noisy audio signal for signal denoising. As a result, the filter developed using the proposed window provides 5.037 dB better (S/N) signal-to-noise ratio as contrasted to the Gaussian filter. Also, it provides 2.072 dB and 2.109 dB improved signal-to-noise ratio, superior to the Kaiser and Dolph-Chebyshev filters, respectively.

title: MentalBot: An AI-Driven Conversational System for Mental Health Monitoring Using Smart Wearable
authors: Stambamkadi Yagnyasri, Sandeep Lonkala
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: There is an increased risk of mental disorders like stress, anxiety and depression because of high rate of lifestyle changes, academic stress, job related stress and lack of social life. In spite of increased understanding of mental health, mental healthcare is not very accessible because of the social stigma, high treatment cost, and lack of skilled practitioners. Over the past years, with the progress of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Natural Language Processing (NLP), and wearable technologies, new opportunities of the 24-hour and constant mental health assistance became a possibility. The present paper introduces MentalBot, an artificial intelligence-based mental health monitoring, a conversational system that combines intelligent chatbot interface and smart wearable data in order to provide ongoing emotional evaluation. The system offered allows users to communicate emotions in a more natural way utilizing text-based conversational interfaces, whereas basic physiological measurements like heart rate and physical activity are measured using wearable devices using Google Fit APIs. NLP is used to process the content of conversation to identify emotional conditions like stress or anxiety. The system continues a conversation context through a vector database in order to have continuity and relevance in the answer. In comparison with the conventional mental health applications that are based on the periodic assessments, MentalBot offers a user-friendly, noninvasive, and scalable application associated with continuous emotional awareness. The results of experimental testing show a better level of user engagement and emotion recognition and can be easily trusted when it comes to producing responses, which means that the system could be used as a helpful tool to maintain mental health.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Parkinson's Disease Severity Detection Using Hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Fuzzy Logic
authors: R. Vidhya, B. Hariharan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Parkinson's disease (PD) significantly affects gait dynamics, requiring accurate and early-stage severity detection for effective clinical intervention. This study proposes a deep learning (DL)-based PD severity detection framework using Vertical Ground Reaction Force (VGRF) signals. The methodology comprises three phases: data acquisition, preprocessing, and classification. In the data acquisition stage, VGRF signals from healthy subjects and PD patients are collected from the PhysioNet Gait Dataset. During preprocessing, the raw signals undergo normalization and windowing to enhance stability and ensure consistent temporal analysis. For classification, a hybrid Convolutional Neural Network-Fuzzy Logic (CNN-FL) model is employed, where the CNN extracts discriminative gait features and the Fuzzy Logic system performs interpretable severity-level classification. The CNN parameters are further optimized using the Perfumer Optimization Algorithm (POA) to improve convergence and enhance classification accuracy. The proposed model is implemented in MATLAB and evaluated using standard performance metrics, achieving an accuracy of 98.9%, demonstrating its strong effectiveness for reliable and clinical PD severity assessment.

title: Fine-Tuning and Deployment of a Vision-Language-Action Based Robotic Foundation Model for Open-Source Robot Arms
authors: Mücahit Emre Kabaoğlu, Mustafa Hikmet Bilgehan Uçar
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents an end-to-end robotic infrastructure for deploying vision-language-action (VLA) models on the open-source BCN3D Moveo robot arm. The approach combines a ROS2-based real-time control stack, a high-fidelity digital twin in NVIDIA Isaac Sim, a LeRobot-formatted dataset converted from RLDS, and efficient fine-tuning strategies for the GROOT 1.5 foundation model. The proposed framework targets low-cost manipulation with a single RGB camera while addressing sim-to-real transfer, generalization to unseen objects, and data scarcity. Experimental evaluation reports a 60% task success rate (95% CI: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$38.7\%-78.1 \%$</tex>) on the selected manipulation setup, with most failures stemming from pre-grasp alignment uncertainty and partial visual occlusion. An ablation study over training data volume (39 vs. 47 episodes) and fine-tuning epoch depth shows that richer task variation shifts the optimal checkpoint from mid- to late-stage training, yielding a 7.8 % improvement in offline MSE. Comparative fine-tuning experiments further reveal that backbone LoRA training for GR00T and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pi_{0}$</tex> is blocked by HuggingFace Transformers incompatibilities, while OpenVLA full fine-tuning fails due to out-of-memory constraints. Offline action-space error analysis reports MSE, RMSE, and MAE values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1073,0.3275$</tex>, and 0.1523, respectively.

title: AI-Driven Predictive Model for Coronary Artery Disease Diagnosis
authors: Uma Maheswari S, C Srinivasan, Ebenezer Y, Priya Dasarwar, Nilamadhab Mishra, Prakash S
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Coronary artery disease (CAD) is a predominant cause of global death, and prompt identification is essential for effective treatments. Conventional diagnostic techniques are frequently intrusive, costly, or protracted. This research presents an AI-based predictive model for CAD detection utilizing clinical factors from a publically accessible dataset containing 303 patient records, which includes 216 CAD cases and 87 non-CAD controls. The model utilizes XGBoost, chosen for its superior predictive accuracy and resilience with tabular clinical data. Essential clinical attributes, including age, cholesterol levels, blood pressure, and several laboratory metrics, were preprocessed and underwent feature selection to enhance model efficacy. The XGBoost model achieved an accuracy of 98.35%, and feature importance analysis indicated that age, cholesterol levels, and resting blood pressure were the predominant predictors. The results indicate that AI-driven models can deliver rapid, non-invasive, and extremely precise CAD risk assessments, potentially aiding physicians in early diagnosis, enhancing patient outcomes, and diminishing dependence on invasive procedures. Future efforts will focus on external validation and incorporation into clinical decision-support systems.

title: Quantifying the Impact of Load Outliers on Demand Charges for Non-residential Customers
authors: B Hari Kiran Reddy, Sivanand Puliyadi Ravi
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Non-residential electricity tariffs commonly include demand charges that bill customers based on their maximum power draw within each billing period. Because these charges depend on short-lived load peaks, statistically rare events can disproportionately influence monthly costs. This paper introduces a statistical metric to quantify the share of billed demand-charge exposure attributable to load outliers, defined relative to customerspecific variability. Analytical models illustrate how the frequency and magnitude of these outliers depend on the underlying load distribution and sampling depth, providing insight into why rare events dominate billing outcomes. Using smart-meter data from 730 business buildings recorded at 15 -minute resolution, results show that less than 5 % of intervals account for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70-90 \%$</tex> of total demand-charge costs. The findings establish a quantitative basis for interpreting demand-charge volatility and can inform tariff design and load-management strategies across diverse nonresidential settings.

title: Design and Development of a Microcontroller-based Lithium ION Battery Tester
authors: Charugundla Aasritha, Jalalu Guntur, Gudipudi Akanksha, Gudipudi Mahesh, Gorla Jai Krishna
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In educational labs, renewable energy systems, and electric automobiles, battery performance measurement is crucial. However, commercial battery analysers are frequently costly and complicated, and traditional multimeters only offer rapid data. The design and development of a low-cost smart battery performance analyser utilising the PZEM-015 energy metering module is presented in this work. In addition to directly calculating cumulative ampere-hour capacity and energy during charge and discharge cycles, the suggested system measures voltage, current, and power in real-time. Measured ampere-hour values and rated battery capacity are used to create a basic State of Charge (SoC) estimation. The technology focusses on direct electrical sensing and integration rather than intricate battery management algorithms.The experimental findings show that under regulated discharge settings, voltage behaviour, current flow, energy delivery, and capacity can all be reliably tracked. The suggested analyser is appropriate for educational labs, small workshops, and field-level battery testing applications because of its simplicity, affordability, and measurement precision.

title: ChipGPT: Leveraging Large Language Models for Automated and Optimized Soc Floorplanning
authors: Aswini S, Anto Kumar R P, Varun CM, Seenuvasan P, Dhanalakshmi R, G Sekar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The escalating complexity of system-on-chip (SoC) designs has rendered traditional floor planning a critical physical design stage that is increasingly manual, timeconsuming, and expertise dependent. Although automated tools exist, they often converge to suboptimal solutions and struggle with complex multimodal constraints. This study introduces ChipGPT, a novel framework that leverages the advanced reasoning and sequence modeling capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate and optimize SoC floor planning. ChipGPT encodes the netlist, macroproperties, and design constraints into a structured textual prompt. An LLM agent, acting as an intelligent optimizer, interprets the prompt and proposes iterative floorplan refinements. These proposals are evaluated by a cost engine that calculates the wirelength, area, and congestion, with the results fed back to guide subsequent reasoning. On standard benchmarks, ChipGPT demonstrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> reduction in total wirelength compared to conventional simulated-annealing and commercial auto-placer baselines. It generates highquality, human-competitive floor plans in a fraction of the time. This study establishes a new paradigm by demonstrating LLMs' potential of LLMs as intelligent agents for complex, constraint-driven optimization in electronic design automation.

title: Advancing Digital Wallet Security Through Multi-Factor Authentication and Homomorphic Encryption-Based Privacy Preservation
authors: Musa Dima Genemo, Kotha Anil Kumar, Quazi Taif Sadat, Kathari Santosh, Dr.G.Sreeram, I Infant Raj
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Digital wallets have been provided by FinTech businesses thus reengineering the financial services but nonetheless there is a possibility of keyloggers, bogus personas and assaults of the server with conventional systems using PIN authentication. The current multi-factor authentication configuration usually leaves sensitive biometric information exposed as the logins are processed. To address these security issues, the paper proposes the MHESecAuth that employs Multi-Factor Homomorphic Encryption and Smart Contracts to authenticate. The permission to make transactions is secure since the system operates with PINs, OTPs and no one can view the biometrics. As per experimental research, the system can be accurate at 96.4 percent, false acceptance rate is 0.08 percent, false rejection rate is 0.6 percent and encryption consumes 160ms. The framework optimizes security, privacy and performance against both MFA, encryption-only and combined solutions and is therefore suitable in the development of quantum-secure digital wallets and still blockchain-based audit and transparency.

title: Hybrid Feature Selection and Classification for EEG-Based Epileptic Seizure Detection Using SMO and Advanced Classification Models
authors: Sumant Kumar Mohapatra, Madhusmita Mohanty, Deba N. Pattanayak, Kaliprasanna Swain, Sabita Mali
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Epileptic seizures are characterized by instant and abnormal activity of the brain, and their early detection is crucial & important for proper clinical diagnosis and effective treatment. An electroencephalogram (EEG) is most effective to monitor the relevant electrical activity in the brains of seizure patients through non-invasive electrodes. This paper proposes a hybrid framework for the automated detection of seizures in patients through a combination of time-domain EEG signals, from the Bonn University EEG Dataset, with meta-heuristic feature selection algorithms and advanced classification architectures. The Hjorth parameters and Welch’s Method for Power Spectral Density (PSD) are implemented for efficient feature extraction from the recorded EEG signals, which are further selected and optimized by the ANOVA Test. The bio-inspired Starling Murmuration Optimizer Algorithm (SMO) is utilized for model parameter optimization. The final classification is performed using Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS), Extreme Learning Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ELANFIS) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) models, followed by a comparative evaluation to ascertain the most effective and accurate classifier under identical evaluation parameters. It was found that the SVM classifier provides the output in the form of statistical parameters like Accuracy, Sensitivity, and Specificity as 98.7%, 99 %, and 98.83% respectively which reflects its probability for efficient real-time clinical applications.

title: A Multimodal Hybrid Ensemble Approach Integrating ML and Explainable AI for Alzheimer's Prediction
authors: S Selvaragini, Gnanajeyaraman Rajaram
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: A severe brain disorder named Alzheimer's disease (AD), which worsens the memory and operational impairments. It is crucial to detect the disease at an early stage, which enhances the patient's health and therapeutic planning. In this paper, we proposed an ensemble approach integrating diverse learning classifiers such as Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbor (KNN), and Naïve Bayes (NB) to optimize the prognostic efficiency and dependability. This proposed architecture comprises data preprocessing, feature extraction, and a feature selection process based on a voting method for effective classification performance. The outcome shows that the proposed ensemble model achieves the highest accuracy and F1 score of 99.56%, outperforming traditional existing models stated in the study. This model offers a promising solution for achieving optimized prognostic accuracy, reliability, and practicality for predicting AD.

title: PLC based Water Distribution and Leakage Detection System with Quality Monitoring
authors: M.Lizzy Nesa Bagyam, Mr.S.Jegan, Dhivin Ragul B, Mouleeswari K, Madhukumaran M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: A dependable water distribution system that is easy to maintain and can provide reliable, continuous monitoring of the quality of the water distributed is an essential goal of the Water Distribution Monitoring System. The PLC-Based Intelligent Water Distribution System has been developed to allow for real-time detection and monitoring of flow, pressure, pH, turbidity and tank level values of the water being distributed, thereby ensuring that water is delivered reliably, and requires little, if any, human operator input. The Siemens S7-1200 PLC collects and processes information from the sensors to operate solenoid valves and/or pumps automatically using Ladder Logic programming. Information about the flow of water can be viewed by operators in real-time using the Siemens KTP700 HMI, thereby providing an opportunity for operators to become aware of problems that may arise in the system, receive alerts and perform control commands. In addition to being able to detect pressure changes in a flow and ascertain water quality standards, operators will be able to identify leaks and maintain stable levels of water. Experimental tests indicated that the PLC-Based Intelligent Water Distribution System is capable of detecting leaks, keeping levels of water stable and measuring the pH and turbidity of the water with accuracy. The ability of the PLC-Based Intelligent Water Distribution System to do so provides a clear opportunity for developing automated water networks and for implementing Sustainable Water Management practices.

title: A Robust Data Security Architecture for Iot-Enabled Smart Agriculture Using Machine Learning
authors: S. Sharmila, V. Jeyalakshmi
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Smart agriculture systems rely on continuous monitoring of crop conditions using IoT sensors and automated disease detection techniques. In this work, an integrated framework is developed using soil moisture, temperature, humidity, and NPK sensors connected to an Arduino platform for real-time environmental data collection. A lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is implemented for leaf disease classification using field-captured images, achieving an accuracy of 89.4 % with fast inference suitable for real-time use. To ensure secure data transmission, symmetric encryption methods (AES and DES) are applied to protect sensor readings and classification results from unauthorized access. AES provides stronger security, while DES offers faster but less secure performance. The overall system demonstrates efficient monitoring, reliable disease detection, and secure communication, making it suitable for practical smart agriculture applications.

title: Design of Low-Power and Area-Optimized Array Multiplier using Hybrid Logic Structure
authors: M Madhusudhan Reddy, Shaik Ameenulla, K. Hareesh Kumar Reddy, Belam Praveen Kumar, R.Mounika, Mangipudi Venu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: In DSP applications and embedded systems, multiplication is a fundamental building block that consumes a significant amount of execution time. When designing circuits in nanoscale technology, power dissipation, latency, and energy efficiency are the most critical factors. The design metrics i.e., energy consumption and over all area utilization can be improved through proper circuit optimization. In this work, Gate Diffusion Logic (GDI) and Transmission Gate Logic (TGL) are employed to design the array multiplier, where the AND gate, Half Adder (HA), and Full Adder (FA) serve as the primary building blocks. The simulation results are obtained using the 45-nm technology node in the Cadence EDA tool with a supply voltage of 1 V. The proposed array multiplier is compared with existing designs and achieves reductions of 65% in transistor count, 40% in delay, and 33% in power consumption.

title: Digital Twins in Power Electronics and Motor Drives: Architectures, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Akkarapaka Chiranjeevi Manikantan, Rohan Mathai Joseph, Allen Sony, Pravika M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The Digital Twin (DT) technology is becoming a game-changing tool for monitoring, control, and lifecycle optimization in applications related to motor-drive and power-electronic systems. This review is dedicated to the recent progress made in DT architectures applied to electrical machines, power converters, and electrified drivetrain subsystems. Early approaches to DT have relied primarily on hierarchical or physics-based models that support virtual commissioning and industrial deployment. More recent work introduces estimation-driven and hybrid frameworks that integrate real-time telemetry to enable enhanced state estimation, thermal forecasting, and degradation-aware performance evaluation. Representative advances consider observer-based twins in electric drives, thermal digital twins for mission-profile temperature prediction, Bayesian inference in inverter fault diagnostics, and DT-enabled design optimization of permanent-magnet machines. System-level studies further refer to the use of DTs in electrified vehicles and wide-area power-electronic infrastructures. Nevertheless, reaching real-time synchronization, multi-physics coupling, scalable deployment, and unified models that capture motor-converter interactions poses significant challenges. The present review synthesizes the current body of research, identifies gaps in methodology, and outlines future research directions towards resilient, integrated, industry-ready digital twin solutions for modern motor-drive and power-electronic applications.

title: Health Recommendation Model for Student Drug Addiction Risk Using Automated Machine Learning and Optimized Feature Selection
authors: Madhusree Kuanr, Subrat Kumar Nayak, Sujit Bebortta, Nrusingha Tripathy, Soumyarashmi Panigrahi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Drug addiction among students nowadays is a serious concern as it affects mental health, academic performance, and long-term well-being. Thus, early risk identification is essential for prompt prevention. This study presents a Health Recommender System that uses Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) and multi-objective feature selection to predict the risk of drug addiction among students. It uses a Multi-objective Harris Hawks Optimization (MOHHO) approach that jointly optimizes feature cardinality and classification performance along a Pareto front to choose compact, high-utility feature subsets. Crowding distance has been used to find diverse, well-distributed solutions. The chosen classifier is trained on each Pareto-reduced subset to create ranked prediction models after AutoML (TPOT) is used to find an efficient classifier and pipeline configuration on the entire dataset. The proposed model recommends the top-5 features and a prediction model for the accurate prediction of drug addiction in students. The proposed model outperforms the traditional feature selection methods like Recursive Feature Elimination, Mutual Information, and correlation-based selection in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.

title: Enhancing Security Performance in Large-Scale UAVs System Based on Mean Field Game Theory
authors: Yuandong Xu, Lan Tang
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The growing use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in wireless communication networks has increased the potential of eavesdropping, especially in the large-scale networks, which are the most vulnerable to this threat. This paper provides a solution, which uses mean field game (MFG) to improve the security and performance of large-scale communication networks of UAVs. The joint optimization of the UAV swarm in flight energy consumption, swarm congestion, obstacle avoidance risks, Quality of Service (QoS) constraint, and anti-eavesdropping requirement is modeled as a coupled system of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) and Fokker-Planck-Kolmogorov (FPK) equations. A G-proximal Primal-Dual Hybrid Gradient (PDHG) algorithm is embraced to resolve this complicated system effectively. As shown in numerical simulations, the offered approach can make UAVs autonomously plan secure flight routes and dynamically change transmit power to avoid obstacles and guarantee the proper and safe communication with ground stations in a timely manner. Findings confirm the convergence and success of MFG-based model with probabilistic constrained conditions.

title: Deep Hybrid CNN–ViT Model with Neural Architecture Search for Robust Breast Cancer Image Classification
authors: Bipasha Patnaik, Santanu Kumar Sahoo
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Breast Cancer continues to be a leading cause of cancer-related mortality among women worldwide, which underscores the need for comprehensive and early-stage detection techniques. Advanced deep learning (DL) models enable computational tumor classification by hierarchically extracting distinctive spatial, textual, and contextual representations from high-dimensional medical imaging data. This research presents a detailed comparative evaluation of four distinct DL architectures for cancerous tissue image identification: a baseline convolutional neural network (CNN), MobileNetV2, ResNet-18, and a hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer (CNN-ViT) model. An optimized preprocessing pipeline uses standardized image resizing, intensity scaling, denoising mechanisms, and augmentation-based data enhancement to reduce acquisition-induced artifacts, class distribution distortion, sample availability, improving representational consistency, generalization across unexplored data distributions. A comprehensive evaluation is conducted during the pre-processing and post-processing phases, using domain-relevant performance metrics. The preprocessing pipeline has been improved to achieve smoother optimization, reduce generalization error by minimizing noise-induced variance, increasing feature stability and improving predictive fidelity. Among the models evaluated, the CNN-ViT framework achieves 98.50% classification accuracy and strong class separability under imbalanced data distributions. The convolutional inductive frameworks for hierarchical local representation learning and transformer-based self-attention for global dependency modelling provide an accurate with scalable computer-aided breast cancer diagnosis framework that captures complex morphological patterns with long-range spatial connections.

title: Multi-Layer Perceptron for Hybrid Energy Storage Efficiency Prediction
authors: Kathaian R, N Mohankumar, Sirasani Srinivasa Rao, S K Saravanan, Prabhu V S, Sarathkumar D
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The efficient functioning of hybrid energy storage systems (HESS) is crucial for optimizing renewable energy use and reducing power losses. This work introduces a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) model designed to forecast the operational efficiency of HESS using a real-world dataset including timestamped records of renewable energy inputs, storage system measurements, load demand, power distribution, and efficiency classifications. The information encompasses solar, wind, and grid power, battery State-of-Charge (SoC), supercapacitor charge, hydrogen generation, and system efficiency categorized as Low, Medium, or High. The proposed MLP model exhibits robust predictive performance, with an overall accuracy of 94.2% in identifying system efficiency under diverse operating settings. The model consistently distinguishes between Low-, Medium-, and High-efficiency states, demonstrating its capacity to include complex nonlinear interactions among renewable energy inputs, storage dynamics, and load demands. These findings highlight the capability of MLP-based modelling for real-time efficiency assessment and energy management in hybrid storage systems. The technique provides a scalable, data-driven solution that enhances energy allocation, minimizes power loss, and improves operational stability for resilient and sustainable renewable energy networks.

title: A Lightweight and Explainable Framework for Emotion Cause Pair Extraction
authors: Latha R, Karthikeyan K, Dinesh Kumar K, Muthu Krishnan V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Emotion-Cause Pair Extraction (ECPE) from conversational text is challenging due to context ambiguity, sarcasm, and informal language. Existing ECPE methods often fail to correctly link emotions with their true causes when such variations appear in real-world conversations. To address this problem, this paper proposes a lightweight and explainable ECPE framework that combines structured linguistic processing with deep semantic modeling. The framework includes hybrid text normalization, sarcasm detection, and dependency-based clause segmentation, followed by a shared DistilBERT encoder. A joint multi-task learning architecture is used to predict emotions and their corresponding causes simultaneously. To improve training robustness, a unified master dataset with clause-level annotations and sarcasm labels is constructed. Experimental results show improved emotion–cause alignment and reduced computational complexity compared to larger transformer-based models. The proposed system supports detailed customer feedback analysis and enables emotion-aware intelligent applications.

title: Comparison of Intelligent Thrust Control Strategies for Wall Climbing Adhesion Systems for Wall Dust Cleaning Application
authors: Parthiban B, Gokulraj R, Gopikrishnan P, Kushaan Anand, Rajesh M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Manual wall cleaning of ceiling sidewalls is a dangerous and labor-intensive process, especially at greater heights. This paper proposes a Wall Dust Mechanoid robot for wall cleaning, which moves via a thrust-based adhesion mechanism driven by a Brushless Direct Current (BLDC) motor that produces a maximum thrust force of 10.045 N, while the robot’s movement can be controlled remotely through a web-based dashboard by a human user. Intelligent control of thrust force for wall adhesion regulation is critical due to the dynamic changes in wall conditions that cause the thrust force to change continuously. This paper proposes five control strategies for thrust force control solely for the BLDC motor, which include No Control, Proportional Integral Derivative (PID), Fuzzy Logic, Model Reference Adaptive Control (MRAC), and a Multilayer Perceptron Artificial Neural Network (MLP-ANN). A physics-based MATLAB model that uses a simulation model based on the actual hardware specifications was developed to compare the performance of the five control strategies. The results showed that the MLP-ANN control strategy has the least error in thrust force control while reducing the fall risk index by 58% compared to the No Control strategy, followed by MRAC (45%), Fuzzy Logic (35%), and PID (27%).

title: Cancelable scheme for fingerprint template protection using PUF and ARX Encryption
authors: Singarapu Pavan Kumar, Dilip Kumar Vallabhadas, M Sitharam, Jhansi Rani Inturi
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: With the fast growth of modern technology, biometrics has become a common method of verifying a person’s identity. However, a major challenge in biometric authentication is ensuring template security, which is essential to protect user privacy. To enhance this security, the original feature-based template is transformed into a pseudo-template before use. In this paper, we first extract discriminative features from the biometric trait. After feature extraction, features are then flattened and converted into secure binary templates using Physical Unclonable Function(PUF) scheme coupled with a Addition Rotation XOR (ARX) mechanism, ensuring both revocability and non-invertibility. The final fused template forms a cancelable biometric representation that protects against template compromise while preserving recognition performance. The proposed approach is evaluated on the Children Multimodal Biometric Database (CMBD), demonstrating strong privacy protection with high precision. The experimental results show that the proposed scheme achieves an Equal Error Rate (EER) of 0.014%.

title: Enhanced Wireless Charging Station with Smart User Management and Battery Management System
authors: M. Sivaram Krishnan, Samuel Morris T, Girinath A, Irshad Ahmad S, Praveen Kumar S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This work outlines a smart IoT-based wireless electric vehicle (EV) charging platform that involves RFID authentication and an intelligent Battery Management System (BMS), to enhance charging safety, automation, and convenience to the user. The device operates by using Inductive Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) to transmit energy through a transmitter and receiver coil mounted on the floor and onboard respectively, eliminating the use of physical hookups. An IR sensor detects vehicle presence and parking orientation, and RFID authentication helps to ensure secure prepaid user identification before the initiation of the session. The AC output is rectified and regulated forming a stable DC charging bus. A BMS monitors the battery voltage and battery temperature to prevent overcharging and thermal stress, and as the battery approaches full charge, it switches to CV mode and reduces the charging current with minimal voltage stress. The sensors, authentication, relay control, and data collection are controlled by an Arduino Nano, and a Wi-Fi module is used to transmit the session data to the ThingSpeak cloud platform in real time. According to the laboratory prototype test, stable wireless power transfer was achieved through a 5 to 10 cm air gap, producing a regulated output of 9 to 12 V DC at 200 to 1300 mA. The specified voltage limit and over-temperature or under-balance conditions reliably triggered automatic shutdown. The cloud updates of ThingSpeak are delayed by 2 to 5 seconds relative to local LCD readings, confirming consistent data integrity of battery voltage, charging time, cost deduction, and remaining balance. The prototype demonstrates a fully automated, secure, and verifiable wireless EV charging system suitable for next-generation smart charging infrastructure.
