title: IoT Data Processing Framework for Crop Monitoring in Smart Agriculture
authors: Narinder Kaur, Sumanpreet Kaur, Sonia F Panesar, Prabh Deep Singh, Kiran Deep Singh, Manish Sharma
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Current farming is currently under an unprecedented challenge of satisfying the global food security needs and ensuring maximum resource use and sustainability in the environment. Internet of Things (IoT) solutions in smart agriculture can provide some promising opportunities to overcome these issues with the help of real-time monitoring of crops and decision-making based on the data. Nevertheless, the sheer amount of sensor information being generated in agricultural fields poses serious computational issues as measured by latency in processing sensor information, network bandwidth and resource utilization. The paper will present an IoT-based data processing system that has been specifically made to handle crop monitoring solutions with a three-tier system that features sensor networks, fog computing devices at farm gateways, and cloud-based analytics. We present a Crop-Aware Data Processing and Scheduling Algorithm (CDPSA) which places greater priority on time sensitive agricultural information like irrigation notifications and disease identification and causes efficient scheduling of standard monitoring activities. The framework is tested on CloudSim simulation platform in terms of various farm-scale situations. Experimental findings show that, the proposed CDPSA paradigm is 42 percent end-to-end latency reduction as compared to cloud-only solutions, 38 percent processing time reduction and 35 percent less energy consumption but offers high throughput to the agricultural decision-making process.

title: Cross-Multilingual Code-mix Analysis & Translation
authors: Jinal Patel, Archana Magare
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Language identification on social media platforms has become progressively more complex in recent years due to the utilization of multiple languages across various regions. In various social media posts, individuals predominantly utilized regions where English is neither spoken nor written, which also included instances of code-mixed text. Particularly in the WhatsApp chat conversation, informal language and a highly mixed linguistic style are evident. In this paper, we concentrate on Indian languages and introduce an integrated framework for cross-lingual code-mixed analysis and translation. Indian users commonly utilize mixed languages such as Hindi + English, Gujarati + English, Marathi + Hindi, among others, but predominantly use the combination of Hindi and English known as Hinglish. Our approach integrates code-mix identification, machine translation, sentiment analysis and name entity recognition to analyze highly informal, code-mixed and noisy data. We developed BERT-based models which utilizes IndicBERT for Indian languages, to enhance performance across diverse tasks. This study offers comprehensive evaluations of cross-multilingual processing for WhatsApp chat data, highlighting applications including digital communication analysis, sentiment analysis, and multilingual AI systems.

title: Core Components of Enterprise AI Agents
authors: Büşra Tural, Zeynep Örpek
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLM) and Generative AI (GenAI) have transformed artificial intelligence (AI) from a tool for mere analysis and prediction into AI agent structures capable of task planning, interacting with different systems, and autonomously executing specific business processes. The widespread adoption of AI tools has made the development of agent-based automation systems more technically accessible, creating significant opportunities for organizations in terms of efficiency, speed, and decision quality. These systems have the potential for use in areas such as data collection, data interpretation, process automation, decision support generation, and coordination between different enterprise applications. However, the integration of agent-based AI solutions into enterprise environments involves architectural and managerial requirements that are not limited solely to model performance. Enterprise data infrastructures and the need for integration with other enterprise information sources necessitate that AI agent applications be built on a robust and sustainable data architecture. Issues such as data security, access authorization, inter-system integration, traceability, and scalability are critical factors for the effective use of these technologies at the enterprise level. The autonomous decision-making capabilities of agent systems bring about new requirements in terms of ethics, security, and governance. The accuracy of model outputs, the protection of data privacy, the explainability of decision-making processes, and the establishment of control mechanisms are among the fundamental issues to be considered in enterprise AI applications. In particular, maintaining human oversight, increasing the reliability of AI systems, and making risks manageable are crucial for sustainable use. This study addresses the necessary architecture, data, and governance components for deploying AI agent applications in enterprise environments. AI agent structures, expected to play a significant role in enterprise digital transformation processes, will strengthen data-driven decision-making mechanisms, and the use of these components in business processes provides a framework for the success of AI Agent projects. The case study proposed in this work concretely demonstrates this approach.

title: Non-Invasive Estimation of Achilles Tendon Load in Fast Bowlers Using IMUs and Machine Learning for Tendinitis Prevention
authors: Ganga Raj, Obada Al-Khatib, Kiyan Afsari, Abeer ElKhouly
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Fast bowling in cricket is a high-impact athletic activity associated with Achilles Tendinitis due to repetitive loading of the Achilles Tendon (AT) during key motion phases. This study presents an out-of-lab, wearable tendon load monitoring system that combines burst vibration stimulation, inertial measurement units (IMUs), and machine learning to non-invasively estimate AT load during fast bowling. Five bowlers of varying skill levels performed 4 ball deliveries each, with data collected from a custom-built wearable sensor setup in the area around the AT. Signal processing and feature extraction were conducted using MATLAB, data were analyzed, and machine learning models were trained in Python to predict the tendon load proxy. The results demonstrated that professional bowlers consistently exhibited higher and biomechanically more consistent AT loads during Front Foot Contact and Ball Release phases, while recreational bowlers showed erratic patterns. Random Forest models <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}=0.996\right)$</tex> and XGBoost models <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(R^{2}=0.999\right)$</tex> yielded high accuracy, with XGBoost better identifying key AT load predictors such as phase duration, phase, and maximum amplitude. The study demonstrates the feasibility of using a low-cost setup of wearable technology to estimate the tendon load outside the lab, providing valuable information to coaches, physiotherapists, and sports science professionals in the management and monitoring of bowler performance.

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Tumor-Stroma Classification in Colorectal Cancer Pathology
authors: Aysun Yılmaz Kızılboğa, Hamza Osman Ilhan
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: In this study, five deep learning models—ResNet50, a fine-tuned version of ResNet50, MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3, and a custom-built CNN—were evaluated to assist tumorstroma ratio (TSR) assessment in histopathological images of colorectal cancer. All models were evaluated on a three-class (tumor, stroma, other) image patch-level classification problem. Performance evaluation was conducted using accuracy along with class-based precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. In addition, an ensemble strategy combining the softmax outputs of individual models was evaluated. The experimental results demonstrate that the fine-tuned ResNet50 model achieves the highest accuracy among the individual architectures. However, the proposed ensemble approach achieved the highest overall performance, with 96% accuracy and an F1-score of 0.99 for the tumor class. While MobileNet-based architectures provide well-balanced performance with reduced computational costs, the Custom CNN architecture attained high sensitivity for the tumor class, primarily due to the weighting strategy used to address class imbalance.

title: Graph Neural Network Based Spatiotemporal Traffic Prediction Framework for Smart Communication Networks
authors: Dharani R, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, Bh.Prashanthi, Roopa M, S.Agnes Shifani, Navdeep Singh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Spatiotemporal traffic prediction has become a cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems within smart communication networks. Accurate forecasting of traffic flow enables congestion mitigation, real-time routing, and dynamic resource allocation. Conventional time-series or convolution-related models may not be able to capture the non-Euclidean nature of urban road networks and the intricate time-dependent interdependencies of road traffic. This paper presents an original Graph Neural Network (GNN) architecture, named ST-GWNet +, a combination of Graph Wavelet Embeddings, Temporal Transformers, Edge Memory Fusion and Dual-Level Attention, which can be used to capture complex spatiotemporal interactions. We achieve local traversals of fine-grained traffic transition and long-range temporal dependencies of our architecture by combining spectral and temporal learning units. When evaluated on a real urban traffic dataset, ST-GWNet++ has a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 3.22, RMSE of 4.38, and an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.918, surpassing the best baselines, namely, DCRNN, STGCN, and GMAN. The model proposed is also highly interpretable and has high generalization capabilities of time horizons. The piece of work prepares the way to more adaptive and explainable traffic management solutions and provides a scalable edge deployment of smart cities.

title: Real-Time Sensor Drift Detection in Iot Edge Devices Using Lightweight Machine Learning
authors: Kumaraswamy Guralla, E. Narasimhulu, Shaik Naseema, Jonnala Nanda Kishore, M. Bharath Kumar Reddy
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Low-cost environmental sensors are widely used in Internet of Things (IoT) systems; however, their reliability degrades over time due to gradual sensor drift. Detecting such drift is challenging using traditional threshold-based methods, especially when changes occur slowly. This paper proposes a lightweight edge-based approach for real-time sensor drift detection using supervised machine learning. Temperature and humidity data collected from a DHT11 sensor are processed using statistical feature extraction techniques, and multiple classification algorithms are evaluated. The selected Decision Tree model is deployed directly on an ESP8266 microcontroller, enabling real-time local inference without cloud dependency. The model achieved 96.8% accuracy with low inference latency (18 ms), demonstrating its effectiveness and feasibility for real-time edge deployment. The results highlight the potential of lightweight machine learning models for improving sensor reliability in IoT applications.

title: An Attention-based Spatio-Temporal Deep Learning Framework for PMU-based Fault Detection in Smart Grids
authors: Shekar Ramachandran, Ayushi Khandal, Abhilash K S, Vinitha C, Rajesh K. P, Sreekanth S
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Accurate and timely fault detection is critical for confirming the reliability and resilience of modern smart grids considered by high penetration of renewable energy sources and dynamic operating conditions. Phasor Measurement Units (PMUs), with their high sampling rates and time-synchronized measurements, provide rich spatio-temporal information that can be leveraged for advanced disturbance analysis. This study proposes an AI-powered deep learning context for fault detection and classification with PMU measurements from a benchmark smart grid environment. The proposed approach integrates a Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory (ConvLSTM) network with a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to effectively capture both temporal dynamics and spatial correlations across multiple PMU locations while adaptively emphasizing fault-relevant features. PMU voltage and frequency signals from the IEEE 39-bus test system are preprocessed, temporally segmented and transformed into image-like representations using pseudo-color mapping. The ConvLSTM component models the evolution of disturbances over time, while the CBAM attention mechanism enhances discriminative feature learning through channel and spatial attention. Experimental results validate that the suggested ConvLSTM-CBAM model attains superior performance compared to conventional CNN, LSTM and baseline ConvLSTM architectures. The model attains an overall classification accuracy of 98.53% with high recall, precision and F1-scores across fault, loss of generation and synchronous motor switching events. The findings confirm the effectiveness of combining spatio-temporal modeling with attention mechanisms for reliable PMU-based fault analysis, highlighting the proposed framework’s potential for real-time smart grid monitoring and protection applications.

title: AI Agent-Assisted Lightweight Intrusion Detection for IoT Devices
authors: Doğancan Karakoç, Gökhan Dalkılıç, Mustafa Furkan Ceylan
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid increase in Internet of Things (IoT) devices has raised serious security concerns, mainly due to growing traffic volumes and more complex cyber threats. At the same time, many existing intrusion detection systems (IDS) are not well suited for resource-constrained edge environments. In this study, a lightweight IDS framework supported by an Artificial Intelligence (AI) agent is introduced for such settings. The system is built around a compact 1D-ResNet model with 28,759 parameters (approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 7 K B}$</tex>), allowing multiclass attack detection with low computational cost. Rather than focusing only on accuracy, the model is designed to operate as a fast pre-filter. It scans network traffic and identifies suspicious flows at an early stage, so that more detailed analysis can be handled by more complex models. This method is scalable and supports layered IDS designs. Proposed model trained on the CIC-IoT-2023 dataset, which has a significant class imbalance. To overcome this imbalance problem, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied to improve the detection of minority attack types. In addition, a cloud-based AI Security Analyst agent powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash converts model outputs and metadata into structured, human-readable reports. Lightweight 1D-ResNet model performed 82.84% accuracy, while keeping computational overhead low. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach is suitable for real-time edge deployment.

title: A Secure Framework for Smart Waste Management Using IoT, Machine Learning, and Blockchain
authors: Abdhisuta Dash, Bhabani Sankar Samantray, K Hemant Kumar Reddy, Fahad Amin, Sambit Kumar Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Urban areas create more solid waste which needs waste management systems that depend on advanced waste management technologies to operate effectively. The Smart Waste Management (SWM) framework integrates IoT and ML and Blockchain technologies to create a secure and scalable urban waste management system which uses data for waste management operations. IoT sensors collect real-time data on bin fill levels, environmental parameters (ammonia, methane, temperature, humidity), and geolocation. The system employs a privacy-preserving anonymization layer which protects confidential information that is stored permanently on the Hyperledger Fabric blockchain. The ML layer includes predictive models and optimization models which use Quantum-inspired Temporal Fusion Network to forecast time periods and Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning to optimize routes and Neural Architecture Search with Graph Neural Networks to adjust models in real time. The proposed framework is depicted through its architectural layers and system operational model. The experimental results show that the system maintains high reliability because it achieves transaction latencies below 100 milliseconds and success rates above 95% while decreasing both operational expenses and unnecessary resource consumption. The research shows how emerging technologies enable smart city governance to achieve sustainable operations which create a foundation for upcoming urban infrastructure development.

title: Hybrid Active-Passive Power Quality Enhancement for Power Distribution Systems
authors: Bandar Alshehri, Turki Omar Al-Shehri, Mohanna Usama Mohanna, Abdulwahab Ateeq, Zakaria Elbarbary, Saad F. Al-Gahtani, Mohammad Irshad
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Power quality degradation in low-voltage commercial and industrial electrical systems has intensified due to the increasing penetration of nonlinear and electronically controlled loads, resulting in excessive harmonic distortion, poor power factor, and voltage profile deviations. These disturbances lead to higher power losses, accelerated equipment aging, and challenges in complying with international power quality standards. This paper proposes a modular power quality enhancement system for large commercial buildings and industrial facilities, combining a shunt active power filter (SAPF) with a detuned reactorcapacitor compensation unit to simultaneously mitigate current harmonics and compensate reactive power. The proposed architecture is modeled and evaluated in MATLAB/Simulink, with real-time validation conducted using an OPAL-RT-based simulation platform. Performance assessment is carried out under multiple nonlinear load scenarios representative of commercial and industrial operating conditions, focusing on key power quality indices including total harmonic distortion (THD), power factor, and voltage profile regulation. Simulation results demonstrate a significant reduction in current THD and an improvement in power factor to near-unity operation, confirming the effectiveness, scalability, and suitability of the proposed modular solution for enhancing power quality in modern commercial and industrial low-voltage power distribution systems, thereby improving energy efficiency, reducing grid losses, and supporting climate-sustainable next-generation power grids..

title: Explainable Deep Neural Networks for Predicting Mutated Patterns in SARS-CoV-2 Variants with Geographic Analysis
authors: Varshitha Bammidi, Santhosh Amilpur, Chandra Mohan Dasari
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) is the causative agent of the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic, which is known for its high transmissibility and the development of several genetically different variants as a result of constant mutations. Because of the rapid evolution of the SARS-CoV-2 variants, we have a great need for computational tools for the classification and analysis of mutation patterns. Because of the rapid evolution of the SARS-CoV-2 variants, we have a great need for computational tools for the classification and analysis of mutation patterns. Deep learning (DL) models are the mainstay of current SARS-CoV-2 classification techniques. However, these methods facilitate variant classification and the efficient classification of pathogens. Although DL models have been widely used for large-scale mutation detection, their ability to clarify the biological significance of detected mutations is limited by the lack of explainability frameworks. The usefulness of current explainability methods for variant classification in genomic research is limited because they mainly rely on feature visualization without quantitative validation. In this work, we suggest a deep learning framework that uses convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for detailed mutation pattern analysis and SARS-CoV-2 variant classification. Our method incorporates layer-wise relevance propagation (LRP) to improve interpretability and make it easier to identify biologically significant sequence features, in contrast to traditional classification-centric models. CNN models were trained using a balanced, preprocessed dataset that included one-hot encoded genomic sequences of five major variants. The model’s effectiveness was demonstrated by its 98% classification accuracy. To find unique mutational signatures, variant-specific mutation analysis was also carried out. To investigate regional differences in mutation patterns by country, geographic analysis was carried out. The Explainability results were validated using explainability metrics, namely fidelity and sparsity. Our results show the significance of applying explainability methods on top of DL models to close the gaps in SARS-CoV-2 research.

title: AMCT: An Adaptive Minimum Completion Time Algorithm for Task Offloading in IoT-Fog-Cloud Environments
authors: Sanjaya Kumar Panda, Ayush Rana, P Navya
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) devices has generated massive volumes of compute-intensive tasks with varying requirements. However, these devices are unable to process these tasks due to their limited processing power. Therefore, they offload these tasks to nearby fog devices (FDs) or cloud datacenters (CDs) to achieve efficient performance and energy efficiency. However, it is a significant challenge to develop a task offloading algorithm that minimizes the average completion time (ACT). Recently, four task offloading algorithms, namely global gateway (GG) with first-come first served (FCFS) and cooperation (referred to as GGFC), GG with FCFS and no cooperation (GGFNC), GG with random selection (RS) and cooperation (GGRC), and GG with RS and no cooperation (GGRNC), were developed to achieve efficient performance and energy efficiency. This paper presents an adaptive minimum completion time (AMCT) task offloading algorithm for the three-tier IoT-fog-cloud environments. AMCT hierarchically distributes tasks either to FDs based on a score value or to the CDs, ensuring the MCT. The performance of the AMCT algorithm is evaluated using a simulation setup comprising six FDs and two CDs, which involves 50000 tasks. Simulation results show that the AMCT algorithm outperforms GGFC, GGFNC, GGRC, and GGRNC algorithms by reducing the ACT, average transmission time, and energy consumption by up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48.81 \%, 57.10 \%$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 5. 2 0 \%}$</tex>, respectively, demonstrating its applicability in IoT-fog-cloud environments.

title: Environmental and Social Risk Management for Dam Decommissioning: A Mixed-Methods Case Study
authors: Mohammed Alsharqawi, Siman Imran Khan, Athira Kudilil Shaji, Aiswarya Shylajan, Santo Sunny
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Decommissioning large dams brings a tangle of environmental, social, and regulatory risks, and the Mullaperiyar Dam in Kerala, India (built in 1895 and now flagged for aging fabric, seismic exposure, and downstream hazards) offers a timely test of Environmental and Social Risk Management (ESRM). This research analyzed the case with a mixed-methods approach: reviewing environmental impact assessments (EIA) and regulatory filings; compiling baseline biophysical and socioeconomic data with GIS/remote sensing; conducting risk assessment and dam-break analysis; and running a structured engagement program that included two public consultations (April 15 and June 10, 2022) and a perception survey. Flooding, habitat disruption, and community displacement emerged as the highest-impact risks, while sediment management (rated lower in probability) still proved consequential for downstream conditions. By 2023, implementation tracking showed strong progress in habitat restoration <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 80 {\%})$</tex> and community reallocation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 60 {\%})$</tex>, moderate gains in flood-risk reduction <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{5 0 {\%}})$</tex>, and lagging sediment control <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 35 {\%})$</tex>. Stakeholder feedback was mostly positive (about 65%), but it also underscored ongoing concerns about accountability and long-term livelihoods. The main takeaways are practical: embed adaptive management with clear monitoring triggers; accelerate sediment routing and controls; and institutionalize co-monitoring, open data, and third-party audits, especially to strengthen Kerala-Tamil Nadu coordination. The study's limits include its single-case design, reliance on project documents and self-reported perceptions, and interim outcome metrics. Even so, the case shows how proactive, inclusive ESRM can improve trust, reduce harm, and guide measurable mitigation, linking compensation and ecosystem restoration to transparent reporting and revisiting risk weightings when “lowlikelihood” issues, like sediment, persistently hinder delivery.

title: WaveFusionML: Multi-Resolution Learning for Electromagnetic Field-Aware Antenna Design
authors: Vinoth M
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: WaveFusionML proposes a multi-resolution learning framework for the electromagnetic field-aware antenna design that unites physics-informed representations with data-driven optimization. The method put forward breaks down the near and far-field electromagnetic responses at different spatial and frequency scales, which not only makes it possible to learn complex field interactions more efficiently but also keeps the model physically consistent. WaveFusionML, by combining global field patterns that are of low resolution with local features that are high resolution, thus fine-tuned, facilitates faster design convergence and raises the predictive accuracy of antenna performance metrics. Numerical tests on standard antenna geometries have shown that the approach presented in the paper can lead to an average 12.6% increase in radiation efficiency compared to conventional single-resolution neural design models. Moreover, the optimization scheme results in a 38.4% saving of overall computational time while the error in far-field gain prediction across the frequency band tested remains below 2.1%. The findings of this study demonstrate that multi-resolution electromagnetic learning has the potential to considerably improve both the capability and the productivity of the antenna design processing pipelines of the future. The reduction in computational burden and enhancement in radiation efficiency contribute toward energy-efficient wireless system design, supports objectives for sustainable energy usage in low - power digital technologies.

title: Particle Swarm Optimized PI Control of a PV-Fed DC–DC Boost Converter for High-Performance Renewable Charging Systems
authors: R.Anitha, Gaurav Londhe, R.Tamilamuthan, T.Sridevi, M.Lakshmanan, Shreyas Rajendra Hole
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The growing integration of PV energy sources into renewable charging systems mandates effective and robust power conversion techniques for intermittent and nonlinear power stages. The DC-DC boost converters are used overwhelmingly to regulate the variable PV panel's voltage levels. However, the proportional integral (PI) controller configuration has had its own problems of slow transient response, overshoot, and noisy performance under irradiance and load variations. This range opened up fresh avenues in the context of PI tuning for the boost converter in renewables. So, in this paper, the authors proposed PSO-based PI controllers in the task of enhancing these renewable-based boost converter voltage regulation, stability, and overall system efficiency. In the presented method, the PSO algorithm was employed for the optimal tuning of the PI controller through a minimization of the multi-objective fitness function, significantly utilizing time-weighted voltage error considerations and overshoot controls. The average model of the boost converter was derived, and the optimized PI controller was then applied to regulate the duty cycle to bring, under varying operating conditions, a constant quality output voltage. The control strategy was taken up and evaluated using MATLAB simulations based on actual converter and load parameters. Simulation results portray an almost harmingly improved dynamic performance of the PSO-PI controller, registering rise time reduction to about 0.021 s and a settling time of 0.038 s as compared to 0.045 s and 0.085 s with a conventional PI controller. Voltage overshoot has substantially been reduced from 9.6% to 1.8%, and the steady-state error decreased from 0.62 V to 0.08 V. Even more, the converter efficiency has been throughly improved from 89.4% to 95.2%. These results prove that the optimization-based control system results in a high performance to the solar-based renewable charging system.

title: Optimizing Latency, Throughput, and Accuracy in Large-Scale Messaging Applications Through Advanced Data Engineering Techniques
authors: Deepak Venkateshappa
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Large-scale messaging systems constitute a core component of modern data-driven applications, enabling reliable and asynchronous communication among distributed producers and consumers. Such systems are crucial for real-time platforms including social networks, cloud services, financial systems, and Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructures, where severe latency bounds and sustained high throughput are required to ensure service responsiveness and user satisfaction. However, traditional optimization approaches primarily rely on static configuration, reactive scaling, and rule-based resource management. These methods often respond only after performance degradation occurs and are unable to adapt effectively to bursty and rapidly changing workloads, resulting in queue buildup, unstable latency, and inefficient resource utilization. This study introduces a data engineering-driven distributed streaming architecture that integrates Apache Kafka for scalable message ingestion, Apache Spark Structured Streaming for stream-based processing and feature extraction, and a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) for short-term traffic forecasting. A publicly available dataset consisting of one million Reddit comments is utilized as the input data source. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture sustains throughput up to 65,000 messages per second while maintaining average end-to-end latency below 150ms under high concurrency. Furthermore, the TCN model achieves a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.29 and a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.41 on normalized traffic rates, indicating outperforming short-term forecasting ability. The proposed approach lies in its ability to combine scalable stream processing with predictive workload awareness, enabling proactive performance management and improved stability. These findings confirm the effectiveness of advanced data engineering techniques for optimizing latency, throughput, and reliability in large-scale messaging applications.

title: Cloud-Based Big Data Platform for Electric Vehicle Battery Degradation Analysis
authors: S P Vimal, C Srinivasan, S Padmapriya, Ayalapogu Ratna Raju, Mohan Krishna Seerla, Elankurisil Arasan
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: The efficiency and condition of the batteries in an electric vehicle (EV) significantly affects the vehicle's performance and dependability. However, EVs gradually degrade due to constant use, temperature variations, and charging cycles, which reduces their overall performance and lifetime. In this study, we provide a big data platform that runs on clouds and can track, analyze, and forecast when batteries will die. Power, current, temperature, and state of charge (SOC) are just a few of the operational data that the system gathers and processes using Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, cloud computing, and advanced data analytics. The platform analyses data from machine learning algorithms to determine the battery's health, identify trends of early deterioration, and estimate its remaining usable life (RUL). Users, fleet managers, and manufacturers all benefit from the cloud's scalable storage, lightning-fast computing, and remote access. This method encourages the sustainable functioning of EVs by enhancing battery management, decreasing maintenance costs, increasing energy efficiency, and promoting smart, data-driven decision-making.

title: Performance Evaluation of an Efficient Wearable Antenna for Biomedical Applications
authors: Shamil H. Hussein Alnajjar, Shahad M. Khalil
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Wearable antennas play a crucial role in continuous health monitoring, wireless data transmission, and body-worn communication systems. However, their performance is often limited by interaction with the human body, signal attenuation, and design constraints. In this work, a conventional MSPA antenna was compared with a proposed design incorporating square-shaped SRR-EBG metamaterial (MTM) unit cells operating at 2.4 GHz. The integration of the EBG structure significantly improved the antenna's electromagnetic performance, radiation stability, and safety near human tissue. The proposed design achieved stable resonance at 2.4 GHz with a reflection coefficient of less than - 21 dB, a peak gain of approximately 4.96 dB, and a high efficiency greater than 70%. Furthermore, the specific absorption rate (SAR) value decreased significantly from 1.96 W/kg (without EBG) to 0.0935 W/kg (with 8 MTM SRR-EBG squares), ensuring compliance with IEEE and ICNIRP exposure limits. Finally, the improved MSPA based EBG configuration demonstrates a compact, efficient, and electromagnetically safe solution, suitable for wearable biomedical sensors and wireless body-wide network (WBAN) applications.

title: Papaya Leaf Nutrient Deficiency Classification Using Transfer Learning based Deep Learning Models
authors: Ajahar Pathan, Swati Pandey
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Nutrient deficiency significantly affects papaya crop growth and yield, making early and accurate identification essential for effective crop management. Traditional nutrient assessment methods are often time-consuming and require expert intervention, motivating the need for automated image-based solutions. This study presents a deep learning–based framework for multi-class classification of papaya leaf nutrient deficiency conditions using leaf images. A convolutional neural network trained from scratch is first employed as a baseline model, followed by the application of multiple transfer learning architectures, including VGG19, Xception, InceptionV3, and DenseNet201, to enhance classification performance under limited data conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that transfer learning models outperform the baseline CNN, with InceptionV3 achieving the highest test accuracy of 90%. The findings indicate that pretrained deep learning models are effective in capturing discriminative visual patterns related to nutrient deficiencies and can support reliable automated papaya leaf nutrient deficiency detection.

title: Research on Target Centroid Prediction of Low-Resolution Images Based on Improved PSO-SE-ResNet Network
authors: Weizheng Wang, Huawei Liu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: In response to the difficulties in identifying the centroids of targets in low-resolution images and the problem that the setting of hyperparameters in the traditional SE-ResNet network relies on empirical values, this paper first adopts an edge extraction method that combines a multi-color space conversion strategy, the K-means clustering algorithm, multi-scale Canny edge detection, and comprehensive contour scoring to extract the contours of bird targets and applies it to the calculation of the initial centroid coordinates. Then, various random data augmentation methods are used to address issues such as the insufficient number of training sets and further enhance the robustness and generalization performance of subsequent network training. Finally, a PSO-SE-ResNet network based on particle swarm optimization for hyperparameters of the squeeze-excitation residual network is proposed, enabling the network to be trained under optimal hyperparameters and obtain a complex mapping network between low-resolution bird images and normalized centroid coordinates. The test results show that the final score of the edge contours of the bird targets used is not less than 0.84, and the fluctuation of the final test score does not exceed 0.037. The optimization results of the PSO-SE-ResNet network for the initial learning rate, learning rate decay factor, learning rate decay period, batch size, and number of epochs are 4.08×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup>, 0.9, 11, 22, and 86, respectively. In addition, the total RMSE value of the normalized centroid position prediction of the PSO-SE-ResNet network is 0.0758, demonstrating its good prediction performance and application potential in accurately predicting the centroids of bird targets in low-resolution images.

title: Water Surface Cleaning Robot with Alert Notification And Obstacle Avoidance
authors: Sathana Keerthi R, Dhivya J, Karthika V, Akalya Devi G
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This work introduces an autonomous water surface cleaning robot designed to tackle the growing problem of floating waste in water bodies. The robot moves in a predefined zigzag pattern to ensure systematic coverage of the surface. When an obstacle is detected, it automatically reverses and resumes its path without external control. A conveyor belt mechanism is used to extract debris efficiently and deposit it into an onboard dustbin. To reduce manual intervention, the robot is also equipped with a monitoring system that sends an alert to the user if the dustbin is full or when the battery level drops below 20%. With these combined features, the system provides a reliable, low-cost, and eco-friendly solution for maintaining cleaner lakes, ponds, and reservoirs. Prototype testing shows that robots can operate effectively in real conditions, making it a promising tool for sustainable water management and environmental protection.

title: Social Entrepreneurship in Africa: Bridging Cultural Values and Sustainable Solutions
authors: Emmanuel Ahatsi, Joseph Akpan, Oludolapo Olanrewaju
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper explores the African perspective on social entrepreneurship and its strategies for sustainable development, critiquing Western models that overlook the continent's unique contexts. Through an integrative literature review, the study shows that African social entrepreneurs are influenced by personal experiences and cultural values, such as Ubuntu, rather than solely by prosocial incentives. It underscores the importance of bricolage in overcoming resource constraints and harnessing community knowledge. Successful examples discussed include M-Pesa, myAgro, and Solar Sister, which exemplify the efforts of social entrepreneurs to address various social issues. This work enhances global social entrepreneurship theory by emphasizing the roles of institutional arbitrage, community engagement, and relational capital in developing hybrid solutions that integrate both formal and informal sectors in Africa.

title: Hybrid Energy Storage Modeling for Energy Efficient Electric Public Transportation Considering Power Converter Losses
authors: Henar Mike O. Canilang, Yurim Cheon, Youjeong Yoon, Wansu Lim
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid energy storage system (HESS) modeling framework for hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs) that integrates battery and supercapacitor (SC) dynamics. The modeling framework considers the losses of the DC-DC converter with respect to the vehicle model. Unlike conventional approaches that assume ideal power converters, the proposed model includes conduction and switching losses of the DC-DC converter, enabling realistic evaluation of energy efficiency and power sharing behavior in HEVs. The model is evaluated under representative urban driving conditions with frequent acceleration and regenerative braking, reflecting typical HEV operation. The proposed model provides a practical platform for energy storage system (ESS) design and hybrid energy management studies in sustainable transportation electrification.

title: Hybrid PLC-Decentralized Control for Adaptive Load Balancing in Multi-Conveyor Systems: An Experimental Study
authors: Vladimir Hristov, Ivan Chekurov, Ivan Shopov
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and experimental evaluation of a hybrid adaptive load-balancing system for a multi-conveyor network, integrating a central Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) with decentralized motor-driven roller (MDR) intelligence. Unlike conventional systems that rely either on centralized or distributed control exclusively, this hybrid architecture leverages the strengths of both paradigms: realtime decision-making from the PLC and local autonomous behavior from intelligent MDR zones. A cost-effective object classification method using a single photoelectric sensor is implemented. The system is experimentally validated on a laboratory testbed using a Siemens S7-1200 PLC programmed with Ladder Diagram (LD) and Structured Control Language (SCL). Experimental results demonstrate 94.7 % sorting accuracy, which is comparable to industrial vision-based systems (94 %) reported in recent literature, while using significantly simpler hardware. A comparative analysis with alternative approaches-including vision-based systems, cloudcontrolled architectures, and autonomous decentralized control -highlights the trade-offs between accuracy, complexity, cost, and scalability.

title: Design of Adaptive RF Front-End Circuits for Resilient Wireless Communication in Dynamic Environments
authors: Lalit Sachdeva, Utkarsh Anand
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Traditional Radio Frequency (RF) front-end circuits utilize fixed parameters that often fail to maintain performance in dynamic wireless environments characterized by signal fading, interference, and high mobility. This paper presents the design and hardware verification of an adaptive RF front-end architecture engineered for enhanced communication resilience. The proposed system integrates a unified Dynamic Control Unit that orchestrates Automatic Gain Control (AGC), frequency hopping, programmable filtering, and beamforming in real-time. The methodology involved a four-phase approach: mathematical modeling, SDR-based prototyping using the NI USRP-2954 platform, and field testing across a 50-meter range. Experimental results demonstrate that the adaptive circuit significantly outperforms traditional fixed systems, achieving a Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) of 28.6 dB compared to 16.4 dB for static designs. Furthermore, the system maintained a low Bit Error Rate (BER) of 0.001 and a throughput of 120 Mbps, representing a 60% improvement over standard configurations under 40% interference saturation. Crucially, the architecture reduced system latency to 10 ms, a 44% improvement essential for time-critical applications. While real-time adaptation increased power consumption to 95 mW, the performance-to-power trade-off remains optimal for battery-constrained IoT and autonomous vehicular networks. These findings provide a scalable foundation for 5G and 6G infrastructures. Future work will explore the integration of machine learning algorithms to further refine predictive adaptability and energy efficiency in complex spectral environments.

title: Automated Collision Proxy Reconstruction for Non-Manifold Meshes by Multi-Directional Ray Casting
authors: Xiaojiang Su, Ping Wei, Yaqin Yu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Generating volumetric collision proxies for 3D models is essential in video game development and visual effects production, enabling efficient physics simulations such as spacecraft collisions and debris interactions. Traditional volumetric methods, including Signed Distance Fields (SDF) and OpenVDB-based approaches, require input meshes to be watertight (closed and manifold), imposing significant preprocessing burdens on production assets that are typically non-manifold and contain topological defects. This paper presents a novel algorithm that bypasses these requirements by employing multi-axis ray casting combined with Boolean intersection operations. Our method projects rays from three mutually perpendicular directions, determines solid intervals based on extreme intersection points, and computes the volumetric intersection to reconstruct an accurate collision proxy. Experimental analysis demonstrates that this approach is robust to mesh defects, fully automated, and capable of preserving concave surface details, offering a practical solution for real-time collision detection in production environments.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Models for Comparing Teacher Effectiveness and Student Engagement Across Schools
authors: Swati Das, Manjusha Dubey Tiwari, Dhara Ben, Shraddha Verma, Satish Kumar Sahu, Parul Dubey
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: Preservice teacher efficacy and student perceived engagement are vital predictors of quality education and learning outcomes. Traditional assessment methods are still largely subjective and penalizes students based on differences between urban and rural schools. We build a multimodal dataset from 20 schools in Chhattisgarh, India with data for 60 teachers, 1200 students, ~200 hours of classroom recordings, lecture transcripts, surveys and academic performance data. We propose a hybrid deep learning framework with Natural Language Processing using BERT for text-based analysis, Computer Vision using CNN+LSTM for behavioral analysis, and predictive modeling using Random Forest and Gradient Boosting. In particular, our main contribution is the combination of these multimodal approaches to a medium level in a common framework to measure classroom performance as well as student involvement. Model performance was evaluated through accuracy, F1-score, RMSE and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and then effect sizes were evaluated through statistical tests. Results show urban schools are more effective (p < 005) in achieving higher levels of engagement in teaching and learning practiced in rural schools. Also, teaching quality and engagement are better related to academic performance in urban contexts, whereas rural outcomes are limited by geographuc isolation (p.4). These results highlight the promise of multimodal AI frameworks for supporting evidence-based education policy, and precision interventions.

title: An Integrated Framework for Real-Time Phishing Website Detection using XGBoost and BERT
authors: G. Lalitha Kumari, K. Koteswara Rao, K. Bhavana, I. Sri Naga Venkata Raja, K. Venkateswara Rao, Mogal Safiya
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Phishing websites remain a major cybersecurity threat, exploiting technical vulnerabilities and human trust to steal sensitive data such as login credentials and financial information. Existing website security systems commonly rely on predefined rules, stored patterns, and trained models to identify malicious activities. While these approaches provide fast detection, they can be further strengthened by incorporating intelligent analysis techniques.In this work, a hybrid website safety detection system is proposed using machine learning and natural language processing techniques. The system utilizes a trained XGBoost classifier to analyze structural URL features and a BERT-based language model to examine webpage content for phishing indicators. The outputs of both modules are combined to generate a final risk assessment. The proposed framework is implemented as a real-time backend service and integrated with a Chrome browser extension, enabling efficient and reliable website safety verification.

title: Federated Reinforcement Learning with Blockchain-Integrated Trust for Secure Routing in 5G-Enabled Vehicular Networks
authors: M.Sri Lakshmi, N Basavaraju, Shaik Khadeer Basha, Telugu Johitesh, Yerukula Surya Sumanth, M. JayaSunitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Secure and low-latency routing in 5G-enabled Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) is fundamentally challenged by high node mobility, dynamic topology changes, and the threat of malicious routing behaviour. Existing protocols including AODV and standalone reinforcement learning (RL) approaches fail to jointly address privacy-preserving distributed learning, trust-based route verification, and real-time adaptability. This paper proposes the Federated Reinforcement Learning-based Secure Routing (FRL-SR) framework, which integrates decentralized Q-learning at vehicular agents, weighted federated aggregation at roadside unit (RSU) edge servers, and a blockchain-based trust verification layer operating on PBFT consensus. Vehicles learn local routing policies from neighborhood observations without sharing raw data; RSUs periodically aggregate global models via data-weighted FedAvg; and every routing update is validated through blockchain transactions recording node ID, model hash, trust score, and digital signature. Extensive simulations on NS-3.39 with SUMO mobility and 5G NR-V2X communication demonstrate that FRL-SR achieves a mean Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) of 94.0%, an average End-to-End Delay (E2E) of 42 ms, a Trust Violation Rate (TVR) below 1.5%, and model convergence in 14 federated rounds outperforming AODV, RL-Only, and FedRL baselines by 18–22%, 28–35%, and 23%, respectively. The results confirm the viability of FRL-SR as a scalable, secure, and latency-optimal routing solution for next-generation intelligent transportation systems.

title: A Machine Learning Approach Based on Combined Feature Ranking and Targeted Ablation in Early-Stage Diabetes Risk Prediction
authors: Nurullah Öztürk, Sermin Güngör
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Diabetes is a rapidly increasing chronic disease worldwide. Early diagnosis of diabetes is crucial for preventing complications. This study presents a machine learning framework that integrates feature selection, classification, and interpretability analysis for early-stage diabetes risk prediction using the EarlyStage Diabetes Risk Prediction dataset. In the preprocessing phase, categorical variables were quantified, training-test separation was performed in a stratified manner, class imbalance was addressed only in the training layers using SMOTE, and standardization was applied for scale-sensitive models. Feature evaluation was performed using Random Forest significance scores, the ChiSquare test, and Mutual Information measures; a combined feature ranking based on the consensus of these three approaches was created to reduce bias from a single method. Under this ranking, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbours, Logistic Regression, and Naive Bayes models were compared. According to the report results, tree-based ensemble models performed the strongest; specifically, the Random Forest baseline scenario achieved an F1 score of approximately 0.99, while the joint ranking scenario reached a level of 1,000. Additionally, targeted ablation analyses showed that performance systematically decreased when dominant symptoms such as polydipsia, polyuria, and sudden weight loss were removed. Feature count analysis indicated that a level of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k=12$</tex> offered a balanced practical optimum between performance, simplicity, and generalizability. The study proposes a low-dimensional, understandable, and portable decision-support framework for early-stage diabetes prediction. The findings demonstrate that feature selection plays a critical role in machine learning studies conducted on health data and that reliable diabetes prediction models can be developed using a limited number of distinctive features.

title: Revenue Management and Operational Efficiency in Aviation Enterprises: A Categorical Analysis of Contemporary Literature (2023-2026)
authors: Pırıl Tekİn, Fatma Şeyma Yüksel
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The aviation industry is undergoing a dynamic transformation driven by digitalization and evolving passenger behaviors. This study provides a systematic literature review of various contemporary studies published between 2023 and 2026, focusing on revenue management, artificial intelligence integration, and operational efficiency in aviation. The analysis categorizes the literature into three primary domains: “Algorithmic Transformation,” “Strategic Decision Support,” and “Ecosystem Efficiency.” The study reveals that Agentic Artificial Intelligence (Agentic AI) and hybrid modeling approaches will serve as the future cornerstones of the industry.

title: AI-Driven Data Analysis Pipelines for Enterprise Data Engineering
authors: Sailaja Songa, Govinda RaviRaj Mulasa, Santhosh Deep Moturi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The increasing scale and operational complexity of enterprise data environments demand structured and scalable approaches for automated analytical processing. This paper proposes a comprehensive software engineering framework for AI-driven data analysis pipelines tailored for large-scale data science and enterprise data engineering systems. The framework organizes enterprise data sources, ingestion and validation, preprocessing and data management, feature transformation, hybrid modeling and evaluation within a modular and governed pipeline architecture that supports traceable and reproducible execution. Trip-level records from the New York City Taxi Trip Duration dataset were processed through these layered stages to produce consistent temporal-spatial analytical representations. Within the modeling layer, a Temporal Convolutional Network learns sequential dependencies and temporal dynamics from chronologically ordered trip data, and the learned representations are subsequently refined using a Gradient Boosting regressor to capture nonlinear relationships and improve predictive accuracy. Experimental evaluation using standard regression metrics demonstrates that the integrated architecture achieves superior predictive performance and stable convergence compared with individual models. The framework is termed comprehensive because it integrates data engineering, feature transformation, AI-driven modeling, evaluation, governance, and lifecycle control within a unified software engineering architecture. The results confirm that embedding AI-driven modeling within a structured software engineering pipeline enhances analytical reliability, scalability and governance, thereby enabling dependable and enterprise-ready data analysis workflows for high-volume data environments.

title: Spatio-Temporal Machine Learning Framework for Dengue Incidence Rate Forecasting Using National Health Surveillance Data
authors: Anastasia Mia Martalia, Saiful Akbar
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever (DHF) remains a persistent public health challenge in tropical countries, including Indonesia, due to its complex temporal dynamics, spatial heterogeneity, and frequent data sparsity at fine administrative scales. Accurate and timely forecasting of dengue risk is therefore essential for early warning systems and targeted intervention planning. This study proposes a spatio-temporal machine learning framework for forecasting the Dengue Incidence Rate (DIR) using national disease surveillance data from the Ministry of Health of the Republic of Indonesia (Kemenkes RI), covering the period 2005-2024 across 119 districts and municipalities in 6 provinces on Java Island, Indonesia. To address zero inflation and population heterogeneity inherent in health surveillance records, dengue incidence is modeled using DIR, which normalizes reported cases by population size. The proposed framework integrates temporal features (lagged values, rolling statistics, and seasonal components), spatial dependency features derived from inversedistance weighted spatial lags, population-based covariates, and outbreak-aware regime features designed to emphasize high-risk periods. Multiple predictive models including CatBoost, Support Vector Machines (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, and a Stacking Ensemble are evaluated under temporal-only, spatial-only, and spatio-temporal configurations using a time-series cross-validation scheme. Experimental results demonstrate that the spatio-temporal Stacking Ensemble achieves the best overall performance, with RMSE of 0.301, MAE of 0.224, and Spike RMSE of 0.397. Feature importance analysis reveals that outbreak-aware spatial lag interactions, autoregressive incidence history, and local outbreak indicators dominate predictive performance. Furthermore, Moran's I analysis reveals weak positive global spatial autocorrelation (mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I}=\mathbf{0. 0 8 1}$</tex>), suggesting that spatial clustering effects are localized and outbreak-conditioned rather than globally persistent. These findings highlight the effectiveness of Stacking Ensemble model combined with outbreak-aware spatio-temporal features for dengue forecasting using national health surveillance data.

title: SNR Prediction and SNR-Aware Optimization based on Artificial Neural Networks in Wireless Communications: A Review
authors: Mustafa Qasim, Cemal Kavalcioğlu, Samuel Nii Tackie, Kamil Dimililer
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper offers a systematic review of research examining the application of neural networks and deep learning methodologies for estimating and predicting the signal-to-noise ratio/interference (SNR/SINR) in contemporary wireless communication systems, specifically emphasizing dynamic 5G and 6G contexts marked by intricate operating conditions, including interference, multipath, and the Doppler effect. The analyzed literature was classified according to input representation types, encompassing I/Q signals, channel state information (CSI), spectral power density (PSD), and spectral diagrams, in addition to contextual and ambient attributes. The categorization was based on the employed model architectures, including convolutional neural networks, LSTM and GRU models, CNN-LSTM hybrid architectures, and lightweight models appropriate for edge deployment. The review findings indicate that deep learning models exhibit a significant capacity to discern intricate nonlinear correlations in wireless channel data, allowing for more reliable estimations and forecasts than conventional methods that depend on extensive measurements. These models are crucial for facilitating various operational applications, including the management of sophisticated communication systems, scheduling, alleviating overload, and enhancing spectrum use efficiency. Nonetheless, other academic and practical obstacles persist, namely restricted generalizability across diverse contexts and devices, data drift, latency and computational resource limitations, as well as the absence of standardized datasets pertinent to the domain. The document advocates for the standardization of assessment methodologies and the creation of lightweight, uncertainty-averse models via closed-loop testing.

title: Two-Stage Nonlinear Torque Observer-Based Robust Backstepping Speed Control for IPMSM Drives
authors: Moustafa Hassanein, Fatih Adıgüzel
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper proposes a unified observer-controller framework for Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (IPMSM) drives to achieve high-accuracy speed regulation under unknown time-varying load torque. A two-stage Nonlinear Extended State Observer (NL-ESO) framework is developed in the mechanical channel: the first stage reconstructs the lumped disturbance and load torque, while an auxiliary nonlinear differentiator provides a filtered estimate of the reconstructed load torque derivative for implementation completeness. The speed and current loops are closed using a Lyapunov-based integral backstepping controller within a cascaded structure. Closed-loop stability is analyzed using a composite Lyapunov function combined with Input-to-State Stability (ISS)-type characterizations of both observer and differentiator errors, establishing uniform ultimate boundedness (UUB) under bounded uncertainty. MATLAB/Simulink results confirm accurate speed tracking, strong disturbance rejection against sudden load changes, and feasible voltage/current demands under SVPWM limits.

title: Large Language Model Guided Reinforcement Learning for Heat Pump Control in Smart Buildings
authors: Gökhan Demirel, Natascha Fernengel, Hallah Butt, Natalie Rapp, Kevin Förderer, Veit Hagenmeyer
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Heat pumps are essential for the decarbonization of the heating sector but often operate inefficiently due to volatile electricity prices, forecast uncertainties, and diverse comfort preferences. Traditional rule- and model-based controllers ensure stable operation but lack adaptability under dynamic conditions, while data-driven reinforcement learning (RL) provides decisionmaking flexibility at the cost of interpretability and transparency. This paper presents a hybrid high-level control framework in which a large language model (LLM) serves as a metapolicy, supervising a portfolio of RL-based low-level controllers for residential heat pump operation, formulated as a partially observable Markov decision process (POMDP). The LLM processes structured JSON-formatted prompts containing building states, short-horizon forecasts, candidate actions, and one-step rewards, and outputs either a discrete controller selection (guided mode) or convex weights for action fusion (weighted mode). Benchmark experiments in a realistic building simulation testbed compare four state-of-the-art LLMs (Mistral-7B, Llama-3.1-8B, DeepSeek-Chat, and GPT-4.1) against standalone RL (PPO, SAC, DDPG, and A2C). A two-dimensional decoding sensitivity analysis identifies balanced stochastic decoding hyperparameter regions (temperature and nucleus sampling) that prevent response errors, thereby stabilizing control performance. Results show that LLM-guided RL achieves up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 1 \%}$</tex> higher mean reward over the best standalone RL controller.

title: A Sustainable Embedded Monitoring Architecture of Real-Time Electric Leakage Detection and Automated Subchannel Remote Alerts
authors: Kaviyarasu P, Saravanan K, P. Nisha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: We have shown a smart embedded architecture, where online monitoring is to identify, and monitor the faults of current leakage in the electric system. A framework of an Arduino UNO microcontroller, voltage and current acquisition modules is introduced in this work to dynamically measure the working parameters. The internal circuit will be able to raise an acoustic alert and light an LED interface to indicate any over-threshold alteration in current flow and respond to it locally in real-time. Meanwhile, a GSM communication unit transmits automated alerts to remote operators that can go on with timely preventative measures. With a LoRa communication connection added, it becomes even more convenient to access the wider range of data at greater distances when using it in monitoring applications. Power autonomy is achieved through a photovoltaic power generator that includes in-built storage, and is structured towards long-term working conditions in off-grid and emergency conditions. A DC/DC converter provides a power source that is a static energy source to sensors and microcontrollers. This architecture emphasizes on miniaturization, scalability and cost that enables the usage of the architecture in household networks up to industrial plant and commercial buildings. The research is based on the premise that incorporation of renewable energy consumption in smart cities and proper leakage detection will save energy, protect fragile electrical devices and fire risks. The given arrangement contributes to the development of electrical safety systems as an independent, quick reaction, cost-efficient intrusion detection system of leakage, enhanced by effective communication and real-time intelligence.

title: Unregistered Spectral Image Fusion: Unmixing, Adversarial Learning, and Recoverability_supp1-3698775.pdf
authors: Xiao Fu
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper addresses the fusion of a pair of spatially unregistered hyperspectral image (HSI) and multispectral image (MSI) covering roughly overlapping regions. HSIs offer high spectral but low spatial resolution, while MSIs provide the opposite. The goal is to integrate their complementary information to enhance both HSI spatial resolution and MSI spectral resolution. While hyperspectral-multispectral fusion (HMF) has been widely studied, the unregistered setting remains challenging. Many existing methods focus solely on MSI super-resolution, leaving HSI unchanged. Supervised deep learning approaches were proposed for HSI super-resolution, but rely on accurate training data, which is often unavailable. Moreover, theoretical analyses largely address the co-registered case, leaving unregistered HMF poorly understood. In this work, an unsupervised framework is proposed to simultaneously super-resolve both MSI and HSI. The method integrates coupled spectral unmixing for MSI super-resolution with latent-space adversarial learning for HSI superresolution. Theoretical guarantees on the recoverability of the super-resolution MSI and HSI are established under reasonable generative models—providing, to our best knowledge, the first such insights for unregistered HMF. The approach is validated on semi-real and real HSI-MSI pairs across diverse conditions.

title: MoonPoly: Bridging Code Generation and Adaptive Execution via Micro-Kernel Polymerization for Optimizing Dynamic-Shape Tensor Operators
authors: Yangyu Zhang, Guangli Li, Feng Yu, Fan Luo, Qianqi Sun, Xueying Wang, Jiacheng Zhao, Huimin Cui, Xiaobing Feng, Jingling Xue

authors: Shalini Agarwal, Hemant Kumar Singh, Manoj Kumar Sah, Yogesh Kumar Meena, Ashish Tiwari, Vedant Sharma

title: Collaborating with AI in Programming Education: A Qualitative Study of Student Experiences with a Chatbot-Supported Learning Environment
authors: Mohammad Amin Kuhail, Ahmed Shuhaiber, Sinan Salman, Nazik Alturki
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Introductory programming education is often experienced as uncertain and demanding, especially during debugging and problem-solving. This study qualitatively explores students' experiences with an AI-supported collaborative coding environment that integrates shared code editing, peer discussion, and an LLM (GPT-4) providing concise hints rather than full solutions. After completing programming tasks, 102 undergraduate students shared open-ended reflections, which were analyzed thematically. Students largely described the environment as supportive, highlighting how immediate AI assistance within a shared workspace helped reduce frustration and maintain momentum. The chatbot was often perceived as a facilitator of discussion that clarified misunderstandings and complemented peer interaction. Participants also noted limitations, including occasional unclear responses, challenges in effective prompting, and minor interface issues. Overall, the findings suggest that AI can serve as a complementary scaffold in collaborative programming learning environments, shaping students' engagement and sensemaking during complex tasks.
