title: DMGS-SLAM: A Monocular Gaussian SLAM Method with Dynamic Window Optimization and Depth Priors
authors: Jingwen Liu, Tao Zuo
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Three-Dimensional (3D) Gaussian Splatting shows great promise in the field of visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) due to its efficient rendering speed and outstanding scene representation capability. However, several challenges remain when deploying 3D Gaussian Splatting in a monocular system. First, continuous updating with new observations leads to overwriting of previously estimated Gaussians, gradually degrading the map's long-term consistency. Second, unreliable monocular depth cues cause unstable Gaussian initialization, resulting in scale ambiguity and structural distortion. To address these challenges, we propose DMGS-SLAM (Dynamic Window Optimization and Monocular Depth Priors-based Gaussian Simultaneous Localization and Mapping). Our system uses a dynamic keyframe window optimization that jointly optimizes the current frame, co-visible keyframes, and a subset of past frames, preventing long-term drift while maintaining realtime performance. Additionally, we integrate UniDepth, a monocular depth estimation network, to provide dense depth priors. Extensive experiments on the TUM RGB-D dataset (Technical University of Munich RGB-D dataset) and Replica dataset demonstrate that DMGS-SLAM achieves superior performance. Specifically, our method reduces the root mean square error (RMSE) of absolute trajectory error (ATE)from 1.618 cm to 1.406 cm on TUM and from 0.370 cm to 0.293 cm on Replica, corresponding to relative improvements of 13.1 % and 20.8 %, respectively. rendering quality improves substantially compared to MonoGS (a monocular Gaussian Splatting-based SLAM method) and SplaTAM (a real-time Gaussian Splatting SLAM system), with Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) increasing from 20.166 dB and 19.337 dB to 27.035 dB on average, respectively. These results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly enhances tracking accuracy, robustness, and 3D reconstruction quality, establishing an effective foundation for photorealistic monocular dense SLAM in resource-constrained environments.

title: Effects of ultrafast phase modulation on dynamics and stability criteria of passive mode-locking
authors: Xuecheng Wu, Dongyuan Li, Menghao Fang, Fengqiu Wang
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: Dispersion or chirping, plays an important role in stabilizing optical solitons as found in mode-locked lasers. However, the effect of a transient chirping on passive mode-locking has rarely been investigated. Here, we demonstrate that an intracavity electro-optic phase modulation (PM) operating about a high-order harmonic frequency of the cavity can cause deterministic and reproducible fluctuation dynamics. Specifically, the offset frequency Δf = |f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PM</sub> − N f<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rep</sub>| synthesizes a slow periodic driving force, yielding soliton fluctuations whose frequency is locked to the detuning Δf. When Δf is increased, the soliton response evolves from breathing to wavelength oscillation. More interestingly, for the on-resonance case where Δf ~ 0, we observe a clear stability threshold for the cavity solitons, as set by the strength of the transient chirp exerted by the phase modulation. Beyond the threshold RF drive power of 14 dBm, passive mode-locking is no longer sustained, and the laser exhibits signatures of active mode-locking, as evidenced by changes in both RF and optical spectra. Our results provide new routes for active control of soliton dynamics and may provide new insights into understanding the balancing between different physical factors in mode-locking.

title: Mechanically Frequency-Reconfigurable and Circularly-Polarized Fabry-Pérot Cavity Antenna
authors: Ying Liu, Weihao Tang, Yongtao Jia, Jiahao Zhang, Haoyu Lei, Zhong-Xun Liu, Hu Liu, Shuxi Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A left-handed circularly polarized (LHCP) and frequency reconfigurable Fabry-Pérot cavity antenna (FPCA) is proposed using mechanically reconfigurable Fabry-Pérot cavity (FPC). By using chiral metasurfaces as the top and bottom boundaries of the FPC, LHCP waves can be multiply reflected within the cavity while preserving the same handedness. Moreover, the resonant frequency is directly dependent on its twist angle. Hence, the antenna can realize frequency reconfiguration by rotating the chiral partially reflective surface (PRS). The chiral bottom reflector is designed with low polarization loss. Additionally, a chiral PRS is designed to achieve both chiral reflection and polarization-stable transmission during the rotation of the PRS, ensuring stable LHCP radiation during reconfiguration. Finally, a Fabry-Pérot cavity antenna is obtained under the excitation of an LHCP source antenna. It achieves narrowband performance in terms of gain and axial ratio bandwidth with a high directionality. Moreover, a wide and continuous frequency reconfiguration range of peak gain is realized from 5 to 6.02 GHz. The maximum peak gain of 19.98 dBic with a variation of less than 1.9 dB is realized. Maximum aperture efficiency reaches 55.55%. A prototype has been fabricated and measured for verification. Both simulation and measurement results show good agreement, confirming the feasibility of the proposed design method.

title: MLGA-Mamba: Multiscale Locally Gated Attention and Dual-Stream Decoder Guided Vision Mamba for Change Detection of Fast-Growing Urban Regions
authors: Fei Song, Xiaolin Wei, Fei Luo, Daiyang Xiao, Tao Lei, Qiang Cheng, Jia Liu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Urban land cover and land use (LCLU) changes are a dynamic and frequently rapid process that serves as a constitutive element of urban development. Nevertheless, the precise identification of urban changing ground objects at different scales remains challenging in high-resolution (HR) remote sensing images because of the difficulties associated with effectively modeling discriminative features across temporal and spatial variations. In this paper, a novel multiscale locally gated attention (MLGA) and dual-stream decoder guided vision mamba (MLGA-Mamba) is proposed for change detection of fast-growing urban regions from HRremote sensing images. Specifically, the collaborative integration of the MLGA and multi-angle SSM as the encoder facilitates the large-scale spatial feature extraction and multiple-receptive field local details through a parallel multibranch design, while leveraging an adaptive learnable gating mechanism to suppress background noise interference. Simultaneously, the dual-stream task-specific decoder with prior knowledge (DSTSD) is derived to distinguish changes by constructing a multiscale semantic-aware decoder with spatial-temporal parallel and cross relationship modeling, and a difference-aware decoder with scene semantic guided (DAD). To dynamically facilitate the spatial-temporal interaction of DSTSD, DAD infers priors from invariant scene semantic features through top-K minima selection and feature clustering. Experiments are conducted on the prevalently utilized LEVIR-CD, CDD and SYSU-CD datasets with urban buildings and green spaces across various scale. Compared with other state-of-the-art methods, the MLGA-Mamba provides favorable performance in terms of accuracy and generalization capability.

title: YOLO-MESO: Multi-Scale and Small Object-Aware Improvements for Blood Cell Detection
authors: Yingzhuo Xu, Xiaochi Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address challenges in blood cell detection, such as large variations in target scales, sensitivity to edges, loss of small target features, and high rates of false negatives and false positives in complex backgrounds, we propose an improved YOLOv11-based blood cell detection model, YOLO-MESO. The model optimizes both the backbone and neck networks: the backbone incorporates a CSP-MSES module, which uses multi-scale pooling, feature reconstruction, and fusion to enhance contour and fine-grained feature representation of blood cells, improving feature extraction for complex targets. The neck network introduces a Small Object Enhance Pyramid (SOEP) structure, combined with SPDConv and CSPOmni-Kernel, to enhance high-resolution detail retention, multi-scale feature fusion, and context modeling, thereby improving detection performance for small blood cells and targets in complex backgrounds. Experiments on the BCCD dataset show that YOLO-MESO achieves mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 of 92.43% and 64.74%, respectively, which are improvements of 1.60% and 2.62% over the baseline YOLOv11n, demonstrating the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed model in blood cell detection tasks.

title: Coded Data Rebalancing for Distributed Data Storage Systems with Cyclic Storage
authors: Abhinav Vaishya, Athreya Chandramouli, Srikar Kale, Prasad Krishnan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: We consider replication-based distributed storage systems in which each node stores the same quantum of data and each data bit stored has the same replication factor across the nodes. Such systems are referred to as balanced distributed databases. When existing nodes leave or new nodes are added to this system, the balanced nature of the database is lost, either due to the reduction in the replication factor, or the non-uniformity of the storage at the nodes. This triggers a rebalancing algorithm, that exchanges data between the nodes so that the balance of the database is reinstated. The goal is then to design rebalancing schemes with minimal communication load. In a recent work by Krishnan et al., coded transmissions were used to rebalance a carefully designed distributed database from a node removal or addition. These coded rebalancing schemes have optimal communication load, however, require the file-size to be at least exponential in the system parameters. In this work, we consider a cyclic balanced database (where data is cyclically placed in the system nodes) and present coded rebalancing schemes for node removal and addition in such a database. Our algorithms have time-complexity polynomial in the system parameters. These databases (and the associated rebalancing schemes) require the file-size to be only cubic in the number of nodes in the system, which we argue is the minimum required for rebalancing on such databases. We bound the advantage of our node removal rebalancing scheme over the uncoded scheme, and show that our scheme has a smaller communication load. Further, we also argue that in some parameter regimes, cyclic databases have better asymptotic tradeoffs between the rebalancing load and the file-size, when compared to earlier work by Krishnan et al. In the node addition scenario, the rebalancing scheme presented is a simple uncoded scheme, which we show has optimal load. Finally, we show an information-theoretic lower bound for single node-removal rebalancing under the requirement of achieving a target cyclic database, and show that our achievable rebalancing loads are order-optimal (in the number of nodes), more specifically within a multiplicative gap of 2 from the lower bound obtained.

title: Monocular Images based Freeboard Measurement of Inland Ships
authors: Tianlei Wang, Yan Fang, Hanming Zou, Dekang Liu, Jiuwen Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Freeboard measurement based on computer vision has significant practicality for the intelligent surveillance of shipping transportation. However, existing methods fail to adapt to this task due to the complex background of inland waterway and the irregular shape of ship side shell caused by the hull inclination. To address these issues, a novel visual measurement paradigm for the freeboard estimation of inland ships (ISFE) is proposed at the absolute scale of the scene from monocular images. ISFE consists of a pixel-level height estimation stage and a horizon line-based real-world height estimation procedure, requiring solely the record of the camera optical center height. Specifically, a deck and water line detector (DWLD) incorporating an enhanced Mask R-CNN and a novel unsupervised line regression is proposed for pixel height estimation. Subsequently, a data-driven horizon line detector is developed as an intermediary to establish the relationship between the real-world height and the monocular image. Experiments on a real-world freeboard measurement dataset are conducted and the results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ISFE. The source code is available at https://github.com/TianleiWang/ISFE.

title: A Soft-Switching Resonant Inverter with Integrated Energy Buffer for Input Power Shaving in Pulsed Load Applications
authors: Hatif Bin Abdul Majeed, Zeljko Pantic
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Low-frequency pulsed loads introduce significant dc-side current pulsations, increase current stress, and degrade power quality in grid-connected resonant power conversion systems. This paper presents a resonant inverter-based system with an integrated energy-buffering (EB) branch that mitigates these effects without requiring an additional power-conversion stage. A systematic design methodology is developed based on steady-state and extended zero-voltage-switching (ZVS) analyses for both power-delivery and recharge operating phases. In addition, a modulation and control framework with ac/dc decoupling is introduced that utilizes differential-mode for ac power transfer and common-mode for dc energy buffering, enabling reduced current stress while maintaining ZVS operation throughout the operational cycle. The proposed system is validated using a 1-kW hardware prototype operating at 85 kHz. Experimental results demonstrate stable operation under dynamic load profiles, ZVS operation across all operating phases, and dc-to-dc efficiency exceeding 92% at full load. Furthermore, the proposed approach achieves an 82.5% reduction in dc-side current pulsations and a 150% reduction in input current dc ripple factor for load profiles ranging from 100 ms to 700 ms, establishing its effectiveness for low-frequency pulsed-load applications.

title: Adaptive Duty-Ratio-Modulated Direct Torque Control Based on Extended Virtual Voltage Vectors for PMSM Drives
authors: Yiwei Hao, Gaolin Wang, Jiayi Zhao, Guoqiang Zhang, Qiwei Wang, Li Ding, Guangdong Bi, Dianguo Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Duty-ratio-modulated direct torque control (DDTC) suffers from the limited phase resolution of voltage vectors and strong sensitivity to motor parameter variations, degrading its torque and flux control performance for permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drives. To address the issue, an adaptive duty-ratio-modulated direct torque control strategy based on extended virtual voltage vectors is proposed. By analyzing the coupling effects of the duty ratio on torque and flux, an efficient vector combination mechanism is developed, in which the torque deadbeat response and the flux ripple are adopted as optimization criteria. This method dynamically extends virtual voltage vectors with continuously adjustable phases, achieving coordinated optimization of the output torque and flux. To overcome the dependency of duty-ratio calculations on accurate motor parameters, the torque production strategy is investigated to vectorially project torque control errors. Leveraging a voltage-vector phase-mapping scheme, the torque slope undergoes an equivalent linear correction and is integrated into the duty-ratio model to achieve adaptive regulation, thereby effectively suppressing torque ripples and enhancing the robustness. Experimental results on a 2.2-kW PMSM drive platform validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: A High Efficiency and High Precision PMU for Neural Stimulation Applications
authors: Xincai Liu, Jiatong Wu, Shihao Xiao, Gang Dong, Xufeng Liao, Zhangming Zhu, Lianxi Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: A high efficiency and precision power management unit (PMU) for neural stimulation application is introduced in this paper. The PMU consists of a dual-loop burst mode boost converter and the low-power output stage (LPOS). The dual-loop hysteretic structure in the boost converter decouples the ripple window from the feedback divider, thereby maintaining a constant output ripple at high output voltages and improving voltage accuracy. In addition, the optimal inductor peak current (ILP,opt) is analytically derived from a loss model, which improves light-load efficiency. Furthermore, the high-PSR bandgap reference (BGR) in the LPOS is proposed to suppress input disturbances and provide high-precision VREF for the neural stimulation system, while a retention memory buffer is used to reduce power consumption. Additionally, an operational transconductance amplifier (OTA) with assistant paths is proposed to reduce power consumption further. The PMU is implemented in a 0.18 μm BCD process, occupying a core area of 1.74 mm². It operates over an input voltage range of 2.6-4.2 V and delivers a regulated 10, 15, and 20 V output. The converter achieves a peak conversion efficiency of 85.8% across a wide load current range from 10 μA to 10 mA, while consuming an ultra-low quiescent current of only 280 nA and exhibiting a line regulation of 0.00083 V<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup>, along with a BGR PSR of -138 dB.

title: GMC-Bind: A Multimodal Framework for RNA-Protein Binding Site Prediction with Bidirectional Cross-Attentional Fusion_supp1-3700035.docx
authors: JiaXing Luo
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate identification of RNA-protein binding sites is crucial for understanding gene regulation and disease mechanisms. However, existing deep learning models still face challenges in synergistically modeling multi-scale sequence motifs and dynamic structures. To address this, we propose GMC-Bind, a multimodal framework combining multi-scale window convolution, graph attention, and bidirectional cross-attention mechanisms, which achieves efficient synergistic modeling of local sequence features and global structural dependencies. The framework employs multi-scale convolution to extract variable-length motif features, utilizes graph attention to encode the spatial topology of RNA secondary structures, and facilitates cross-modal interaction between sequence and structural features through a bidirectional cross-attention mechanism based on positional encoding, ultimately generating binding site predictions. Extensive experimental results on the RBP-24 dataset demonstrate that GMCBind achieves optimal performance in 17 out of 24 datasets, with an average AUC of 95.8%, significantly outperforming existing baseline methods and providing an effective new approach for RNA-protein binding site identification. The source code of our method can be found in <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/benqin66/GMC-Bind/</uri>.

title: SC-MEC: Spatial-Consistent Motion Error Compensation for Dynamic 3D Imaging_supp1-3697052.pdf
authors: Yajun Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: 3D shape measurement based on phase-shifting profilometry (PSP) has attracted extensive attention due to its high accuracy and non-contact characteristics. However, conventional PSP assumes a static scene during image acquisition, which severely restricts its applicability to dynamic scenarios. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a spatial-consistent motion error compensation (SC-MEC) framework for dynamic 3D imaging. Unlike existing methods that model motion-induced distortions solely as phase errors, the proposed approach jointly models pixel mismatch and phase error induced by object motion within a unified spatial framework. The motion vector of the target is estimated directly from reconstructed point clouds at adjacent time steps and, together with the calibrated spatial mapping between image and world coordinates, is used to initialize pixel displacement and phase deviation estimation. Based on this coupled error model, a global optimization strategy using simulated annealing is employed to iteratively refine both pixel-and phase-level compensation parameters by minimizing the geometric discrepancy between reconstructed point clouds.There are three major contributions in our method.Firstly, a spatial consistent motion error compensation (SC-MEC) framework is proposed, which uses the spatial motion information of the target to jointly model the pixel mismatch and phase error, and realizes the robust compensation under the complex non-uniform rigid body motion. Secondly, the motion vectors and compensation parameters are estimated directly from the adjacent reconstructed point clouds, which need not be labeled and are hardware independent, so it has wide applicability. Thirdly, a new optimization paradigm is introduced to solve the coupling error model in structured light measurement, and the simulated annealing algorithm is used to explore the global solution space to more accurately calculate the motion error in structured light measurement.

title: Dual-Layer Adaptive Filtering and Asynchronous Trajectory Interpolation for Low-Cost Robotic Manipulators
authors: Zihan Chen, Jingyi Ou, Wenbo Zhu, Yiran He, Wei Yu, Haibing Li, Haowen Tang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: With the rise of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) large language models, embodied intelligence has demonstrated immense potential in complex manipulation tasks. However, the challenge of reliably deploying high-frequency policy outputs onto low-cost serial servo hardware remains a core bottleneck hindering the widespread adoption of robotics. Traditional methods, such as direct command issuance or smoothing techniques relying on internal servo registers, often face a trade-off between suppressing mechanical resonance (jitter) and avoiding phase lag, leading to uncontrollable oscillations in the end-effector and subsequently disrupting the visual feedback loop. To address this challenge, inspired by the concept of control decoupling, this paper proposes a two-layer adaptive mapping architecture. In the upper-level spatial domain, by cascading exponential moving averages (EMA) with nonlinear dead-zone clipping, the method eliminates strategy noise at the source and constructs a static freezing window to optimize the visual capture environment; in the lower-level temporal domain, the introduction of asynchronous-independent threads executing high-frequency linear interpolation effectively bridges the discrete gap between model inference and low-level execution. Experiments show this reduces mechanical jitter by 18.8% and boosts grasping success from 65% to 80%, providing a highly scalable paradigm for the robust deployment of embodied intelligence on budget-friendly hardware.

title: A Blockchain-Assisted Anonymous Authentication Protocol for Data Reporting in VANETs
authors: Rui Su
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) have recently gained growing attention. In VANETs, vehicles report traffic data to Roadside Units (RSUs) to improve Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) services. Many anonymous authentication data reporting protocols have been proposed to ensure security and privacy. However, these protocols typically require numerous preloaded pseudonyms or rely on irrevocable group signatures, leading to high storage/computation costs or lack of revocation mechanisms, hindering practical deployment. To address these issues, this paper proposes a blockchain-assisted anonymous authentication protocol for data reporting in VANETs. Our protocol allows authenticated vehicles to report messages to RSUs without massive pseudonym storage costs. Moreover, it achieves efficient revocation — the TA merely updates the vehicle’s status on the blockchain, without any additional cryptographic computation or public key redistribution, unlike existing protocols. Security analysis shows that our protocol satisfies source authentication, anonymity, unlinkability, traceability, revocability, nonframeability, and nonrepudiation. Our protocol ensures security and privacy with lower computational overhead than P2BA and TRAJ (slightly higher than GSAA-PS), and the lowest communication overhead overall.

title: Enhancing Real-Time Scalper Bot Detection in Ticketing Systems Through Transactional Behavior Analysis
authors: Kousik Naren A, Vanusha D, Vathana D, Delsi Robinsha S, Helen K Joy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Ticket scalping has intensified dramatically since automated bots started using online booking systems to their advantage. The bots employ automation to get multiple ticket allocations within seconds leading to higher prices from resale while bypassing human users. Traditional detection methods like CAPTCHAs, browser fingerprinting, and interaction analysis often can't keep up with today's advanced bots that closely imitate human behavior. This paper introduces ScalpWatch architecture which uses user transaction patterns to get a deeper understanding of user activities and thereby improving bot detection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves 99.1% accuracy, with a precision of 99.4% and a recall of 98.8%, ensuring high reliability in distinguishing scalper bots from human users. The implemented solution provides a beneficial approach to detecting and preventing scalper bots during real time operations. It can also work smoothly alongside existing detection tools, helping promote fairer ticketing and supporting the Sustainable Development Goals by ensuring more equal access to events.

title: Benchmarking Multi-Agent LLM and Single Agent LLM Efficiency for Contextual Text Generation
authors: K. Arora, A. Naim, S. Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Text generation is commonly done with large language models, yet the question of whether the task breaking down into several agents is better-performing than the classic single-agent model remains open. To examine this the focus of this study was to compare the performance of Multi agent LLM and Single agent LLM for contextual text generation. The aim was to determine which system generates the answers that are nearer to the reference text and reflect the valuable information more effectively. This was achieved by comparing both setups using Google's Gemini-Flash-Latest model. For standard benchmarking fundamental science domains were selected as the basis of each setup. The Single agent setup worked like a traditional monolithic LLM. It independently generated responses for each query. On the other hand, the Multi agent setup divided the given tasks into subproblem and solved them in a structured way. It had multiple agents each doing their specific separate task like there were distinct agents for retrieval, reasoning, and summarization. To evaluate the generated outputs of each system they were tested on NLP metrics like BLEU, ROUGE, METEOR, and BERTScore under same conditions. These metrics were selected because of their ability to analyze text similarity, fluency, and semantical accuracy. The results showed that a multi agent system outperforms a single agent system.

title: Development of a Real-Time IoT-Based Air Quality Monitoring Station with Integrated PCB Power Management
authors: Sabatini Saha, Pooja Anand, Yashwant Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Air pollution has become one of the major concerns in terms of environment and public health due to the continuous rise in particulate matter and harmful gases present in urban regions. Traditional air-quality monitoring stations are bound in coverage, expensive to install, and cannot provide location-specific real-time information. These establish the need for a low-cost, continuously available monitoring approach using the Internet of Things. This paper presents a design and implementation of an IoT-based real-time air-quality monitoring integrated with a power management system on a printed circuit board. The proposed system measures PM2.5, PM10, carbon-dioxide concentration, and ambient temperature using dedicated sensors interfaced with a microcontroller ESP32. Sensor data are locally processed and then wirelessly transmitted to a cloud platform for continuous observation and visualization of the air-quality index, allowing timely awareness and informed decision-making. In the experimental evaluation, stable communication, reliable sensing performance, and efficient power utilization have been achieved. A compact and scalable, energy-efficient solution has been developed suitable for smart-city deployment in long-term environment monitoring.

title: Multi-Class Classification of Human Skin Types Using Deep Neural Networks and Ingredient-Based Skincare Recommendation
authors: Nafisa Haider, Sudhanshu Tripathi, Anil Saroliya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The accurate identification of human skin types is essential for personalized skincare applications. Conventional assessment techniques often show subjectivity and require expert involvement. The paper suggests a multi-class skin type classification framework based on deep learning, with an ingredient-based skincare recommendation system, which is automated. There are six categories of facial skin images, which include acne-prone, combination, dry, normal, oily, and wrinkleprone. Four convolutional neural network architectures-ResNet, EfficientNet, DenseNet, and ConvNeXt-which are implemented and evaluated. According to experimental results, ResNet and EfficientNet have classification accuracies of 77% each, DenseNet has 79% and ConvNeXt has a higher classification accuracy of 84 % on the test dataset than the other models. Depending on the estimated skin type, a rule-based recommendation module offers appropriate skincare ingredient recommendations. Also, an interactive interface based on Gradio is created to allow real-time image classification and customized ingredient suggestions. The suggested system shows that deep learning-based skin analysis is effective and can be used in intelligent skincare recommendation systems.

title: AttCL-HAR: An Attention-Based CNN-LSTM Framework for Inertial Human Activity Recognition
authors: Xingyou Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) is an important research topic in intelligent health monitoring, smart elderly care, and human-computer interaction. To address the challenges of strong non-stationarity, significant inter-subject variability, and high similarity among activity categories in inertial measurement unit (IMU) data, this paper proposes a HAR method that uses long short-term memory (LSTM) for temporal modeling and integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with multi-head self-attention (MHA). The method combines sliding-window preprocessing, LSTM-based temporal modeling, CNN-based local feature extraction, and MHA to enhance discriminative representation. Experiments on UCI-HAR, WISDM, and PAMAP2 show that the proposed method achieves accuracies of 94.13%, 99.62%, and 91.79%, respectively, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Self-Assembled Monolayer Functionalized Q-BIC Terahertz Metasurfaces for High-Sensitivity Biosensing
authors: Ji Lei Hou, Pi Bin Bing, Xin Yan, Lan Ju Liang, Lin Wu, Ning Kun Liang, Zhong Yang Li, Gui Fang Wu, Hai Yun Yao, Zhen Hua Li, Zhao Qing Sun, Zhen Dong Wang, Jian Quan Yao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) provide a controllable interfacial engineering strategy for terahertz (THz) sensing by enabling precise tuning of electromagnetic boundary conditions. In this work, a sensing platform based on quasi-bound states (Q-BIC) in the continuum THz metasurfaces is developed, integrating SAMs-enabled surface functionalization with colloidal gold to construct a synergistically engineered interface. This hybrid configuration allows coordinated modulation of resonance characteristics and interfacial electromagnetic responses, leading to markedly improved sensing performance. The metasensor incorporating SAMs-functionalized colloidal gold exhibits the highest sensitivity, achieving a limit of detection of 19.59 pg/mL for bovine serum albumin（BSA）, with a resonance peak modulation depth 3.6 times greater than that of the colloidal gold-only metasensor. The enhancement primarily originates from the strong near-field confinement induced by the Q-BIC mode and the spatially controlled distribution of colloidal gold within hotspot regions enabled by SAMs functionalization. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively leverages synergistic interfacial engineering and nanostructure integration, providing a robust pathway for developing high-performance THz biosensors based on Q-BIC metasurfaces.

title: IEEE Standard for Health informatics--Device interoperability--Part 10422: Personal health device communication: Device Specialization--Urine Analyzer
authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: Within the context of the IEEE 11073 family of standards for device communication, a normative definition of data communicated between personal telehealth urine analyzer devices and computer engines (e.g., cell phones, personal computers, personal health appliances, and set top boxes) is established by this standard in a manner that enables plug-and-play interoperability. Appropriate portions of existing standards are leveraged, including IEEE 11073 terminology, information models, application profile standards, and transport standards. The use of specific term codes, formats, and behaviors in telehealth environments restricting optionality in base frameworks in favor of interoperability is specified. A common core of communication functionality for personal telehealth urine analyzers is defined in this standard.

title: Combating Temporal Misalignment: A Phase-Guided Method for Motor Imagery EEG Classification
authors: Wei Liang, Fulei Zhao, Xiaoyong Li, Jing Jin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Temporal misalignment and inherent latency variability in electroencephalography (EEG) signals pose significant challenges for motor imagery (MI) brain-computer interface (BCI) decoding. While deep learning methods have achieved remarkable progress, they typically assume temporal consistency and often fail to generalize across sessions with substantial phase shifts. To address this limitation, we propose a unified framework that integrates a hybrid convolutional neural network (CNN) and temporal convolutional network (TCN) backbone with a novel differentiable phase shifting mechanism, which formulates temporal synchronization as a fully differentiable spectral phase-correction module. Specifically, a lightweight guidance network predicts sample-specific phase offsets from frequency-domain representations, which parameterize a complex-valued rotation operation performing continuous phase adjustment while preserving amplitude information, enabling end-to-end gradient-based optimization of phase alignment. A variance regularization term further constrains the corrected latent representations to form a compact and consistent feature manifold. Experiments on the benchmark MI BCI Competition IV-2a dataset demonstrate that the proposed method significantly enhances the extraction of classdiscriminative features and achieves an average classification accuracy of 80.94 %, outperforming standard baselines. These results indicate the effectiveness of explicit phase modeling in mitigating temporal jitter and indicate that autonomously learned shift-invariant representations provide a robust solution for decoding complex EEG dynamics, supporting the development of more reliable BCIs in real-world scenarios.

title: Urban Pipeline Safety Monitoring and Early Warning Decision Support System with Artificial Intelligence Application
authors: Qi Cao, Fulei Zhao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: With the acceleration of urbanization, urban pipeline infrastructure faces severe challenges in safe operation, especially in coastal industrial cities where pipelines suffer serious damage and environmental erosion. To solve this problem, this study designs and develops an Artificial Intelligence based urban pipeline safety monitoring and early warning decision support system. Adopting the agile Scrum development process, the system takes Convolutional Neural Network as the core analysis model, integrates pressure, temperature, humidity, water volume, voltage, current, flow rate, and amplitude eight major prediction parameters, realizes intelligent fusion of multi-source heterogeneous data and dynamic risk assessment. The system can effectively identify pipeline defects, predict risks, and improve early warning capability and emergency response efficiency, providing reliable technical support and decision-making basis for urban pipeline safety management. Practical verification in Maoming City shows that the system achieves 98.2% accuracy in pipeline defect identification, and shortens early warning response time by 65% compared with traditional systems. This research breaks through the limitations of single-scenario application of existing pipeline monitoring systems, and provides a replicable intelligent solution for safety management of urban pipeline networks in coastal industrial areas, which promotes the intelligent upgrading of urban infrastructure monitoring technology.

title: Half Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)

title: Attention-Enhanced Surrogate Modeling for Robust and Interpretable Multilayer Photonic Film Design
authors: Huan Tan, Xin Gui
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Fast and reliable forward modeling is essential for data-driven inverse design of multilayer photonic films. Although neural surrogate models can greatly accelerate spectrum prediction compared with traditional solvers, their practical use is often limited by poor out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization and weak physical interpretability. In this work, we develop an attention-enhanced one-dimensional convolutional surrogate model for transmission-spectrum prediction of five-layer dielectric thin films. On a standard random split, the proposed model achieves a test global R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9977 ± 0.0004. Under a strict OOD split defined by extreme layer-thickness combinations, it also achieves the best performance, with a test global R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9709. Permutation feature importance, controlled thickness sweeps, and an additional physical intervention experiment consistently show that the learned sensitivity of the substrate-adjacent layer is aligned with the underlying refractive-index contrast. These results demonstrate that the proposed framework is accurate, robust, and physically interpretable for multilayer photonic film modeling.

title: ViFormerNet: A Dual-Branch Multi-Task Framework for EEG-Based Vigilance Estimation
authors: Zijie Cui, Xiaoguang Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenges in alertness assessment using electroencephalogram (EEG) signals, such as loss of topological information, neglect of the continuity of fatigue evolution, and individual heterogeneity, this paper proposes ViFormerNet, a dual-branch multi-task modeling framework. This framework captures temporal continuity and macroscopic topological relationships simultaneously through global spatiotemporal branches and hierarchical branches of brain regions, and effectively enhances feature representation efficiency by sharing Conformer weights using a parameter reuse strategy. Furthermore, the model integrates ordinal classification multi-task learning and a data-driven Optimal decision threshold mechanism (ODT), achieving a precise mapping from continuous PERCLOS metrics to fatigue states, effectively overcoming individual differences. Experimental results on the SEED-VIG dataset show that ViFormerNet achieved a peak Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.9616 and a classification accuracy (Acc) of 97% in independent subject trials. In the leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) experiments, the model maintained an average PCC of 0.8532 and an average classification accuracy of 82%. In the comparative experiment, the model significantly outperformed the mainstream baseline model. Ablation experiments confirmed the effectiveness of the dual-branch architecture and multi-task constraints, providing a highly robust solution for real-time alertness monitoring.

title: Robust DOA Estimation for Single-Snapshot Sparse Arrays Based on a Spatial Feature Enhancement Network
authors: Tianyu Liao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address array sparsification caused by limited snapshots and element failures in vehicle-mounted radar, as well as insufficient feature extraction due to the neglect of physical spatial topology in existing methods, this paper proposes a robust DOA estimation algorithm for single-snapshot sparse arrays based on a spatial feature enhancement network. The algorithm utilises a sparsity enhancement strategy to simulate diverse failure modes, constructing a network framework comprising feature construction, convolutional extraction, attention enhancement, and spatial spectral output. The model introduces one-dimensional convolutions to replace fully connected layers, thereby deeply exploring the local phase correlations of array signals; simultaneously, it integrates a CBAM module to adaptively allocate weights across both channel and spatial dimensions and generate spatial masks, effectively suppressing noise from faulty elements. Experiments demonstrate that this method significantly improves estimation accuracy, multi-target resolution and interference robustness.

title: Secure Homomorphic Encryption and D2D-Aided Digital Cousin Framework for Privacy-Preserving 6G Data Sharing
authors: Zerong Zheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The sixth-generation (6G) wireless system is expected to support massive connectivity, real-time intelligence, and ubiquitous data services. Digital Cousin (DC), as a lightweight and generalized virtual representation, has recently emerged as a practical alternative to high-fidelity Digital Twin (DT) models. However, the large-scale acquisition, transmission, and processing of heterogeneous data required for DC construction pose significant challenges in terms of efficiency, privacy protection, and trusted computation. This paper proposes a Secure Homomorphic Encryption–Driven and Device-to-Device (D2D)-Assisted Digital Cousin (SHEDC) framework to enable privacy-preserving 6G data sharing. The framework integrates D2D transmission for low-latency data forwarding, multi-access edge computing (MEC) for distributed intelligence, and homomorphic encryption (HE) to ensure confidentiality during end-to-end data collection and processing. A hierarchical three-layer system architecture is developed, including data collection, secure communication, and DC processing. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly reduces spectrum utilization and transmission latency, while maintaining acceptable energy consumption and providing strong privacy guarantees against potential D2D and MEC attacks. The results verify that SHEDC offers an effective and scalable solution for secure DC construction in future 6G networks.

title: Distributed Cooperative Localization With Joint Estimation of Target Position and Transmit Power
authors: Yingquan Li, Bodhibrata Mukhopadhyay, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Localization in wireless networks is traditionally performed through centralized architectures that process measurements at a single node. However, such architectures not only lead to a high computational burden but also suffer from limited robustness, as the failure of the central node terminates the localization operation. They also require extremely high communication bandwidth, as all nodes must transmit their measurements to a central server. Moreover, assuming prior knowledge of each target node's transmit power is impractical, as it varies with battery level, hardware imperfections, and antenna orientation. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Distributed Cooperative Localization technique (DCLU), which jointly estimates the location and transmit power of the nodes. The joint maximum-likelihood formulation is non-convex and discontinuous, and cannot be solved using off-the-shelf optimization techniques. We address this challenge by constructing a convex approximation of the ML objective, which enables efficient distributed estimation using a coordinate descent algorithm. We further establish theoretical guarantees for convergence, including the rate of convergence. Compared with centralized approaches, DCLU offers enhanced scalability and robustness. We also derive the Cramer-Rao lower bound (CRLB) as a performance benchmark. Extensive simulations and on-field experiments demonstrate that DCLU achieves higher localization accuracy while significantly reducing computational complexity relative to existing techniques.

title: Spectral Decomposition and Responsive Scaling: A Dual-Pattern Approach for Efficient Serverless Auto-Scaling
authors: Pengwei Wang, Lin Sun, Haoquan Qi, Yichen Zhong, Zhijun Ding, Shun Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computers
abstract: Auto-scaling is a critical mechanism for adapting to dynamic workload fluctuations in serverless environments, ensuring that sufficient instances are available to handle incoming requests. However, existing approaches usually rely on fixed parameter configurations or single-scale prediction methods, which are inadequate for timely instance adjustments in response to workload variations. This limitation exacerbates the cold start problem while also causing either excessive resource allocation or inefficient utilization. In this study, we propose a FRequency-Adaptive Prediction (FRAP) method that utilizes Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) to perform spectral decomposition of request frequency into high-frequency and low-frequency patterns. The high-frequency component, representing bursty fluctuations, is predicted using an Improved Fourier Series Fitting (IFSF) method, whereas the low-frequency component, reflecting long-term workload trends, is predicted using the Holt-Winters (HW) method. This dual-pattern modeling enables accurate identification of intrinsic characteristics. Based on this, we design a three-layer responsiveness-aware scaling model that integrates Instantaneous Response (IR) Model, Comprehensive Prediction (CP) Model, and Long Term Adjustment (LTA) Model. By dynamically adjusting the weights assigned to each model, the proposed FRAP-based Dynamic Weighted Scaling (FRAP-DWS) strategy generates adaptive scaling decisions, thereby reducing the occurrence of cold starts. Experimental results on the Azure Functions dataset demonstrate that our method improves the quality-price ratio by at least 16.7% compared to SOTA approaches.

title: Hand Gesture-Based Interaction System for 3D Medical Images
authors: Deepika Pantola, Deepika Vatsa, Samyak Sanjiv Gupta, Khyati Satija
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The creation of a novel hand gesture interaction system intended for use in medical scanning and diagnostic procedures will be examined in this research paper. This system eradicates the requirement for physical input devices by exploiting computer vision and the MediaPipe frameworks to enable medical professionals to interact with and navigate through 3D medical imagery with ease using only hand movements. We employed OpenCV for hand gesture processing and the image manipulation, and MediaPipe for robust hand tracking and landmark extraction. To build a reliable gesture classification mechanism, we used scikit-learn to train and evaluate machine learning models, particularly by training on the tailored dataset to suit our hand gesture classes. Python was used as the main language for the implementation because of its extensive ecosystem and compatibility with required libraries. We used the Windows 3D Viewer Application to visualize and interact with 3 Dimensional objects, which made it possible to test and demonstrate synchronized gesture based controls in a medical healthcare setting.

title: Efficient Traceable Secret Sharing with Non-Imputability for Off-Chain Verifiable Credential Storage
authors: Weinan Ju, Feiyu Wu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs) are fundamental infrastructures of Web3.0, enabling user-centric digital identity management. To address the high cost of on-chain storage, existing solutions adopt off-chain distributed storage, which introduces a critical security concern: the inability to hold leakers accountable when VC shards are leaked. Existing Traceable Secret Sharing (TSS) schemes suffer from quadratic storage overhead O(|s|<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and high tracing complexity O(n<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>), and lack non-imputability. This paper proposes an efficient traceable secret sharing scheme. Without modifying the standard Shamir share structure, our scheme precisely identifies leaky nodes from a compromised reconstruction box using polynomial interpolation and factorization. The tracing algorithm achieves O(f<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> + nf) time complexity, where f is the number of leaky nodes and n is the total number of shares, offering up to two orders of magnitude improvement over prior TSS solutions. The scheme also introduces a non-imputability mechanism based on one-way functions to prevent malicious tracers from framing honest nodes. Experimental results show that our scheme incurs negligible overhead compared to standard Shamir secret sharing in share generation and reconstruction.

title: Eigenvector Reconstruction-Based DOA Estimation for Coherent Signals
authors: Lin Yang, Sujie Wu, Guang Yang, Yuming Tang, Daoxing Guo
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In multipath environments, the coherent signals may cause rank deficiency of the received-data covariance matrix, which significantly degrades the performance of direction-of-arriva1 (DOA) estimation algorithms and may even lead to estimation failure. To address this issue, this paper proposes an eigenvector reconstruction-based DOA estimation algorithm for coherent signals. Specifically, the eigenvector corresponding to the largest eigenvalue of the received-data covariance matrix is first extracted to construct an equivalent covariance matrix, which enhances the useful signal component while suppressing noise to a certain extent. Then, forward-backward spatial smoothing (FBSS) is introduced to restore the rank of the covariance matrix and achieve decorrelation of coherent signals. Compared with conventional spatial smoothing methods and covariance reconstruction-based methods, the proposed algorithm exhibits stronger noise robustness, higher estimation accuracy, and better stability under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and small snapshot conditions. Simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed method for coherent signal DOA estimation.

title: Understanding Intra-Host Network Bottlenecks within RDMA Servers
authors: Kefei Liu, Jiao Zhang, Zhuo Jiang, Xin He, Ke Cheng, Yang Bai, Dongyang Song, Ruixue Wang, Weiqiang Cheng, Tao Huang
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: As the beginning and end point of Remote Direct Memory Access (RDMA) networks, intrahost networks are critical to RDMA performance. Network bottlenecks within RDMA servers can severely degrade RDMA performance, and must be quickly diagnosed and resolved when they occur. However, this is not an easy task. The causes of intra-host network bottlenecks are diverse and complex, including link downgrades, misconfigurations, and traffic contention. However, service developers and RDMA network operators typically do not specialize in intra-host networks. When dealing with intra-host network bottlenecks, they are often confused about the possible causes as well as how to resolve them. Unfortunately, there is little literature systematically studying intra-host network bottlenecks that developers and operators can refer to. This article aims to help network operators, developers, and researchers better understand network bottlenecks within RDMA servers. First, we introduce the typical network topology and RDMA traffic within RDMA servers. Then, we dive into the common causes of network bottlenecks within RDMA servers. We also discuss the impact of these bottlenecks on network performance and propose their solutions. Finally, we present existing schemes for intra-host bottleneck detection and diagnosis.

title: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Reliability-Oriented In-Situ Characterization of Thermal Interface Materials under Harsh Conditions for Power Electronics
authors: Yang Zhao, Wei He, Fan Zhou, Dinghua Hu, Qiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Reliable characterization of thermal contact resistance (TCR) under harsh operating environments remains a critical measurement challenge for thermal interface materials (TIMs) used in power electronics. Existing transient and steady-state techniques are fundamentally limited by their reliance on stress interruption, post-test evaluation, and controlled baseline measurement conditions, preventing continuous in-situ observation of interface degradation under harsh environmental and mechanical stresses. This work presents an integrated thermal characterization assembly (ITCA) that enables continuous, real-time, in-situ measurement of TCR during sustained exposure to harsh environments, including broadband random vibration (15–2000 Hz, 7.7 Grms), low-pressure (down to 6.5 kPa), and extreme temperatures (−55°C to 71°C). The proposed instrumentation incorporates a complete uncertainty quantification framework following ISO-GUM guidelines. System-level validation using Ti-6Al-4V reference specimens demonstrates long-term measurement stability within ±0.5% over a 4 h continuous operation period. The capability of the proposed measurement system is demonstrated using three representative TIM archetypes (thermal grease, thermal gel, and thermal pad). Continuous in-situ measurements reveal distinct TCR evolution behaviors under environmental stress, including pressure-induced degradation, vibration-driven fluctuation amplification, and stress-assisted interfacial redistribution, which cannot be captured by conventional interrupt-based methods. By enabling uninterrupted TCR monitoring under realistic harsh environments, the proposed ITCA provides a robust measurement platform for reliability-oriented thermal characterization. The methodology is scalable, and can be readily extended to other interfacial thermal measurement scenarios where continuous insitu assessment is required.

title: Dynamic target tracking algorithm for substation inspection robot
authors: Yong Li, Wei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Dynamic target tracking in substation environments is challenged by occlusions, varying illumination, and small or densely packed objects. To improve the performance of inspection robots, this paper proposes an enhanced tracking framework that integrates a lightweight YOLOv11s detector with an optimized DeepSORT tracker. The YOLOv11s backbone is augmented with the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) for better feature representation and the Adaptive Kernel Convolution (AKConv) module for dynamic adjustment of receptive fields, achieving higher accuracy with reduced computational cost. For the tracking stage, the ReID network in DeepSORT is improved by adopting a composite loss function that combines triplet, center, and contrastive losses, enhancing feature discriminability under appearance changes and occlusions. Experimental results on a self-collected substation dataset (2600 images) show that the proposed method achieves 97.3% mAP in detection, improves MOTA by 9.4% and reduces identity switches (IDs) by approximately 12.5% compared to the baseline DeepSORT. The approach demonstrates strong robustness and efficiency, making it suitable for real-world substation inspection tasks.

title: Lie-Algebraic Approach to Geometric Nonlinear $\mathcal {H}\_\infty$ Inverse Optimal Attitude Tracking on $SO(3)$
authors: Junsik Kim, Youngjun Joo, Youngjin Choi
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a geometric nonlinear <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {H}\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> inverse optimal attitude tracking framework formulated on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$SO(3)$</tex-math></inline-formula> using its Lie algebra <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathfrak {so}(3)$</tex-math></inline-formula>. By adopting exponential coordinates <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\xi }\in \mathfrak {so}(3)$</tex-math></inline-formula>, attitude errors are mapped directly onto the Lie algebra. This preserves a bijective, distortion-free relationship with the geodesic distance within the injectivity radius (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Vert {\xi }\Vert < \pi$</tex-math></inline-formula>), while eliminating singularities and unwinding issues inherent in Euler angles and quaternions. A sliding-mode-inspired nonlinear <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {H}\_\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> controller is developed, with robust stability established using the exponential coordinates and a systematic tuning methodology provided to facilitate practical implementation. Quadrotor simulations demonstrate faster convergence in large-angle maneuvers by mitigating the vanishing error and sluggish response of conventional methods. Experiments on a robotic manipulator validate the tuning rule for the prescribed disturbance attenuation level <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\gamma$</tex-math></inline-formula>, showing that the peak tracking error scales as <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\gamma ^{2})$</tex-math></inline-formula> for small gains and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {O}(\gamma)$</tex-math></inline-formula> for large gains. These results demonstrate the robustness and predictability of the proposed framework and the effectiveness of Lie algebra-based attitude control on <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$SO(3)$</tex-math></inline-formula>.

title: Row-Vector Transformer (RVT): AI-Driven Energy-Efficient Received Power Prediction for Sub-6 GHz RIS in 6G Wireless Networks
authors: Muhammad Abul Hassan, Fabrizio Granelli, Ali Hassan Sodhro
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Electromagnetics, Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Accurate and energy-efficient prediction of received signal power is critical for optimizing reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) in beyond-5G and emerging 6G wireless communication systems. These surfaces, capable of dynamically reconfiguring the propagation environment, demand real-time, high-precision modelling to maximize spectral and energy efficiency. Traditional model-based and deep learning approaches often rely on synthetic data or treat features independently, limiting their ability to capture complex, nonlinear electromagnetic interactions inherent in real-world RIS deployments. In this work, we propose the Row-Vector Transformer (RVT)—a novel AI framework designed for RIS-assisted 6G propagation modelling—that constructs a single, dense token from the entire feature set, including receiver angles, RIS phase configurations, and normalized received power measurements. Unlike conventional transformer architectures (e.g., FT-Transformer) that process features column-wise, RVT processes each sample holistically, enabling more effective extraction of electromagnetic channel dependencies and propagation patterns. Experimental validation on three real RIS measurement datasets demonstrates that RVT consistently outperforms advanced deep learning baselines, including 1D-CNN, LSTM, BI-LSTM, GRU, FCN, FT-Transformer and TabNet, achieving the lowest mean squared error (MSE), mean absolute error (MAE), and root mean square error (RMSE), along with faster convergence and greater stability. The results confirm RVT’s potential as a cutting-edge, AI-enabled solution for real-time RIS power prediction, contributing towards intelligent, adaptive, and energy-efficient channel modelling in future 6G wireless networks.

title: Super-Aligned Carbon Nanotube-Based Resistor Array for High-Speed and Polarization Infrared Scene Simulation_supp1-3694063.avi
authors: Yang Zhao
publish: N/A
abstract: The resistor array generates infrared radiation by Joule heating to simulate complex infrared scenes, helping evaluate the performance of the infrared imaging system. However, it remains challenging to simultaneously achieving resistor arrays with high-speed response and polarized emission. Here, we present a super-aligned carbon nanotube (SA-CNT) based RA, leveraging its ultralow thermal mass for high-speed response and intrinsic optical anisotropy for polarized emission without external optics. We fabricated a RA comprising <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 15$</tex-math> </inline-formula> pixels that exhibits both high-speed thermal response and polarization-dependent emission. Scaling predicts that reducing the pixel pitch to the state-of-the-art <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$46~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m could enable sub-2 ms response time and frame rates exceeding 500 Hz, which remains a projection, as thermal dynamics are not strictly linear under scaling. Polarization measurements reveal pronounced anisotropic thermal emission consistent with Malus’s law, yielding a polarization radiance ratio of 1.6. Our approach opens up new ways for next-generation infrared scene simulation system. [2026-0040]

title: Robust Wideband Spectrum Sensing in Low-SNR Environments with a Dynamic Convolution-Augmented Transformer
authors: Shilong Guo, Zhuhua Hu, Xianghui Li, Xiaopeng Liang, Yaochi Zhao, Zixun Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The escalating spectrum scarcity in the post-5G era has catalyzed an urgent demand for intelligent wideband spectrum sensing (WSS) techniques that harmonize high precision with computational efficiency. Although Transformer architectures demonstrate superior capabilities in modeling long-range dependencies, their direct adaptation to WSS is impeded by two fundamental bottlenecks: (i) the inherent inability of global self-attention to effectively localize salient spectral patterns within ultra-long sequences; and (ii) the deficient extraction of fine-grained local structural features, particularly under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regimes. To bridge these gaps, this paper presents a novel Transformer-enhanced framework for WSS, termed Enhanced Attention Mechanism and Positional Encoding for Wideband Spectrum Sensing (EAPC-WSS). EAPC-WSS incorporates two pivotal innovations: a Dynamic Convolutional Positional Encoding (DyCPE) module for adaptive multi-scale positional context modeling, and a convolution-augmented attention mechanism designed to fortify the characterization of localized spectral structures. By synergizing global receptive fields with localized representation learning, EAPC-WSS performs spectrum occupancy identification as a binary classification task. Extensive simulations and hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a USRP testbed validate that EAPC-WSS outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, including TFF aDCNN and CNN-T, yielding an accuracy improvement exceeding 30% in low SNR scenarios. This work establishes a robust and resource-aware paradigm for next-generation cognitive radio (CR) environments.

title: Impedance modeling and sensitivity analysis of fully differential inductive displacement sensor
authors: Jinghua Hu, Changlong Li, Zhenzhong Su, Jie Xu, Shibo Dong, Dong Wang, Wei Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Inductive displacement sensors are widely used in magnetic bearing control systems due to their simple structure, easy integration, and high cost-effectiveness. The frequency of the sensor operates above a few kHz, so non ideal factors such as iron core reluctance and eddy current effects cannot be ignored. However, existing studies do not fully consider non ideal factors, which limits the accuracy of the sensor impedance model. In this paper, the Schwarz–Christoffel transformation is used to compensate for stator-pole fringing effects, complex permeability is introduced to account for eddy-current and hysteresis effects, and a leakage coefficient is adopted to model flux leakage. An improved sensor impedance model and output characteristic model were established. An experimental platform is built to validate the proposed modeling method. The results show that the prediction error of sensitivity does not exceed 4% at different frequencies, verifying the correctness of the model. In addition, this article proposes a highly sensitive radial and axial integrated full bridge inductive displacement sensor, which has higher anti-interference ability and output sensitivity compared to the traditional half bridge differential circuit structure. Meanwhile, a quadrature phase-locked demodulation circuit based on analog switches has been developed, which has high excitation frequency capability and low cost.

title: A Millimeter-Wave Notched Mirror-Grid Dielectric Waveguide Resonator Antenna
authors: Wenke Jiang, Guanghui Xu, Wei Wang, Yanbin Luo, Xianlong Lu, Zhixiang Huang, Xianliang Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, a millimeter-wave (mm-wave) notched mirror-grid dielectric waveguide resonator antenna (GDWRA) is presented. The proposed antenna consists of a grid dielectric waveguide resonator (GDWR) with a notched mirror-line dielectric waveguide structure, and a differential feeding network. Due to notched mirror-line dielectric waveguide structure, electromagnetic fields are effectively confined by surrounding metallic walls for high gain. Simulations and measurements show that the antenna achieves a -10 dB impedance bandwidth of 11.6% (26.9-30.2 GHz) with a peak gain of 11.3 dBi. The overall profile height is only 0.12λ₀. With its integrated layout, low profile, and high gain, the proposed antenna is suited for mm-wave integrated system applications.

title: Bio-inspired Optofluidic Molecular Communication with Photothermally Actuated Microrobot Swarms_supp9-3698265.pdf
authors: Tingting Yu
publish: N/A
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) offers a bio-inspired paradigm for information transfer in environments inaccessible to conventional electromagnetic waves. However, translating MC concepts to the microscale has been hampered by a lack of integrated, biocompatible testbeds. Inspired by biological spectral-dependent photothermal transduction of specific light wavelengths into thermal energy, we present the first fully integrated microscopic MC platform utilizing photothermally responsive microrobot swarms. Our platform employs core-shell microrobots that exhibit a strong photothermal response, enabling precise and non-invasive navigation within microfluidic channels via near-infrared (NIR) light. This optofluidic architecture facilitates a symbiotic dual-bit encoding scheme, which concurrently modulates information onto both microrobot arrival and the optical control states. We demonstrate a complete communication workflow, from microrobot emission and laser-guided modulation to real-time optical detection and signal demodulation. The system achieves a data rate of 0.63 bits · min−1 with a low bit error rate of 4%, validated by a multi-sampling detection algorithm and the transmission of the ASCII string “HELLO WORLD”. This work provides a robust testbed for validating MC theories in biologically relevant microenvironments and serves as a step toward applications in the Internet of Bio-Nano Things.

title: Road Crack Detection Based on Improved YOLOv8
authors: Zhenzhen Li, Yin Ye
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of the road network, the demand for road maintenance and repair is constantly increasing. Road cracks, as one of the most common road diseases, not only damage the appearance of the road but also pose a threat to road traffic safety. The development of deep learning technology has made significant breakthroughs in road crack detection. However, due to the richness of the morphological types of road cracks and the diversity of the real road environment, the current road crack detection is still a very difficult problem. This study aims to achieve real-time and accurate detection of multiple types and various scenarios of road cracks. Based on the YOLOv8 framework, optimization research was carried out. Firstly, the public dataset RDD2022 was cleaned to remove unnecessary types of road cracks and dirty data, and the filtered RDD2022 dataset was obtained. Using the original YOLOv8 model as the base model, the CA attention mechanism was introduced to embed it and enhance the attention to the information of road cracks. Experiments were conducted on the RDD2022 dataset to verify the performance improvement of the improved model. The experimental results show that the improved model has a 2.3% increase in mAP compared to the original YOLOv8, and also has better robustness.

title: Design and Implementation of a Scientific Literature Retrieval System Based on Multimodal Features
authors: Fengping Xu, Yang Huang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To transform multimodal retrieval algorithms into practical tools, this paper designs and implements a comprehensive scientific literature retrieval system. Centered on the self-proposed multimodal feature learning algorithm, the system builds an end-to-end architecture including literature parsing, feature extraction and indexing, retrieval services, and an interactive frontend, and adopts a microservices design integrating GROBID parser and vector database. Evaluation on a self-constructed computer vision literature dataset shows the system retains the algorithm’s high retrieval accuracy (Top1-origin: 94.5%) with an average response time under 500 milliseconds. User studies confirm it outperforms traditional text-based retrieval in multimodal query expression, result relevance and user experience, providing a reproducible engineering solution for multimodal retrieval technologies.

title: Soil-Fertility-Based Yield Prediction for Rice (BPT-5204), Wheat (HD-2967), and Barley (RD-2035) Using Ensemble Machine Learning Models
authors: Avinash Rai, Ruby Dahiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Precise forecasting of crop yield is critical in enhancing the agricultural productivity and management of resources especially when soil fertility is on the decline and climatic vari- ability is on the rise. Conventional techniques of yield estimation that rely on the empirical observations and linear statistical modeling cannot reflect the nonlinear interactions between soil nutrients, physicochemical characteristics of soil as well as environmental conditions. This paper will introduce a machine learning system based on soilfertility to determine the harvest of three large cereal crops, which include: Rice (BPT-5204), Wheat (HD-2967), and Barley (RD-2035). The model incorporates physicochemical (pH, electrical conductivity, organic carbon), soil macronutrients (N, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}$</tex>, K), soil moisture and climatic variables (temperature and rainfall). Linear Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost models were also trained after preprocessing and normalization on a 70:30 train:test split and evaluated based on R 2, MAE, and RMSE. Findings indicate that ensemble models are more effective than the linear baseline, with the most successful predictive accuracy being the Random Forest (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R} 2$</tex> greater than 0.90). The analysis of feature importance determines the high yield determinants to be nitrogen, potassium, soil moisture, and rainfall. A composite Soil Fertility Index (SFI) is further created to make it more interpretable and useful. The suggested model assists in the precision and sustainable farming of the datadriven decision-making that is soil-sensitive.
