title: Low-Power VLSI Design for Edge AI Accelerators in IoT Devices
authors: Nivitha G, Aravind T, R. Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The paper’s main goal is to provide an energy-efficient hardware architecture that can run machine learning activities directly on edge devices with little delay and power expenditure. As the need for smart IoT apps develops, Artificial Intelligence (AI) accelerators that work well with Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) are needed for real-time data processing with stringent power constraints. Traditional AI computation on cloud platforms has security concerns and latency since data is always being sent. So, to move AI processing to the edge, we need small, low-power VLSI architectures that can conduct neural calculations quickly. Conventional accelerators have a lot of switching activity, waste a lot of power, and can’t be easily scaled up on Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) and Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) platforms; thus, they aren’t good for IoT edge situations. The goal of this project is to use VHDL on Xilinx ISE 14.7 to design and build a low-power AI accelerator that improves area, time, and power while keeping computational accuracy. The design uses clock gating, reducing the width of operands, and sharing resources in a matrix multiply–accumulate (MAC) architecture. A control Finite State Machine (FSM) and gated clock logic keep the operation flow running smoothly, while ISim is used to test functionality via simulations. The suggested architecture lowered dynamic power use and area use while keeping throughput the same, showing that it is good for real-time edge AI activities on Internet of Things (IoT) devices.

title: Beyond Silicon Area: Co-Product Allocation for a Binning-Aware Carbon Footprint of Processors
authors: Olivier Weppe, Thibaut Marty, Jean-Christophe Prévotet, Maxime Pelcat
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Central Processing Unit (CPU) manufacturing is a major contributor to the environmental footprint of digital technology. State-of-the-art Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and embodied Greenhouse Gasses (GHG) emission models evaluate carbon impacts of CPU manufacturing based on chip area and lithography resolution, which results in a uniform carbon footprint for an entire range of products derived from the same design. However, this simplification masks the heterogeneity arising from industrial binning, where chips from the same lot are differentiated according to their performance. To resolve this inconsistency, we propose a new analytical framework that considers the different variants of a processor as co-products. We develop and compare several allocation methods for CPU manufacturing impact, including an economic allocation (price-based) and a physical allocation (performance-based). Moreover, we model the influence of production yield by contrasting a uniform distribution hypothesis with a more realistic Gaussian yield distribution. Our results reveal a significant redistribution of the environmental burden when applying our methods: the carbon impact of high-end processors is substantially higher than current estimates, while that of entry-level models is reduced by up to 87%. This methodological framework provides a fairer and more accurate picture, essential for guiding eco-design strategies and hardware acquisition policies for enhancing electronics sustainability.

title: Global Careers, Local Belonging: Digital Technologies and Place-Belongingness in Professional Sport
authors: Hesam Mohseni, António Correia, Johanna Silvennoinen, Tommi Kärkkäinen
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Professional sport is a globalized phenomenon characterized by structural pressures on athletes. Professional athletes often experience high mobility, public visibility, a narrowly defined role-based identity, and institutional regulation of their professional and even private life matters by clubs, federations, and sponsors. Although the impact of these structural pressures on athletes' cultural transition and social belonging has been examined, little attention has been paid to how these pressures shape digitally mediated place-belongingness. This matters in the contemporary world, where digital technologies strongly shape how athletes experience belonging, cope with transition, and adapt to new environments. Building on these structural pressures, this paper extends the conceptual framework of digitally enhanced place-belongingness to the context of professional sport. The extended framework offers guidance for technology designers developing digital platforms that foster place-belongingness among professional athletes, supporting their well-being and social lives. Moreover, sports clubs, teams, and governing bodies can use the insights to improve relocation, integration, and athlete welfare programs.

title: Smart Access Hub for Urban Mobility – A Unified Quick Response (QR) Code-based Urban Service Platform
authors: K Lokesh Reddy, C C Govardhan, M Hemanth Kumar, J Vjiayrani
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The swift urbanization has contributed to the disintegration of essential services in the city resulting in inefficiencies in the urban mobility where citizens have to live through a myriad of fragmented applications. This study aims to design the project, called Smart Access Hub, which is a unified, full-stack design, consolidating parking, event management, and facility operations into an entire, scalable ecosystem. The suggested system adopts a React.js frontend and Django REST Framework back-end to provide a web application in the form of a Single Page Application (SPA). The main innovations are a database-level concurrency control system to avoid the situation of double-booking and a time-sensitive QR code engine to ensure physical security. The results indicate that the system can support an average end-to end latency of 450ms and a transaction success rate of 98 percent when there is a high load. The solution offers urban administrators with cost-effective, data-driven solutions that upgrade mobility infrastructure, which greatly enhances user experience relative to current silo solutions.

title: An EOQ Optimal Ordering Policy with Varying Generalised Time-Dependent Demand Allowing Shortages
authors: Pallavi Joshi, Trailokyanath Singh, Sudhansu Sekhar Routray, Rasmita Rautray
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In this study, an optimal EOQ policy for items having time-dependent generalized demand pattern when the deterioration rate is assumed to be constant. Partially backlogged shortages form is allowed to occur in this system. The constant and linear demand patterns together generalize the time-dependent demand pattern which is a quadratic function of time. We use the necessary and sufficient conditions in order to validate the optimal solutions of the present model. Furthermore, the decision variables such as shortages time-point and replenishment cycle and the total cost function have been computed with the help of a step-by-step solution procedure and Mathematica software 12.3. Finally, a couple of numerical examples and sensitivity analysis of optimal solutions based on one of the selected examples are discussed.

title: A Sentiment Analysis of Financial News for Stock Price Prediction
authors: S.T. Surulivel, Dhivya. V, A.Nandhini Priya, S. Selvabaskar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Financial news plays a significant role in shaping investor sentiment and influencing short-term stock price fluctuations. The rapid expansion of digital financial media platforms such as Economic Times and Moneycontrol has generated large volumes of unstructured textual data, making manual interpretation of market impact increasingly challenging. This study proposes a Natural Language Processing (NLP)-based stock movement prediction framework for Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) using Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) feature extraction and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier. Companyspecific financial news headlines are analyzed and empirically linked to next-day stock returns obtained from Yahoo Finance Experimental results indicate that the proposed SVM-based sentiment classifier achieves an overall prediction accuracy of 85.9 %, with a precision of 85.1 %, recall of 87.8 %, and an F1-score of 86.4 %. The model attains a Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) Area Under the Curve (AUC) value of 0.677, demonstrating a meaningful ability to distinguish between positive and negative financial sentiment. Empirical analysis further confirms a strong relationship between news sentiment and short-term stock price direction, with positive news sentiment associated with an average next-day return of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$+1.21 \%$</tex>, and negative sentiment associated with an average return of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-0.87 \%$</tex>.

title: A Deep Parallel Learning Framework for Accurate Plant Leaf Disease Detection in Real-World Field Conditions
authors: S. Prema, M. Divya, R. Manish Varma, S. Asokkumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: The most important source of food and raw materials to all the countries is agriculture and healthy crops are very vital in the maintenance of human life. One of the biggest causes of low crop productivity is plant diseases which must be determined with high accuracy and timely to avoid huge agricultural losses. Conventional image-processing methods are very dependent on features extracted manually, which are inefficient, yield inconsistent and usually have low recognition accuracy. The methods are time-consuming, have low reproducibility, and cannot perform in uncontrolled real-field settings in order to address these gaps, this paper presents the Leaf Net-Deep Parallel Convolutional Classifier (DPCC) framework, which is a combined deep-learning model capable of achieving precision in detecting leaf diseases. It starts with the Adaptive Botanical Illumination and Noise Suppression (ABINS) module which normalizes the plant leaf images by correcting uneven light, eliminating noises and stabilizes colour distributions. Then, Multi-Scale Botanical Transformer Feature Pyramid (MSBTFP) is used to obtain hierarchical lesion patterns which are fine and multi-level disease features. On this basis of these improved characteristics, the Deep Parallel Convolutional Classifier (DPCC) is a multi-stage-decision system with soft max activation, which is able to acknowledge plant diseases with better reliability, and at the same time, robust against misclassification with its current methods.

title: Auditory EEG-based Biometric Identification using an SE-Enhanced CNN Model
authors: Venkata Sai Jyothsna Kunireddy, Hema Kumar Goru, Yuva Teja Nittla, Srinivas Kumbha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Secure authentication is increasingly relying on biometric technology. However, common features like facial images and fingerprints are prone to spoofing and replication. Since brainwave signals are difficult to mimic outside and reflect internal mental activity, electroencephalography (EEG)-based biometrics provide a more authentic alternative. EEG-based biometric identification is currently a novel and technically challenging field of study, despite its potential. In this study, a lightweight deep learning (DL) framework based on a squeeze-and-excitation (SE) network with a channel-attention mechanism and a convolutional neural network (CNN) is presented. By adaptively recalibrating channel-specific feature representations, the SE-based channel-attention method enables the network to prioritize significant EEG signals for biometric identification. EEG recordings from the PhysioNet database during auditory stimulation were used in the experiments. The proposed SE-enhanced CNN model maintained balanced precision, recall, specificity, and F1-score while achieving an overall accuracy of 98.09%. The findings support the use of EEG-based biometric identification as a secure and useful authentication technique by showing that channel-attention added through SE blocks enhances performance.

title: Deep Spatial-Temporal Representation Learning for Reliable Gesture-to-Speech Conversion using Capsule Networks and Attention-Enhanced Temporal Convolution
authors: Shijina B, B Ben Sujitha
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Gesture-to-speech conversion systems transform visual hand gestures into audible language using intelligent computational models. These systems support individuals with hearing and speech impairments by enabling alternative modes of communication in daily interactions, medical assistance, and emergency situations. Accurate interpretation of dynamic gestures and reliable speech generation are essential to ensure clarity and effectiveness of conveyed information. Earlier solutions predominantly depended on wearable sensors, handcrafted feature engineering, or rule-based image processing techniques. Such approaches often constrained from limited scalability, hardware dependence, user discomfort, and reduced robustness under varying environmental conditions. This study presents a gesture-to-speech conversion framework that integrates deep spatial-temporal learning with automated speech synthesis. A Capsule Network (CapsNet) is employed to encode hierarchical spatial relationships and hand pose characteristics, while a Temporal Convolution Network (TCN) combined with an attention mechanism models motion dynamics across video frames. The recognized gesture labels are subsequently converted into audible output using a text-to-speech module. Experimental evaluation conducted on an Indian Sign Language emergency gesture dataset demonstrates effective recognition capability, achieving an overall accuracy of 96.34%, precision of 96.20%, recall of 96.15%, and F1-score of 96.17%. The findings confirm that the developed framework provides accurate gesture interpretation and reliable audio generation with modest computational requirements, rendering it suitable for practical assistive communication applications.

title: Random Forest Model for Ensuring Integrity of Blockchain-Based Electronic Health Records
authors: Punitha K, Thirumaraiselvan P, Vijaya Naga Valli K, Duraichi N, Monisha R, Reshma Devi N
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Safeguarding the security and integrity of electronic health records (EHRs) is essential in contemporary healthcare systems. Blockchain technology offers a decentralized, immutable framework for maintaining medical data; yet issues persist in identifying rogue nodes and imposing access control. This paper proposes a Random Forest methodology to improve the security and reliability of blockchain-integrated EHR systems. The model utilizes a dataset including 1,800 EHRs gathered from various hospitals and Internet of Things (IoT) medical equipment, integrating clinical data with blockchain metadata, including node identifiers, record hashes, and access protocols. The Random Forest classifier attained an accuracy of 99.17% in predicting access rules and 98.61 % in identifying malicious nodes, demonstrating elevated precision, recall, and F1-scores. Feature significance study identified blockchain-specific characteristics, notably node IDs and record hashes, as the most significant for categorization. The suggested solution integrates machine learning with blockchain to secure data integrity, prohibit unauthorized access, and facilitate patient-centric data exchange. The findings indicate that the integration of cryptographic security with predictive analytics yields a strong, scalable, and efficient approach for secure healthcare data management, facilitating trustworthy and dependable EHR operations in decentralized networks.

title: Cooperative Secondary Control for Negative Sequence Voltage Suppression and Optimal Power Sharing in Islanded Microgrids
authors: Rania Emad Hamdi, Firas Mohammed Tuaimah
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The problem of voltage imbalance caused by unbalanced loads is one of the main challenges of islanded operation of the inverter-based microgrid. A negative-sequence voltage appears, affecting power quality. In addition, negativesequence voltage may cause circulating currents between inverters. In this paper, a new control method to mitigate negative-sequence voltage during unbalanced operation is proposed. The method includes a negative-sequence-voltagesuppression controller as a secondary controller. The traditional droop control method is used as the primary controller. The negative-sequence component of the inverter's output is extracted and sent to the secondary controller. The secondary controller mitigates the negative-sequence component using a proportional-resonant controller to cancel it. The simulation results for the two-grid-forming inverters with a 25 kVA rating each under unbalanced operation illustrate that the negativesequence component is cancelled out, and the three-phase voltage becomes balanced. The method maintains the stable operation of the inverter-based microgrid during the plug-andplay addition of new inverters.

title: Akura: Dyslexia Aware Sinhala Essay Grading System
authors: Nuwani Fonseka, Tiny Handalage, Perera R.L.S. B, Hasindu Rangika, Kalpani Manatunga, Aparna Jayawardena
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Automated Essay Scoring (AES) improves assessment scalability, but most systems are optimized for rater agreement rather than equitable outcomes for neurodiverse learners. This challenge is more pronounced in low-resource languages such as Sinhala, where orthographic complexity and surface-form variation can lead to unfair penalization of dyslexic writing. This paper presents Akura, a dyslexia-aware Sinhala AES framework that integrates dyslexic writing-pattern classification, context-aware sentence reconstruction, biasaware semantic scoring, and multimodal feedback generation. The framework combines human-in-the-loop reconstruction with XLM-RoBERTa-based rubric-aligned scoring and fairness monitoring using Statistical Parity Difference (SPD) and Disparate Impact Ratio (DIR). By unifying detection, controlled normalization, fairness-aware scoring, and accessible feedback, Akura provides a practical approach for inclusive essay assessment in low-resource educational settings.

title: Development and Deployment of a Secure AI-Enabled Edge Computing Framework for Healthcare Data Analytics
authors: Sri Devi R, Reshma. S. M, Harikaran.V
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Data analytics has emerged as a transformative approach in healthcare by enabling accurate disease prediction, efficient medical image analysis, and secure management of sensitive patient information. The primary aim and objectives of this paper is to develop a comprehensive data analytics framework for healthcare that integrates machine learning techniques, deep learning models, cryptographic algorithms, and edge-based storage and processing to enhance decision-making while ensuring data security. The proposed development is the integration of multiple technologies within a single framework, including machine learning algorithms for accurate disease prediction and analysis of healthcare data. The framework employs a Convolution Neural Network (CNN) for automated medical image classification, which enhances diagnostic support. Furthermore, the system ensures patient data confidentiality and security through the implementation of RSA algorithm for protecting sensitive data and scalable edge-based deployment, which enables faster data processing; reduce latency and efficient real-time healthcare monitoring. For medical image analysis, a Convolution Neural Network (CNN) is implemented to perform image classification, enabling effective extraction of spatial features from healthcare images. The RSA cryptographic algorithm is utilized to encrypt sensitive patient information before storage and transmission. The encrypted data is securely stored and deployed on edge computing platforms located close to data sources, ensuring scalability, accessibility, and protection against unauthorized access. Experimental results demonstrate that the disease prediction module achieves an accuracy of 97%, indicating the effectiveness of the Random Forest model in healthcare analytics. Overall, the proposed system provides a secure, efficient, and intelligent healthcare analytics solution, contributing to improved clinical decision support and patient data protection. Future work to implement in distributed network and publish in IPFS.

title: Context-Aware Machine and Deep Learning Framework for Intrusion Detection and Prevention in Industrial Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: N.V. Chinnasamy, G. Sunitha, Mella Sruthi Priya, Mekala Sumanth, Alvarusetty Vilasa, Geepalam Pavan Kumar
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: A quantitative modeling framework is provided in this paper to study how mechanical deformation and gas adsorption affect electrical properties of a top-gated sensing device. The structure proposed is sensitive and the electrostatic control is maximized as compared to the conventional structures. The bandgap modulation theory models mechanical strain effects and carrier concentration change and surface change density models gas adsorption. An intelligence prediction module is incorporated to aid in accuracy in determining the changes in the parameters of a device when operating in different environmental conditions. It has been shown, based on the results of simulations, that threshold voltage stability, transconductance and sensitivity improve significantly. The model that is proposed has a 96.4 to predict accuracy and a lower parasitic capacitance and a higher stability. Limitations and implementation issues are addressed, and this offers a way to scale to an industrial deployment.

title: WhisperNet: An On-Device TinyML Architecture for Real-Time Anomaly Detection in Industrial Machinery
authors: Shoba G, Ezhilvendan M, Sherine Glory J, Sadhasivam M, Vijayakumar K, G Sekar
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Cloud-dependent anomaly detection in industrial Internet of Things (IoT) introduces prohibitive latency, bandwidth costs, and data privacy risks for real-time predictive maintenance. To overcome this, we present WhisperNet, a novel TinyML architecture for direct deployment on resource-constrained microcontroller units (MCUs). WhisperNet employs a hybrid 1D-CNN and Depthwise Separable Convolution design, enhanced with a temporal attention module to efficiently extract salient features from vibration signals. The model was optimized via post-training integer quantization for ARM Cortex-M cores. Evaluated on the CWRU bearing dataset, WhisperNet achieves 94.2% F1-score accuracy with an inference time of 25 ms, consumes under 50 mW of power, and requires only 250 KB of memory. This demonstrates a practical, cost-effective, and privacy-preserving solution for on-device, real-time anomaly detection in industrial machinery.

title: Sustainable Smart Retail Ecosystem Through IoT and Big Data Integration
authors: P Santhuja, H Azath, R Meenakshi, R Jagadeesh Kannan, Santhosh Priya N, R Srivel
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Smart data analytics and the Internet of Things (IoT) work together in a Sustainable Smart Retail Ecosystem to improve operational efficiency, make better decisions, and reduce environmental impact. IoT technologies such sensors, radio frequency identification (RFID) tags, and smart shelves allow for the gathering of data in real-time across inventory systems, consumer behavior, and energy consumption in contemporary retail settings. Analyzing this constant data flow with Big Data technologies yields significant insights for supply chain optimization, resource management efficiency, and customer experience improvement. Retailers may conduct predictive maintenance, minimize energy usage, and decrease waste by integrating these technologies, which contributes to a sustainable operating framework. Additionally, data-driven marketing, demand forecasting, and dynamic pricing are all made possible by sophisticated analytics, which boost profits and lend a hand to green causes. Retailers can create a more flexible, transparent, and resource-efficient ecosystem by using IoT and big data to link corporate performance with sustainability objectives. To achieve sustainable development and ecological harmony, this study aims to build and execute a smart retail framework that supports environmentally friendly business practices and smart automation.

title: Optimizing Cruise Ship Itineraries: A Sustainable Perspective
authors: Chiara Maragò, Rosita Guido, Francesca Guerriero, Gianpaolo Iazzolino
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: The Triple Bottom Line framework, based on the three fundamental dimensions of sustainable development-society, environment, and econ-omy-has become the key to long-term strategy for global industries focused on the transition to sustainability. In such a context, the cruise industry, whose economic impact is huge and growing rapidly, is no exception. The cruise industry sells itineraries, including destinations and activities previously determined. The main factors that must be taken into consideration when designing an itinerary are: time at destinations, distance between destinations, and traveling speed. Passengers' satisfaction and environmental targets are becoming increasingly urgent objectives to achieve. Based on these requirements, this paper proposes a novel multi-objective model to address the problem of cruise ship itinerary design, including economic, environmental, and social indicators. The model is then applied to a real-world case study to assess how speed and sailing route optimization, as well as variable input parameters and operational decisions, impact costs, environmental pollution, and passenger satisfaction in cruise ship itineraries.

title: A Vision-Based Real-Time Navigation Assistance System for Visually Impaired Individuals Using Deep Learning
authors: Gandla Vaishali, Arshia Tharannum, Nagaram Ramesh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The great problem of visually impaired people is independent and safe navigation. They do not have the visual images that they require to identify the obstacles, objects and know the positions of things. Old-fashioned methods and assistive devices, such as white canes and guide dogs, are useful and do not talk much about the surrounding environment and locations. The development of computer vision and deep learning in the recent past allows us to perceive scenes in real time, and thus, there are new opportunities to make work easier in terms of navigation. Today in this paper we present a real time navigation aid using vision. It takes lightweight deep-learning object detectors to identify the objects around and approximate their distance and the direction toward the observer, using a single camera. It then provides voice instructions which are context sensitive, in a text-to-speech engine. It operates without external communication such as GPS, RFID and beacons. The lightweight detection models we selected ensure that the latency of our models is minimal and the models are able to be executed on regular devices. Indoor and outdoor experimental results indicate that the system is capable of detecting objects reliably, performing real-time data processing, and is robust enough to detect objects when lighting and conditions vary. Comparisons indicate that our strategy is better than traditional mobility helpers and other systems that rely on sensors or infrastructures. It provides superior semantic interpretation, higher flexibility and indeed real-time application. The findings prove that the vision-based navigation system can be used and effective. It has a great chance of enhancing the life of the visually impaired in the areas of safety, mobility and independent living.

title: Infrared Image Recognition of Substation Equipment Based on an Improved YOLOv5 Algorithm
authors: Jianchao Wang, Zezhao Shen, Mingjun Han, Mingming Li
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on AI, Computer Security and Trusted Data
abstract: None

title: Digital Griot: An Emotion-Adaptive Generative AI Framework for Interactive Storytelling
authors: Moumoni Roy, Altaf Sarwar, D Umanandhini
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: Emotion-aware text-to-speech (TTS) systems usually rely on static emotion labels, resulting in abrupt transitions and reduced narrative coherence in long-form storytelling. This paper introduces Digital Griot, an emotion-adaptive framework that models sequential transitions and enables context-aware voice selection. A transformer-based emotion recognition module, integrated with a probabilistic transition system, captures intersegment emotional dependencies, ensuring smooth progression across narrative segments. A dynamic voice selection helps to map transition-aware emotional states to appropriate voice profiles based on narrative context and user preferences. The framework is evaluated using both objective metrics and subjective listening tests with 20 participants. Results show improvements in emotion consistency (0.82), narrative coherence (0.80), and Mean Opinion Score (4.6) compared to baseline systems. An optional accessibility extension supports sign-language-based input for inclusive storytelling. The system is lightweight, modular, and compatible with existing TTS engines, enabling real-time deployment in interactive applications.

title: IoT based Employee Monitoring System
authors: Santosh Chegondi, Doondi Kumar Janapala
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: This article suggests and puts into action an employee monitoring system that uses the Internet of Things (IoT) and includes a Raspberry Pi 4, a USB lens, an EM18 RFID scanner, a display with an LCD, a buzzer, a button to press, or the thingspeak cloud platform. The system uses iris scanning and RFID technology to do dual authentication, which makes attendance and entrance management safe and automatic. The USB camera can identify and confirm the faces of employees, keep track of when they come and go, and figure out how many hours they work. A button shows the current attendance numbers. Also, RFID-based room tracking keeps track of how many employees are in different rooms and shows the relevant information on an LCD panel. The thingspeak cloud gets all the data so it can be displayed and monitored in real time. The suggested method makes it easier to keep an eye on staff and makes the workplace more open.

title: Hybrid CNN–Transformer Framework for Behavior-Based Ransomware Detection Using System Call Traces
authors: Khullar Shikha, Jayaprakash B, Vibhor Harit, Chennaiah Kate, P Sudheer, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: The problem of ransomware has become one of the most dangerous in terms of cybersecurity as it uses system-wide functions to bypass the legacy forms of signature protection. In order to overcome this, the current paper suggests an efficient behavior-based ransomware detection system based on system call trace and a hybrid deep learning system. The presented model combines the use of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to obtain learning of fine-grained local behavioral patterns and a Transformer encoder to learn long-term execution dependencies with the help of self-attention modules. This bifurcated design presents the ability to model localized and global ransomware behaviors in detail in real-time. There are extensive experiments on a publicly available Kaggle ransomware detection dataset with stringent preprocessing and evaluation guidelines. The given method obtains a high detection rate of 98 %, and all three metrics of precision, recall and F1 scores are balanced, which proves the high level of generalization and high resistance to complex types of ransomware. The superiority of the proposed hybrid model is confirmed by comparison with CNN, ResNet, and Transformer baselines. The findings show the usefulness of convolutional and attention-based learning, when mixed to ensure viable and valid detection of ransomware in contemporary computing platforms.

title: A Conceptual Framework for an Intelligent Generative System for Spatial Design Based on Affective Computing and Design Thinking
authors: Zhenchuan Liu, Tingting Zhao
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: Generative AI tools are increasingly being used to produce early spatial layouts; however, most generators optimise only feasibility and functional efficiency, leaving how a space ‘feels’, its affective qualities, to subjective post-hoc interpretation. This paper proposes a conceptual framework for an intelligent generative system that integrates affective computing into spatial layout generation and uses a design-thinking-inspired orchestration to manage iteration, traceability, and revision. The system comprises four modules: a brief interpreter that extracts functional constraints and affective intent; a constraint-aware generator optimising a joint objective over functional and affective losses; a multimodal affect estimator operating on renderings, textual cues, and structured spatial descriptors; and an orchestration policy aligned with the empathise-define-ideate-prototype-test loop that updates targets and tradeoff weights. To validate the practical feasibility of the proposed mathematical formulation, we introduce an original quantitative simulation tracking the optimization of a layout across multiple design-thinking iterations. The simulated results demonstrate how dynamic weight adjustments successfully reduce affective loss while maintaining functional feasibility. This is further grounded by an analysis of recent benchmark literature. Published studies show that AI-generated architectural imagery can evoke distinct emotional responses and that denoising diffusion models improve layout generation accuracy in structured pipelines. This position paper establishes a robust algorithmic foundation and evaluation protocol for future human-centered spatial generation.

title: MDCR-Net: Multi-Scale Dilation Channel Refiner Network for Chinese Cuisine
authors: Zhenghang Liu, Bing Wang, Yuxin Lu, Jialong Li
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Software Engineering and Computer Science (CSECS)
abstract: Automated food detection is crucial for intelligent catering and dietary health monitoring, and Chinese cuisine detection faces unique challenges due to severe ingredient scale diversity. Traditional object detectors with fixed receptive fields struggle to capture multi-scale features of Chinese cuisine, leading to low detection accuracy. To address this problem, this paper adopts YOLO26 as the baseline model and proposes a lightweight Multi-Dimensional Channel Refinement (MDCR) module for multi-scale feature modelling of Chinese cuisine. The MDCR module uses parallel dilated convolutions with optimised rates to extract multi-scale features and introduces channel refinement to avoid feature redundancy. A self-built Chinese cuisine detection dataset with 16 categories is constructed for experimental validation, as existing public food datasets lack detection-level annotations and ignore scale diversity. Extensive ablation experiments and parameter tuning experiments show that MDCR-Net (YOLO26+MDCR) achieves an mAP50 of 97.19% on the self-built dataset, with a 16.4% reduction in error rate compared to the baseline YOLO26. Cross-dataset validation on the Common Objects in Context (COCO) 10-food subset further verifies the generalisation and robustness of MDCR-Net in general food detection. In addition, MDCR-Net maintains high inference speed (102.0 FPS on RTX 3090), which meets the real-time requirements of intelligent catering scenarios. Compared with existing multi-scale methods such as Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) and Inception, MDCR has obvious advantages in lightweight and food detection adaptability, providing a novel and efficient solution for Chinese cuisine detection.

title: Dew Computing-based Healthcare Framework for Latency-Sensitive Medical Applications
authors: Narinder Kaur, Prabh Deep Singh, Kiran Deep Singh
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Trends in Electronics and Informatics (ICOEI)
abstract: Due to the fast development of intelligent healthcare systems and Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) that allowed constant monitoring of patients and real-time medical decision-making. Although these developments are of great advantage, they also pose a significant problem in terms of latency, connectivity reliability and responding to events at the right time, particularly in emergency medical situations, in which a single delay can impact the lives of the patients. The conventional cloud-based systems have in most cases high delays in communication and thus cannot be effectively used in time sensitive medical applications like arrhythmia detection, fall detection and critical condition alarms. In order to overcome these shortcomings, this research paper offers a dew computing-based health architecture, which makes real time data processing at medical equipment, eliminating reliance on remote cloud resources. The proposed Dew-Aware Healthcare Processing Algorithm (DHPA) suggests intelligent prioritization of medical tasks with respect to urgency and assigns it on-demand to the relevant computing layers to process efficiently. Comprehensive simulation-based analyses of the system with different configurations reveal that the proposed structure can reduce end-to-end latency by up to 73% and emergency response time by 68 percent in contrast to the available traditional cloud-based solutions. Also, the frame saves about 41 percent of energy. The improvements help in the acceleration of clinical interventions and improve patient safety, especially in critical care settings.

title: A Microbial Fuel Cell Biosensor With Deep Learning-Enabled High Selectivity
authors: Yining Wang, Fengxiang Tang, Boya Liu, Hao Ren
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Real-time water pollution monitoring is essential amid global water quality concerns. This work presents a bioinspired wireless microbial fuel cell biosensor that overcomes selectivity challenges utilizing an AI algorithm. A GRU-based deep learning model is introduced and achieves a high accuracy of 98.9% in classifying <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C u}^{\mathbf{2 +}}, \mathbf{P b}^{\mathbf{2 +}}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C r}^{\mathbf{6 +}}$</tex> by analyzing output current. Moreover, the MFC biosensor responds in mere 48 seconds, which is 2.5 times faster than the previous record, and provides real-time, high linearity sensing with wireless capability for heavy metal ion monitoring in water.

title: Enhancing Brinjal Leaf Disease Detection: A Comparative Analysis of Convolutional Neural Network Architectures
authors: Amit Kumar Mishra, Janmenjoy Nayak, Manohar Mishra
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Trends in Advancements and Applications of Computational Intelligence Techniques (ETAACT)
abstract: In India, agriculture is essential to providing food for a significant portion of the population. Phytopathology are a common challenge in agriculture, where computational vision plays a crucial role. Computer vision uses deep learning, such as convolutional neural networks (CNN), to identify diseases in plants. The CNN model has shown good results in this regard, particularly in enhancing the accuracy of disease detection by utilising labelled data for training. The primary objective of this research is to propose a CNN model for the detection of leaf diseases. On completion of the experiment, the proposed CNN achieve accuracy 98.46% with a loss of 0.0317. Some of the other CNN variants are also tested on the same dataset and focused on being less helpful for analysing the performance of various CNN-based models in the leaf disease problem.

title: Web-Based Comparative Analysis Framework for Metaheuristic Optimization Algorithms: A Unified Educational Approach
authors: Selin Eryaşar, Ali Durmuş, Rifat Kurban
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of metaheuristic optimization algorithms presents a steep learning curve for researchers, often aggravated by the black box nature of algorithmic execution. Existing visualization tools partially address this issue but are frequently limited by desktop-based dependencies or lack realtime comparative capabilities. To bridge this gap, this study introduces a comprehensive, web-based educational framework designed for the real-time visualization and comparative analysis of Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Artificial Bee Colony (ABC), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO). Built on a modular React-FastAPI architecture, the platform enables users to observe stochastic search dynamics across both continuous benchmark functions (Sphere, Rastrigin) and discrete real-world engineering problems (Traveling Salesman Problem and Knapsack Problem). Experimental validation, conducted over 20 independent runs, demonstrates the problem-dependent efficacy of these algorithms; quantitative results reveal that GWO achieves superior stability and convergence rates in both unimodal and multimodal environments, while PSO outperforms in combinatorial routing. The platform is intended as an educational testbed that makes stochastic search trajectories more interpretable and supports reproducible baseline comparisons. Future work will include user studies to quantify learning gains and broader scalability tests with additional algorithms and problem instances.

title: Feynman Explanation Prompting: A Pedagogically Grounded Framework for Large Language Models
authors: Fatih Bildirici, Murat Karakus, Rukiye Savran Kiziltepe
publish: 2026 8th International Congress on Human-Computer Interaction, Optimization and Robotic Applications (ICHORA)
abstract: This paper proposes a prompting framework inspired by the Feynman Method to improve the explanatory and pedagogical behavior of large language models. All experiments were conducted using the GPT-5 model under controlled inference settings. Drawing on the principle that effective understanding is reflected in the ability to explain concepts clearly, the framework formulates prompting as an iterative process of explanation, self-assessment, and revision. It formalizes this process as a five-step Feynman Explanation Prompting (FEYP) template comprising explanation, gap identification, refinement, simplification, and re-teaching. Empirical evaluations across concept explanation, code interpretation, and educational dialogue generation tasks show that the proposed approach produces explanations that are clearer, more coherent, and more pedagogically effective than those generated by standard prompting strategies. In addition, the framework supports self-evaluation by enabling models to identify limitations in their own explanations and revise them accordingly. Overall, the proposed method shifts the role of language models from systems that primarily generate answers toward systems that can produce structured explanations and reflect on their own outputs, contributing to more interpretable and pedagogically aligned language model behavior.

title: Transforming Power Infrastructure Monitoring with Medical Imaging-Inspired AI
authors: Gurnoor Singh Dang, Majid Rodgar, Michael Snyder
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Communication and Signal Processing (ICCSP)
abstract: We propose an innovative adaptation of AI-based medical imaging methods for fault detection in power systems. Our framework, FusionScan AI, repurposes an enhancement pipeline originally built to improve Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) scans, applying it instead to the visual inspection of power infrastructure, including transformers, switchgear, and distribution assets. The approach employs three main phases: deep learning based denoising, adaptive contrast optimization, and edge refinement through convolutional filtering. Together, these operations significantly improve the visibility of thermal and optical sensor data that are often degraded by noise or poor contrast. When tested on infrared thermographic images of high voltage equipment and substations, the system revealed hidden indicators of faults such as insulation breakdown, overheating at joints, and structural irregularities. Drawing inspiration from medical imaging, where similar methods have successfully exposed tumors and microlesions, our study shows that these techniques can be effectively transferred to power system diagnostics. The results highlight the promise of medical imaging-inspired AI pipelines for monitoring distributed energy networks, supporting predictive maintenance, enhancing safety, and improving efficiency in the operation of future smart grids.

title: Multi-Modal Coordinated Sensing for Spatially-Aware Marine Monitoring
authors: Endrowednes Kuantama, Alice James, Avishkar Seth, Ningning Hou, Melanie Bishop
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: Accurate location monitoring of marine life is important for sustainable aquaculture, fisheries, and ecosystem management. Current aerial and underwater drones face limits in accuracy and scale. Aerial drones struggle with depth and glare; underwater drones have a short range and a narrow field of view. Multi-drone systems scale, but coordination over water is hard because GPS is unreliable and noise is high. This paper presents a Coordinated Multi Drone (CMD) architecture that integrates an aerial drone and a surface drone for multimodal sensing. The aerial drone detects surface targets, and the surface drone, with an underwater camera, performs near-field classification. A kernel-based fusion algorithm merges observations in a shared Star Marker Positioning (SMP) frame for precise localization, achieving 92 % spatial alignment. Seaweed is the test case, but the approach generalizes to other marine biomass. Field trials achieved 95% within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 m}$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 2 - 0. 1 2 m}$</tex> localization error and a 25 % improvement in underwater detection. CMD provides a sensing framework for spatially aware marine monitoring using coordinated, multimodal platforms.

title: Power Optimization for Sustainable Smart Speakers: Echo Pop Case Study
authors: Riccha Tripati, Dibyendu Nandy, Marco Pulido, Joey Escobar, Jeongwoo Han
publish: 2026 IEEE Conference on Technologies for Sustainability (SusTech)
abstract: In this paper, we present a comprehensive systemlevel approach to advancing device sustainability through power optimization for smart home devices, with a detailed case study of Amazon's Echo Pop. Through Lifecycle Assessment (LCA), we identified that Echo Pop generates an estimated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 2 ~ k g ~ C O 2 e}$</tex> over its product lifetime, with 24 kg CO2e (57 %) attributed to use-phase emissions, highlighting the critical importance of idle power optimization for decarbonization efforts. We implemented a novel system architecture for energy efficiency that leverages CPU Suspend-to-RAM states and Wi-Fi power save modes. We minimize energy consumption during device inactivity and via coordinating various system services to maintain seamless user experience. The system intelligently transitions between power states using a low power Digital Signal Processing (DSP) core for monitoring ambient audio, while duty-cycling background connectivity tasks. We validated the effectiveness of our multi-domain power management approach across SoC, Wi-Fi, and system-level components, through comprehensive power rail analysis using high-resolution measurement methodologies in a lab setting. Results demonstrate substantial power reduction achievements, with Echo Pop achieving approximately 1.1 W standby power consumption-about 30 % improved over previous generation devices. Ground truth validation through in-field telemetry data confirms our lab projection models, with a daily average energy consumption of 28.21 Wh closely matching the in-field empirical measurements of 28.07 Wh. This work establishes a validated framework for device design that balances environmental impact reduction with maintained functionality and user experience.

title: NO-IGS: Nonlinear Oversampling Based on Informed Granular-ball Space for Imbalanced Regression_supp1-3698843.pdf
authors: Xiaofeng Liao
publish: N/A
abstract: In this paper, we consider the problem of imbalanced regression. This problem is prevalent in real-world regression datasets. Resampling is a critical technique utilized in imbalanced regression, which simply and directly manipulates the quantity of skewed samples to rebalance the data distribution. However, the relevant academic research mainly focused on classification, with little discussion on imbalanced regression. Therefore, an adaptive Nonlinear Oversampling within Informed Granular-ball Space (NO-IGS) is proposed for imbalanced regression as a novel direction. First, NO-IGS proposes a nonconvex optimization model to fit the distribution of the dataset and adaptively split the granular-ball space. Then, NO-IGS proposes another non-convex optimization model to select an appropriate granular-ball space for oversampling. The existence of optimal solutions for both optimization models is proven, and solution algorithms are provided. Finally, based on anisotropic multivariate Gaussian distributions, new samples conforming to the original distribution of the dataset are synthesized in the granular-ball space. Numerical experiments based on dozens of datasets and comparison algorithms demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of NO-IGS.

title: A Bitmap-Guided Collision Tree Anti-collision Protocol for Large-Scale RFID Tags Identification_supp2-3696454.pdf
authors: Xiaolin Jia
publish: N/A
abstract: During the collision resolution phase, many deterministic RFID anti-collision protocols require tags to repeatedly transmit identical ID segments across multiple query cycles. This redundant transmission leads to high communication overhead, increasing identification latency and energy consumption, thereby constraining system performance and scalability. This paper proposes the Bitmap-Guided Collision Tree (BGCT) protocol to address this limitation. BGCT reduces tag transmissions through a progressive information acquisition mechanism, and enables multi-way collision resolution by integrating vector-based grouping with a bitmap. Theoretical analysis and simulation results demonstrate BGCT’s performance advantages. Specifically, for identifying 10000 tags(ID=96 bits), BGCT reduces the total identification time by 27.8% compared to the best competitor in this scenario, EMDT. For 256-bit IDs, assuming uniform or pseudo-random ID distributions, the progressive mechanism mitigates the dependency of communication cost on ID length, maintaining a low expected overhead in contrast to the linear growth observed in other protocols. Furthermore, under non-uniform ID distributions, BGCT demonstrates a collision resolution efficiency 152.9% higher than that of the competing protocols.

title: Dynamic Neural Architecture Search for Micro-Expression Recognition_supp1-3697628.pdf
authors: Monu Verma
publish: N/A
abstract: Micro-expressions (MEs) are transient in nature and reveal enough visual cues to recognize genuine emotions. Neural Architecture Search (NAS) has recently gained significant attention in the field of micro expression recognition (MER). However, existing NAS approaches in MER rely on general-purpose strategies, often reducing network depth to compensate for limited training data. This restriction can limit the discovery of optimal architectures, especially since dataset sizes in MER vary significantly. Moreover, focusing solely on operation selection and connectivity is inadequate, flexibility in depth and structure is essential to adapt to different data characteristics. While shallow networks are often considered suitable for small datasets, this assumption falls short for MER. Despite the scarcity of training samples, MEs are inherently complex, fleeting, and nuanced, requiring models with high representational power for accurate recognition. To address this trade-off, we propose a novel Dynamic Neural Architecture Search framework for MER (DNAS-MER), designed to adaptively balance model depth and architectural design based on the data. The proposed DNAS hierarchically optimizes the network depth, resolution paths, and cell-level operations through a scalable network search space and a multi-scale context-aware (MSCA) cell search space. A novel depth-aware loss function enables the model to automatically adapt depth based on data needs, while MSCA cell search, embedded with adaptive feature fusion operations, enhances ME-specific feature representation to better capture the complexity of MEs. We evaluate DNAS-MER on a composite dataset MEGC2019, CASME-II, and SMIC. Experimental results demonstrate that DNAS-MER consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods for both video and image-based MER tasks.

title: A Unified Distributed Algorithm for Hybrid Near-Far Field Activity Detection in Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Jingreng Lei, Yang Li, Ziyue Wang, Qingfeng Lin, Ya-Feng Liu, Yik-Chung Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: A great amount of endeavor has recently been devoted to activity detection for massive machine-type communications in cell-free multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems. However, as the number of antennas at the access points (APs) increases, the Rayleigh distance that separates the near-field and far-field regions also expands, rendering the conventional assumption of sole far-field propagation impractical. To address this challenge, this paper establishes a covariance-based formulation that can effectively capture the statistical property of hybrid near-far field channels. Based on this formulation, we theoretically reveal that increasing the proportion of near-field channels enhances the detection performance. Furthermore, we propose a distributed algorithm, where each AP performs local activity detection and only exchanges the detection results to the central processing unit, thus significantly reduces the computational complexity and the communication overhead. Not only with convergence guarantee, the proposed algorithm is unified in the sense that it can handle single-cell or cell-free systems with either near-field or far-field devices as special cases. Simulation results validate the theoretical analyses and demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed approach compared with existing methods.

title: ND-Net: Neighborhood Description Network for Large-scale Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation
authors: Weitong Zhou, Shuhao Zhang, Chaoguang Men, Peng Liu, Hong Xie, Jingyi Liu, Meng Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate large-scale point cloud semantic segmentation (PCSS) in urban scenes remains challenging primarily due to the ambiguous boundaries between urban objects, uneven point density distribution, and similar height features of some categories. To improve the large-scale PCSS performance in urban scenes, this study proposed ND-Net (Neighborhood Description Network), a lightweight encoder decoder architecture network that enhances neighborhood description through three key innovative designs. To alleviate the limitations of k-nearest neighbors (KNN) neighborhood query in regions with uneven point density, the adaptive neighbor feature aggregation (ANFA) module integrates feature scaling residual (FSR) unit was proposed, which assigns adaptive weights to neighboring points based on relative elevation information. To further enrich point cloud feature representation and assist in improving category discriminability, this paper proposed the two-stage feature enhancement (TFE) module, which fuses local context information extracted from the explicit 3D space with global bilinear responses derived from the implicit feature space. Additionally, the boundary consistency (BC) loss function is proposed to constrain the label inconsistency between centroids and their neighboring points. The proposed ND-Net was evaluated on three large-scale urban scene PCSS benchmarks, experimental results show that ND-Net outperforms or matches state-of-the-art methods. The results of ablation studies and efficiency analysis further demonstrated that the effectiveness of the three key innovative designs for improving the performance of large-scale PCSS in urban scenes.

title: PriMa$\chi$: Novel Genetic Reinforcement Learning Model for Improving Privacy Preservation in Blockchain
authors: Sheema Madhusudhanan, Arun Cyril Jose
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: As privacy concerns intensify in data-driven systems, this article presents PriMa $\chi $ , a hybrid framework that combines a genetic algorithm (GA) and reinforcement learning (RL) to optimize the privacy–utility tradeoff in differential privacy (DP) through explicit adaptive privacy–utility control. PriMa $\chi $ adaptively selects perturbation configurations to minimize the privacy budget $(\varepsilon)$ while preserving data utility $({\mathcal {U}})$ . To support verifiable privacy-preserving analytics in decentralized environments, we further integrate PriMa $\chi $ with a privacy-aware smart-contract framework that enables on-chain DP enforcement and zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) verification. The framework supports structured, transactional, and spatiotemporal workloads, including decentralized finance, electronic health records, census analytics, and location services. An interleaved Petri net model is used to formally verify privacy-aware state transitions in the smart-contract workflow. Experimental results show that PriMa $\chi $ achieves utility of at least 80% under dataset-dependent privacy budgets in the range $0.003 \leq \varepsilon~\leq\unicode{0x0142}.43$ , while also effectively mitigating model-extraction, membership-inference, and privacy-budget-exhaustion attacks. These results demonstrate that PriMa $\chi $ provides adaptive, auditable, and practically deployable privacy protection for decentralized analytics.
